fremdsprachige_2_1_38.original_entry:
CAMOENS
Die Lusiade des ... Aus dem Portugiesischen in Deutsche Ottavereime übersetzt. Mit einem Bildnis des Dichters. Wien Pichler 1816. XXVIII, 299 S. Magazin der ausländischen classischen Literatur zwölfter Band. Unbeschnittene OBrosch. Gut erhalten
fremdsprachige_2_1_38.verification_notes: missing_person: The title 'Die Lusiade des ...' contains an ellipsis where the translator's name would normally appear, suggesting the translator's name is missing from the entry. api_concerned: The author is listed as 'CAMOENS', the Portuguese poet Luís de Camões (c. 1524–1580), author of 'Os Lusíadas'. The translator is not named in the entry despite the ellipsis in the title suggesting a name should follow 'des'. The 1816 Pichler edition of a German verse translation of Os Lusíadas may be identifiable, but the translator cannot be confirmed from this entry alone.

fremdsprachige_18_1_38.original_entry:
JOYCE, James
Finnegans Wehg. Kainnäh ÜbelSätzZung des Wehrkeß fun Schämes Scheuß von Dieter H. Stündel. Verlag Jürgen Häusser 1.Auflage 1993. Links englischer Originaltext mit Anmerkungen, rechts Übersetzung. Nur rechte Seite nummeriert. 628 S. gr.-2°. OLn. mit bedruckter Folie. Verlagsfrisch.
fremdsprachige_18_1_38.verification_notes: The subtitle 'Kainnäh ÜbelSätzZung des Wehrkeß fun Schämes Scheuß' is an intentional phonetic/parodic German rendering of 'Cannot Oversetting of the Work of James Joyce' — this is a known feature of Stündel's translation of Finnegans Wake, which itself uses multilingual wordplay. The title 'Finnegans Wehg' is the correct title of Stündel's German translation. No place of publication is stated in the entry. The page count of 628 refers only to the numbered (right-hand) pages; the bilingual layout means the actual physical extent is larger. No corrections applied as all unusual features are intentional.

fremdsprachige_22_1_38.original_entry:
MIRZA SCHAFFY
Die Lieder des Mirza Schaffy mit einem Prolog von Friedrich Bodenstedt. 241.-250 Tausend Berlin Becker´s Verlag 1902. XXIV, 199 S. Jede Seite mit üppigem Buchschmuck OLdr.m.Lesebändchen, Goldschnitt und Gold-aufdruck. Berieben.
fremdsprachige_22_1_38.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: 'Gold-aufdruck' (hyphenated due to line break) normalized to 'Goldaufdruck'. api_concerned: 'Mirza Schaffy' (Mirza Shafi Vazeh, 1794–1852) was an Azerbaijani poet; the 'Lieder des Mirza Schaffy' were largely composed or freely adapted by Friedrich Bodenstedt himself, who presented them as translations. The authorship attribution is therefore historically contested — Bodenstedt is often considered the true author. The entry lists Mirza Schaffy as author and Bodenstedt as contributor (Prolog), which follows the traditional title-page attribution but may be misleading. Flagged for human review.

fremdsprachige_23_1_38.original_entry:
OSSIAN
Neuaufgefundene Gedichte Ossians. A. d. Englischen. Mit erläuternden Anmerkungen, und einer Abhandlung über die Werke dieses celtischen Barden. Frankfurt und Leipzig, 1792. (12) 255 S. (A.d.Engl. mit Anmerkungen v. Christoph Heinrich Pfaff). OLn. Sehr gut erhalten.
fremdsprachige_23_1_38.verification_notes: OSSIAN is a legendary/pseudonymous figure — the Ossian poems were largely composed by James Macpherson in the 18th century and presented as translations from ancient Gaelic. The entry lists Christoph Heinrich Pfaff as translator/annotator, but the 'original language' is stated as English (Macpherson's versions), not Gaelic. The authorship attribution to 'OSSIAN' follows the cataloging convention of the time. No publisher is stated in the entry. Human verification recommended regarding the authorship and the missing publisher field.

fremdsprachige_926_38_38.verification_notes: The entry covers Bands 1–4 of a 5-volume set; Band 5 is not present in the collection. The publication year range is given as 1803–1815, suggesting volumes were published across multiple years; only the start year (1803) is used as publication_year. The title 'Siecles de Louis XIV, et de Louis XV' likely refers to Voltaire's 'Le Siècle de Louis XIV' (and its continuation covering Louis XV); the plural 'Siecles' and the combined title may reflect this specific stereotype edition's titling — flagged for verification. The work is in French and does not appear to be a translation, so is_translation is set to false.

griechenland_105_5_10.verification_notes: The author is listed as 'RAMLER, C.W.' — Karl Wilhelm Ramler (1725–1798) was a German poet who did write a 'Kurzgefasste Mythologie', but he died in 1798, well before this 1840 Wien/Sammer edition. The work may have been published posthumously or the initials may refer to a different person. The attribution appears plausible but the long posthumous gap warrants verification. Additionally, the entry states '16 Kupfern' in the edition description but lists '1 Titelkupfer + 15 ganzseitige Kupfer' in the physical description, which is consistent (1+15=16).

griechenland_116_5_10.original_entry:
SCHWENCK, Konrad
Mythologie der Griechen, Römer, Ägypter, Semiten, Perser, Germanen und Slavon. In Sieben Bänden und einem General-Register. Frankfurt/Main Sauerländer.
Band. Die Mythologie der Griechen. Zweite Ausgabe. Mit zwölf lithographierten Tafeln.1855. VIII, 614 SS.
Band. Die Mythologie der Römer für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1845. 495 S.
Band. Die Mythologie der Ägypter für die Gebildeten und die studierende Jugend. Mit 13 lithographierten Tafeln. 1846. 300 S.
Band. Die Mythologie der Semiten ür Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1849. 364 S.
Band. Die Mythologie der Perser für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1850. 403 S.
Band. Die Mythologie der Germanen. Zweite Ausgabe. 1855. 367 S.
Band. Die Mythologie der Slawen für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1853. 482 S. Anschließend: Generalregister. 1855. 98 S.
Vollständige Ausgabe in 7 Bänden und Register, gebunden in 4 Halbleinenbänden der Zeit. Die Einbände unansehnlich, Inhalt jedoch sehr interessant.
griechenland_116_5_10.verification_notes: 1) Volume numbers are not explicitly stated in the entry — each volume is introduced only as 'Band.' without a number. Numbers 1–7 have been assigned in the order they appear in the entry, which matches the logical sequence (Griechen, Römer, Ägypter, Semiten, Perser, Germanen, Slawen). The Generalregister is appended after Band 7 and not counted as a separate volume. 2) Band 4 contains a clear typo: 'ür Gebildete' should read 'für Gebildete' — corrected_by_api set true for this correction. 3) Bands 1 and 6 are described as 'Zweite Ausgabe', while the other bands appear to be first editions — multiple_editions flag set accordingly. 4) The series title in the entry uses 'Slavon' (likely a truncation or typo for 'Slaven'/'Slawen'); the individual volume title uses 'Slawen' — no correction applied to the main title as it may reflect the original title page wording. 5) No overall publication year is given; individual volume years range from 1845 to 1855.

griechenland_57_3_10.verification_notes: Publication year 1982 is likely an error. Gregorovius's 'Athenais' was first published in 1882 by Brockhaus, Leipzig. The year 1982 is almost certainly a typo for 1882 (corrected_by_api set true for this correction). However, since the entry states '2. Auflage', it is worth noting that the known editions of this work appeared in the 19th century; a 1982 Brockhaus reprint is not confirmed by world knowledge, so api_concerned is also set true to flag for human verification.

griechenland_58_3_10.original_entry:
GRIECHISCHE UND RÖMISCHE MYTHOLOGIE (DEMOUSTIERE)
In Briefen an Emilie. Frei nach dem Französischen der Herrn de Moustier und Tressan bearbeitet.
1. Theil fehlt
2. Theil 171 S. Mit 1 Titelvignette und 2 Lithographien
3. Theil VI, 121 S. Mit 1 Titelvignette und 1 Lithographie
4. Theil VI, 158 S. Mit 1 Titelvignette und 2 Lithographien
5. Theil VI, 170 S. Mit 1 Titelvignette und 2 Lithographien
6. Theil V, 156 S. und 23 S. Anhang. Mit 1 Titelvignette und 2 Lithographien
Wien und Prag Franz Haas 1807. OPbde. mit Rückenschild. Gut erhaltene Bände.
griechenland_58_3_10.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry states the work was freely adapted ('frei nach dem Französischen') from 'Herrn de Moustier und Tressan', but no name of the German adapter/translator is given. The author listed in the heading is 'DEMOUSTIERE', which corresponds to Charles-Albert Demoustier (1760–1801), author of 'Lettres à Émilie sur la mythologie'. The co-author 'Tressan' likely refers to Louis-Élisabeth de la Vergne, comte de Tressan. api_concerned: The entry parenthetically attributes the work to 'DEMOUSTIERE' but the subtitle also names 'Tressan' as a co-source; it is unclear whether the German adaptation is a combined work or primarily Demoustier's. The name of the German adapter who 'bearbeitet' the text is absent. Volume 1 is noted as missing from the collection.

arianischen Wandalen und Ostgoten unter Justinian I. (527-565). 1988. 677 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch
Band 3: Die Alte Kirche. Fälschung, Verdammung, Vernichtung. 1990. 713 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch
Band 4: Frühmittelalter. Von König Chlodwig I. (um 500) bis zum Tode Karls 'des Großen' (814). 1994. 616 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch
Band 5: 9. und 10. Jahrhundert. Von Ludwig dem Frommen (814) bis zum Tode Ottos III. (1002). 1997. XXXXIX, 672 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch 2 Ex.
Band 6: 11. und 12. Jahrhundert. Von Kaiser Heinrich II, dem Heiligen (1002) bis zum Ende des Dritten Kreuzzugs (1192).1999. 653 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch.
Band 7: 13. und 14. Jahrhundert. Von Kaiser Heinrich VI.(1190) zu Kaiser Ludwig IV. dem Bayern (1347). 2002. 574 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch
Band 8: Das 15. und 16. Jahrhundert. Vom Exil der Päpste in Avignon bis zum Augsburger Religionsfrieden. 2004. 528 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Erstausgabe Verlagsfrisch
Band 9: Mitte des 16. bis Anfang des 18. Jahrhunderts. 2008. 454 S. OPbd.m.OU. EA
Band 10: 18. Jahrhundert und Ausblick auf die Folgezeit. Könige von Gottes Gnaden und Niedergang des Papsttums 2013. 319 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen Erstausgabe. Verlagsfrisch Zusammen
christentum_24_1_6.verification_notes: Band 5 has the unusual pagination notation 'XXXXIX, 672 S.' — 'XXXXIX' is likely a Roman numeral for 39 (XXXIX), possibly a typo for 'XXXIX'. The page count 672 is used as the main pages value; the preliminary pages notation is preserved in the notes. Band 9 uses OPbd.m.OU (Originalpappband mit Originalumschlag) while all other volumes use OLn.m.OU (Originalleinen mit Originalumschlag) — this discrepancy is noted but not corrected. The entry ends with 'Zusammen', indicating the volumes are catalogued together as a set. Band 5 has 2 copies noted. The top-level copies field is left null as the '2 Ex.' applies only to Band 5.

Das Wort. Kommentar zum Prolog des Johannes-Evangeliums. Übersetzt von Josef Pieper. Lateinisch-Deutsch. Einführende Schriften Band 1. Hrsg. v. Hanns-Gregor Nissing und Berthold Wald. LXXIII, 165 S.

München Pneuma Verlag 2017. OBrosch. Pro Band
christentum_68_3_6.verification_notes: The entry covers two volumes (Band 1 and Band 3) of the series 'Einführende Schriften', implying at least a Band 2 exists that is not described here. The note 'Pro Band' suggests the entry describes individual volumes sold/catalogued separately, but both are listed together. The translator field at the top level has been set to Josef Pieper as the primary translator, but Band 3 also lists Heinrich Raskop and Hans Schulte as co-translators — these additional translators could not be represented in the single translator field and are noted in the volume notes instead. The editors (Nissing and Wald) are explicitly named only for Band 1; it is unclear whether they also edited Band 3. Pages are given as Roman + Arabic numerals (LXXIII, 165 and LXXX, 128) and cannot be reduced to a single integer, so they are recorded in volume notes only.

christentum_129_6_6.original_entry:
THALHOFER, Valentin
Bibliothek der Kirchenväter. Kempten Kösel
Ausgewählte Schriften des Hl. Basilius des Großen. Zweiter Band. 1877. 366 S.
Johannes von Damaskus Darlegung des Orthodoxen Glaubens. 1880. 316 S.
Origenes Ausgewählte Schriften. Erster Band 1874. 339 S.
OLn. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken. Kleinformat.
christentum_129_6_6.verification_notes: The 'Bibliothek der Kirchenväter' is a large multi-volume series published by Kösel in Kempten with many editors and translators; Thalhofer, Valentin was one of the editors/contributors but the series had multiple editors. The entry lists three volumes from different years (1874, 1877, 1880) with different authors/subjects (Basilius, Johannes von Damaskus, Origenes), each being part of the broader series. Volume numbers within the overall series are not given for all volumes (only 'Zweiter Band' for Basilius and 'Erster Band' for Origenes are noted, but these refer to sub-series within the Kirchenväter collection, not overall series numbers). The structure is ambiguous — it is unclear whether these are consecutive volumes owned or scattered volumes from the series. No overall publication year can be assigned. api_concerned: Thalhofer's exact editorial role in this series should be verified.

D. i. Johannes Scheffler: Cherubinischer Wandersmann oder Geistreiche Sinn- und Schlussreime. Herausgegeben von Louise Gnädiger nach dem Text von Glatz 1675. mit 56 Illustrationen. Vollständige Ausgabe. Zürich Manesse 1986. 526 S. OLn.m.OU und Lesebändchen. Sehr gut erhalten.

christentum_78_4_6.original_entry:

christentum_78_4_6.verification_notes: The two volumes are bound together ('Zwei in einem Band'). The pagination 'VIII, 206, 3Bll, 263, 463 SS.' is ambiguous: it is unclear how to distribute the page counts between the two works. The 463 SS. likely belongs to volume 2 (Heilige Seelenlust), but the preceding figures (VIII, 206, 3Bll, 263) may all belong to volume 1 or be distributed differently. Volume page counts cannot be cleanly separated. Additionally, 'Einband Hugo Steiner-Prag' refers to the cover designer, noted under condition.

Carl Friedrich Bardt, Leipzig 1810, X + 307 pp., with title copperplate and vignette; (2) Aulus Persius Flaccus, Satyren, introduced and annotated by Georg Gustav Fülleborn (of Breslau), Wien und Prag bey Franz Haas 1803, with title copperplate and vignette. The main record has been assigned to the Juvenal work as first described. The Persius volume is noted in condition. No publisher is given for the Juvenal volume. The format 'OHLdr.' antike_149_6_9.original_entry:
XENOPHONS
Sämtliche Schriften. Aus dem Griechischen neu übersetzt von August Christian Borhek.
Erster Theil, welcher die Kyropädie mit den vornehmsten Abhandlungen über selbige enthält. Dazu Anhang ,welcher einige der besten Erläuterungsschriften der Kyropädie enthält. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette. XVI, 535 S.
Zweyter Theil, welcher den Feldzug des Prinzen Kyros, mit den besten Erläuterungsschriften desselben enthält. Dazu Thomas Hutchinsons Abhandlung , vom Verfasser des Feldzugs des Prinzen Kyros, und vom Zuge der Griechen. Im Auszuge. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette VI, 413 S.
Dritter Theil, welcher die griechische Geschichte enthält und einer Widmung des Übersetzers Borheck von 1789. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette (XVIII), 509 S
Vierter Theil. Erster Anhang zur griechischen Geschichte Xenophons, oder Einleitung in dieselbe. Dazu Xenophons Lobschrift auf den König Agesilaus, übersetzt von Konrad Borheck. Weiters Xenophons Staatsverfassung der Lakedämonier. Übersestzt von Konrad Borheck. Weiters Xenophon über die Einkünfte, Xenophons Hiero und Xenophons Briefe übersetzt von Konrad Borheck. Mit einem Titelkupfer und Vignette. 464 S.
Wien und Prag bey Franz Haas 1801. Original grüne Pappbände mit Rückentitel, stark beansprucht. Buchblock, Text und Kupfer tadellos erhalten. Der fünfte Band fehlt. Seltenes Werk. Band 1 – 4 von 5
antike_149_6_9.verification_notes: 1) CORRECTED: The entry heading reads 'XENOPHONS' (genitive form); normalized to 'XENOPHON' as the author name. 2) CORRECTED: The translator's name is spelled inconsistently throughout the entry as both 'Borhek' (in the main title line) and 'Borheck' (in volumes 3 and 4). The form 'Borheck' appears more frequently and is used for the named co-translator Konrad Borheck; however, the correct historical spelling of the main translator is 'Borheck' — corrected_by_api set true for this normalization, though api_concerned is also set because both spellings appear in the original and the correct form cannot be verified with certainty. 3) PROBLEMATIC MULTI-VOLUME: The entry explicitly states 'Band 1–4 von 5' and notes 'Der fünfte Band fehlt.' Volume 5 is absent and its content, page count, and title are unknown; it has been omitted from the volumes array. 4) API_CONCERNED: Konrad Borheck is listed as a separate translator for portions of volume 4 (Lobschrift auf Agesilaus, Staatsverfassung der Lakedämonier, Hiero, Briefe, Einkünfte), distinct from the main translator August Christian Borhek/Borheck. The relationship between the two translators (possibly father and son or relatives) cannot be confirmed without further research. 5) TYPO NOTED: 'Übersestzt' in the original entry is an obvious typo for 'Übersetzt'; not corrected in the original_entry field but noted here.

antike_108_5_9.verification_notes: The editor is listed as 'Franz Dirlmeyer' in the entry, but the known editor of the Tusculum Bücherei Phaidon edition published by Heimeran in 1959 is Ludwig Georgii or, more likely, Wilhelm Szabo — however, 'Franz Dirlmeyer' is a plausible name variant of 'Franz Dirlmeier', the well-known classical philologist who edited several Tusculum volumes. Corrected display_name left as written ('Franz Dirlmeyer') since the correction to 'Dirlmeier' is highly probable but not certain; corrected_by_api set to true to flag this discrepancy for review. Actual correction: 'Dirlmeyer' is almost certainly a misspelling of 'Dirlmeier' (Franz Dirlmeier, 1904–1977, classical philologist). Applying correction in verification_notes only; display_name left as-is pending human confirmation.

antike_117_5_9.original_entry:
POMPONII MELAE
Situ orbis libri III. ad optimas editiones collati cum indice locupletissimo.
Des Pomponius Mela drey Bücher von der Lage der Welt. Viennae Antonii Pichler 1807. 390 S. OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken. Kaum benutzter Bibliotheksband aus Kremsier. Selten! Sehr gut erhalten. In dieser Ausgabe nur in Reprints.
antike_117_5_9.verification_notes: The entry uses the genitive Latin form 'POMPONII MELAE' as the author heading, which is the standard RAK cataloging form for classical Latin authors. The actual author's name is Pomponius Mela, a 1st-century Roman geographer. The format 'OHLdr.' (Original-Halbleder) is not a standard abbreviation in the provided format table; it has been expanded as 'Original-Halbleder' based on general bibliographic convention. The entry notes this edition is rare and only available in reprints, which is consistent with an 1807 Viennae imprint of a classical text.

antike_118_5_9.original_entry:

Der Gotenkrieg. Der Vandalenkrieg nebst Auszügen aus Agathias, sowie Fragmente des Anonymus Valesianus und des Johannes von Antiochia. Nach der Übersetzung von David Costa. Essen Phaidon o. J. 375 S. OLn.m.OU.

antike_119_5_9.original_entry:
PROPERZ UND TIBULL
Liebeselegien. Lateinisch und Deutsch. Neu herausgegeben und übersetzt von Georg Luck. Zürich Artemis 1964. XXI, 503 S. OLn.m.OU

antike_120_5_9.original_entry:
RIBBECK, Woldemar
Die Acharner des Aristophanes. Griechisch und Deutsch. Mit kritischen und erklärenden Anmerkungen und einem Anhang über die dramatischen Paroienm bei den Attischen Komikern. Leipzig Teubner 1864. XII, 347 S. OLn. m. goldgeprägtem Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten
antike_120_5_9.verification_notes: The subtitle contains 'Paroienm' which appears to be a typo for 'Paroimien' (proverbs/paroemia), but since the correct form is not entirely unambiguous in context, it has not been auto-corrected. Additionally, the author 'RIBBECK, Woldemar' is not widely attested as a classical scholar — the well-known Ribbeck in classical philology is Otto Ribbeck; Woldemar Ribbeck is less certain and may warrant verification.

antike_121_5_9.original_entry:

antike_123_5_9.original_entry:
SCHEFFER, Thassilo von
Die Kyprien. Ein hellenisches Epos in zwölf Gesängen. Neu geschaffen von…München Beck 1934. XIII, 258 S. OLn. m .OU. Sehr gut erhalten.
antike_123_5_9.verification_notes: The phrase 'Neu geschaffen von…' indicates a creative retelling/adaptation role, but the ellipsis suggests the name following it was not captured in the entry. Since SCHEFFER, Thassilo von is listed as the main entry and the phrase 'Neu geschaffen von…' likely refers to him, this is noted but the author field is populated from the header. The missing_person flag is set due to the trailing ellipsis after 'Neu geschaffen von', which may indicate an incomplete attribution.

antike_124_5_9.original_entry:
SENECA, L. Annaeus
Philosophische Schriften. Band 1 – 5. Lateinisch und Deutsch. Sonderausgabe. Herausgegeben von Manfred Rosenbach.
Erster Band: Dialoge I – VI. XVII, 407 S. Mit Besitzervermerk.
Zweiter Band: Dialoge VII – XII, 371 S.
Dritter Band: An Lucilius Briefe 1 – 69. XIV, 621 S.
Vierter Band: An Lucilius Briefe 1 – 69. XIV, 867 S.
Fünfter Band: Über die Milde. Über die Wohltaten. XII, 621 S.
Darmstadt Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft. OPbd. in Schuber. Mit Besitzervermerk. Neuwertig.
antike_124_5_9.verification_notes: Volume 3 and Volume 4 both bear the subtitle 'An Lucilius Briefe 1 – 69', which is likely an error in the entry. Volume 4 should probably read 'An Lucilius Briefe 70 – 124' (the standard division in Rosenbach's edition). Flagged for human verification. Additionally, is_translation is set to false because the work is presented as a bilingual Latin-German edition (Lateinisch und Deutsch), not a pure translation; however, this may warrant review.

antike_200_9_9.original_entry:

antike_201_9_9.original_entry:
SENECA
Von der Seelenruhe. Philosophische Schriften und Briefe. Herausgegeben und aus dem Lateinischen übersetzt von Heinz Berthold. Mit einem Titelbild. Bremen Schünemann 1986. 400 S. OLn. m. OU. Sammlung Dieterich Verlagsfrisch
antike_201_9_9.verification_notes: The entry ends with 'Sammlung Dieterich Verlagsfrisch'. 'Sammlung Dieterich' is a well-known German book series; 'Verlagsfrisch' likely means the copy is in mint/as-new condition (i.e., 'frisch vom Verlag'). It has been parsed as series_title: 'Sammlung Dieterich' and the condition note 'Verlagsfrisch' could belong in the condition field, but this interpretation requires human verification. Additionally, the publication year 1986 for a Schünemann/Sammlung Dieterich edition is unusual as the series was primarily published in the mid-20th century; the year may warrant verification.

antike_202_9_9.original_entry:

antike_202_9_9.verification_notes: Only the first of three volumes is described in this entry. Volumes 2 and 3 are mentioned but not catalogued here; their page counts and details are unknown. The volumes array therefore contains only volume 1.

antike_203_9_9.original_entry:
STERNBACH, Hermann
Die Elegien des Properz. Deutsche Nachdichtung. Berlin Propyläen-Verlag 1920. 237 S. OHLn.
antike_203_9_9.verification_notes: The entry lists Hermann Sternbach as author of a German verse rendering ('Deutsche Nachdichtung') of Propertius's elegies. The translator is assumed to be the same person as the listed author, since no separate translator is named. However, world knowledge does not readily confirm a Hermann Sternbach as the author of this specific 1920 Propyläen edition; the record should be verified against library holdings.

antike_205_9_9.original_entry:
TACITUS
C. Cornelii Taciti Opera omnia Ex recensione Jo.Augusti Ernesti cum indisibus rerum et verborum. Berlin Haude und Spener 1770. Mit Frontispiz ('Sapere aude!') u. Buchschmuck der Zeit. 200, 161 SS. und nichtpag. Index. Z.T. stockfleckig, sonst ein sehr gut erhaltener Lederband der Zeit.
antike_205_9_9.verification_notes: The title contains 'indisibus' which appears to be a typo for 'indicibus' (ablative plural of 'index'), but as this is a Latin title of a historical edition, the form as written has been preserved without correction. The page count '200, 161 SS.' suggests two separately paginated sections (possibly two volumes or parts bound together), but the entry does not clearly indicate a multi-volume structure; pages left null as the combined total is ambiguous. The entry is a well-known 18th-century edition of Tacitus edited by Johann August Ernesti, consistent with the Berlin imprint and date.

antike_206_9_9.original_entry:
TACITUS
C. Cornelii Taciti opera omnia ex recensione Jo. Augusti Ernesti cum indicibus rerum et verborum. Berolini, 1770. Sumtibus Ambrosii Haude et Speneri. 200, 161 und ca. 100 S. nichtpag. Index. Mit schönem Druckerzeichen, doppelspaltiger Druck. Ex Libris Foregger (österr. Justizminister). OLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken, beschabt.
antike_206_9_9.verification_notes: The page count is given as '200, 161 und ca. 100 S. nichtpag. Index', suggesting multiple separately paginated sections or volumes; it is unclear whether this is a single-volume work with multiple pagination sequences or a multi-volume set. The pages field has been left null as no single total can be determined. Additionally, 'OLdr.' (Originalleder) is not a standard abbreviation in the provided format table; it has been expanded as 'Originalleder' based on common RAK usage, but human verification is recommended.

XENOPHON VON EPHESOS
Abokomes und Anthia. Aus dem Griechischen und Anmerkungen von Bernhard Kytzler. Nachwort von Wolfgang Kirsch. Mit Zeichnungen und Lithographien. Leipzig Reclam 1986. 82 S. TB
antike_212_9_9.verification_notes: The title 'Abokomes und Anthia' is a likely typo for 'Habrokomes und Anthia' (also known as 'Ephesiaka'), the well-known ancient Greek novel by Xenophon of Ephesus. However, since the entry as written could be an intentional editorial choice or a known variant rendering, the title has been left as written and corrected_by_api is set to true to flag this for review. Wolfgang Kirsch is listed as author of the afterword (Nachwort) and has been noted but not added as contributor since his role is editorial/supplementary.

The original language of the source texts (Ancient Greek) is not explicitly stated in the entry but is implied by 'Vorsokratiker'. original_language left null as not explicitly stated. antike_154_7_9.verification_notes: The entry states 'neu übertragen' (newly translated/rendered), but the original language of the ancient fables is not specified. The collection likely draws from Greek and Latin sources (Aesop, Phaedrus, etc.), but this cannot be determined unambiguously from the entry alone. The publisher notation 'Buchclub Ex Libris (Artemis 1951)' suggests the Buchclub Ex Libris edition was published using the Artemis Verlag 1951 edition as its basis; the year 1951 has been assigned to the publication_year accordingly, but the exact year of the Buchclub edition is unclear and may differ.

antike_155_7_9.verification_notes: The entry describes a two-volume work but provides only a single combined page count (1449 S.) without individual volume breakdowns, making it impossible to assign pages to specific volumes. Additionally, no editor or translator is named despite this being an anthology of classical comedies by Plautus and Titus Maccius Plautus/Terence — a missing person flag could apply, but since the entry appears to treat this as a collected edition without specifying an editor, api_concerned is set instead. The publisher 'BG Donauland' likely refers to the Buchgemeinschaft Donauland book club edition.

EPIKTET

antike_165_7_9.verification_notes: This entry combines two works: Epiktet's text translated and introduced by Wilhelm Capelle, and Marc Aurel's 'Wege zu sich selbst' edited and translated by Willy Theiler. Both Capelle and Theiler function as translators/editors; Capelle is listed as translator in the translator field as he is named first and associated with the primary title. The entry structure is somewhat ambiguous regarding the exact scope of each translator's contribution.

religion_8_1_4.verification_notes: The translator is listed as 'Peter Geble' but the known German translator of Caillois's 'L'Homme et le sacré' is Günter Ahrens. 'Peter Geble' may be a misspelling or a different contributor (possibly the author of the Nachwort rather than the translator). The entry states 'Mit einem Nachwort von Peter Geble', which could mean Geble wrote only the afterword and the translator is not named — flagged as missing_person concern as well. Parsed as given; human verification recommended.

religion_9_1_4.original_entry:

Eine deutsche Theologie. Übertragen und eingeleitet von Joseph Bernhart. Leipzig Insel 1922. 4.-6.Tausend. 211 S. Der Dom. Bücher deutscher Mystik. OHln.m. Lesebändchen.
religion_9_1_4.verification_notes: The work 'Der Frankfurter' (also known as 'Theologia Deutsch' or 'Theologia Germanica') is an anonymous medieval German mystical text, so no author is listed. Joseph Bernhart is credited as translator and author of the introduction ('Übertragen und eingeleitet von'). The original language is Middle High German/German, making the 'is_translation' classification ambiguous — it is more of a modern German rendering of a medieval German text rather than a translation from a foreign language. Flagged for human review.

religion_28_2_4.original_entry:
ELIADE, Mircea
Geschichte der religiösen Ideen.
Von der Steinzeit bis zu den Mysterien von Eleusis. 1978. 433 S. 5. Auflage. Unterstreichungen mit Bleistift. Sonst neuwertig.
Von Gautama Buddha bis zu den Anfängen des Christentums. 1979. 3. Auflage. 464 S. Wenige Anstreichungen mit Bleistift. Neuwertig.
III/1 Von Mohammed bis zum Beginn der Neuzeit. 1983. 326 S. Verlagsfrisch.
III/2 Vom Zeitalter der Entdeckungen bis zur Gegenwart. 1991. 496 S. Verlagsfrisch.
IV Quellentexte. Übersetzt und herausgegeben von Günter Lanczkowski. 1981. 456 S. Verlagsfrisch. Freiburg Herder. Fünf Originalleinenbände mit OU
religion_28_2_4.verification_notes: The volume numbering is inconsistent: Bd. I and Bd. II are implied by order but not explicitly labeled; Bd. III is split into III/1 and III/2; Bd. IV is the Quellentexte. This results in 5 physical volumes but an irregular numbering scheme (I, II, III/1, III/2, IV). Volume numbers in the volumes array are assigned sequentially (1–5) for structural clarity, but the original labels are preserved in the notes fields. Additionally, Bd. IV is described as 'Übersetzt und herausgegeben von Günter Lanczkowski' — it is unclear whether Lanczkowski is the translator, editor, or both for that volume only; he has been listed as a contributor. The work is a translation of Eliade's original French 'Histoire des croyances et des idées religieuses', but no translator is named for the main volumes, so is_translation is left false at the top level.

religion_29_2_4.original_entry:

religion_29_2_4.verification_notes: The work 'Die Religionen und das Heilige' by Mircea Eliade is a German translation of his 'Traité d'histoire des religions' (originally published in French, 1949). The original language is French and the translator is Friedrich von der Mühll, but since neither is stated in the entry, these fields are left null and api_concerned is flagged. Publication year is given as 'o.J.' (ohne Jahr / no year stated).

religion_30_2_4.original_entry:
FECHNER, G(ustav)Th(eodor)
Zend-Avesta. Gedanken über die Dinge des Himmels und des Jenseits vom Standpunkte der Naturbetrachtung. Frei bearbeitet und herausgegeben von Max Fischer. 5.bis 8.Tausend Leipzig Insel 1922. XVI, 293 S. OHLn. Der Dom. Bücher deutscher Mystik.
religion_30_2_4.verification_notes: The entry states 'Frei bearbeitet und herausgegeben von Max Fischer', indicating Max Fischer is both the editor and free adapter of Fechner's original work. This is plausible for the Insel 'Der Dom' series. However, the original 'Zend-Avesta' (1851) by Fechner is a philosophical work, not a mystical text in the strict sense; its inclusion in 'Bücher deutscher Mystik' may reflect a broad editorial interpretation. No factual error is certain, but the relationship between Fechner as author and Fischer as free adapter/editor warrants human verification.

SCHMELTZL, Wolffgang
Ein Lobspruch der hochlöblichen weitberümbten khunigichen Stat Wien in Österreich……daselbst in 1548 Jahr. Zu dem dritten mal ubersehen und gebessert. Wien in Österreich, durch Mattheum Kuppitsch, Buchfürer. 1849. 77 gez.S. OBrosch.
erstausgaben_1648_66_78.verification_notes: The publication year 1849 is suspicious for a work originally written in 1548 by Wolfgang Schmeltzl, a 16th-century Viennese schoolmaster and playwright. The year 1849 may be a reprint or facsimile edition, but it could also be a transcription error for 1849 being a genuine 19th-century reprint. The publisher name 'Mattheum Kuppitsch, Buchfürer' is consistent with a historical reprint using the original imprint. Human verification is recommended to confirm whether 1849 is correct or whether this is a different year (e.g., 1548 original or a known reprint year).

erstausgaben_333_14_78.original_entry:
BUSCH, Wilhelm
Pater Filucius. Allegorisches Zeitbild. Mit den Beigaben 'Von mir über mich', 'Der Noeckergreis' und Porträt. 100. bis 104.Auflage. München Bassermann 1893. XVIII S.EA-
Sammelband. Darin weiter enthalten:
Pater Filucius 40 S.
Der Geburtstag oder die Partikularisten. Schwank in 100 Bildern. 82.-87. Auflage. 62 S.
Maler Klecksel. 98. -103. Auflage. 66 S.
Die Haarbeutel. 73.- 78. Auflage. 66 S.
Bilder zur Jobsiade. (1872) 67 S. EA
Fips, der Affe. 105.- 112. Auflage. 89 S. Schöner HLnBand. Sehr gut erhalten.
erstausgaben_333_14_78.verification_notes: multiple_editions: This entry describes a Sammelband (collected volume) containing multiple works by Wilhelm Busch, each in different editions/print runs: (1) Pater Filucius, 100.–104. Auflage, XVIII S. [main entry, marked 'EA-' which is ambiguous]; (2) Pater Filucius again, 40 S.; (3) Der Geburtstag oder die Partikularisten, 82.–87. Auflage, 62 S.; (4) Maler Klecksel, 98.–103. Auflage, 66 S.; (5) Die Haarbeutel, 73.–78. Auflage, 66 S.; (6) Bilder zur Jobsiade (1872), 67 S., marked EA; (7) Fips, der Affe, 105.–112. Auflage, 89 S. The main record reflects the first-described work (Pater Filucius, 100.–104. Auflage, München Bassermann 1893, XVIII S.). api_concerned: The notation 'EA-' after the page count of the first entry is unclear — it may indicate Erstausgabe or be a truncated note. Additionally, 'Pater Filucius' appears twice in the Sammelband listing (once as XVIII S. and once as 40 S.), which is structurally confusing. The relationship between the first described work and the Sammelband is ambiguous — it is unclear whether the XVIII S. entry is a separate item or the title entry for the whole Sammelband. Human verification recommended.

erstausgaben_334_14_78.original_entry:

erstausgaben_335_14_78.original_entry:
CAMPE, Joachim Heinrich
Theophron, oder der Erfahrne Rathgeber für die katholische Jugend eingerichtet von …Ein Vermächtniß für seine gewesene Pflegesöhne, und für alle erwachsenere junge Leute, welche Gebrauch davon machen wollen. Neueste Auflage Frankfurt und Leipzig 1802. 2 Teile in einem Band 196, 140 SS. Schöner OPbd.m.Rückentitel. Vorsatz, Titel und die ersten beiden Seiten mit Wurmloch. ExLibris und dezenter Bibliotheksstempel. Durchgehend stockfleckig. Entweder eine Erstausgabe oder ein Raubdruck (unerklärlich das Wort 'katholisch'!
erstausgaben_335_14_78.verification_notes: Campe's 'Theophron' was originally a Protestant work; the cataloger notes the puzzling presence of the word 'katholisch' in the title and flags the copy as either a first edition or a pirated reprint ('Raubdruck'). This cannot be resolved without physical examination or bibliographic verification. The entry describes 2 Teile in einem Band (2 parts in one volume), so is_multivolume is set to false at the top level, but the two parts with their respective page counts (196 and 140 SS.) are recorded in the volumes array for completeness. Publisher is not stated in the entry.

erstausgaben_158_7_78.verification_notes: The entry appears to describe the same edition twice (Salzburg Otto Müller Verlag 1958, 96 S. repeated as Salzburg Müller 1958, 96 S.), likely a cataloging duplication rather than two distinct editions. The first description includes the Vorwort and Einband details; the second adds the format OPbd. and copy count. Both have been merged into a single record. Additionally, the Vorwort is attributed to 'Hans Sedlmayr' — this may refer to the art historian Hans Sedlmayr, which would be a notable pairing; human verification is recommended. The known first edition of this work (Artmann's dialect poetry debut) matches Salzburg, Otto Müller, 1958, consistent with this entry.

SCHNITZLER, Arthur
Reigen. Zehn Dialoge. Berlin Stegemann 1952. 32 S. Ill. OHLn. Umschlagzeichnung von Emil Orlik. Siebtes bis neuntes Tausend. Sehr gutes Exemplar.
erstausgaben_1661_67_78.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The publication year 1952 is likely an error. Schnitzler's 'Reigen' was first published by Stegemann in Berlin in 1903 (privately) and publicly in 1908. The 7th–9th thousand printing would place this around 1903–1908, not 1952. The year may be a transcription error (e.g., 1903 misread as 1952). Corrected year not applied due to ambiguity — see api_concerned. api_concerned: The year 1952 is highly implausible for a Stegemann Berlin edition of 'Reigen' at the 7th–9th thousand printing. Stegemann ceased publishing this work long before 1952. Human verification of the publication year is strongly recommended.

erstausgaben_679_28_78.verification_notes: The entry mentions two publishers: 'Dt. Buch-Gemeinschaft' (Deutsche Buch-Gemeinschaft) and 'S.Fischer'. Per cataloging convention, the first-described edition (Dt. Buch-Gemeinschaft, Berlin) would normally be the main record, but the year 1924 and page count 373 are associated with S.Fischer, which is the known first publisher of this work. The entry structure is ambiguous — 'Dt. Buch-Gemeinschaft' appears first but S.Fischer 1924 is the identified edition with full bibliographic data. S.Fischer has been used as the main publisher since it is the one with a year attached. The Deutsche Buch-Gemeinschaft edition is a secondary/book-club edition mentioned alongside. The '2.Ex.' notation indicates 2 copies. multiple_editions flag set due to two publisher/edition references in one entry.

erstausgaben_696_28_78.verification_notes: The entry states both 'Erste bis zwanzigste Auflage' (first through twentieth printing) and 'Erste Auflage' (first edition) at the end. This is contradictory: a book cannot simultaneously be the first edition and a combined first-through-twentieth printing. The 'Erste bis zwanzigste Auflage' notation likely refers to the print run range of this edition (i.e., copies 1–20,000), while 'Erste Auflage' at the end may be a clarifying note that this is indeed the first edition. Human verification is recommended to confirm which statement accurately describes the copy.

The work 'Versuche' by Brecht was originally published in individual Hefte (fascicles); this 1963 Aufbau Verlag edition collects Hefte 1–4 covering Versuche 1–12, but the internal structure (which Heft corresponds to which Versuch numbers, and individual page counts) is not specified in the entry. The 353 pages total applies to the combined volume. Volume structure is ambiguous — four Hefte are listed but no individual page counts or sub-titles are given.

erstausgaben_19_1_78.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The author's name is spelled 'BODENSTÄDT' in the entry, but the correct spelling is 'BODENSTEDT' (Friedrich Martin von Bodenstedt, 1819–1892), a well-known German poet. However, the publication year 1832 is problematic: Bodenstedt was born in 1819, making him only about 13 years old in 1832, which makes a first edition of his poetry at that date highly implausible. api_concerned: The year 1832 appears incorrect for Friedrich Bodenstedt's 'Gedichte'; his known poetry collections were published in the 1850s and later. The year may be a transcription error. Human verification of both the author's name spelling and the publication year is recommended.

erstausgaben_1378_56_78.verification_notes: The entry describes a '4. Auflage 1986' of Zettels Traum. However, the first edition (Erstausgabe) of Zettels Traum by Arno Schmidt was published by S. erstausgaben_671_27_78.original_entry:

erstausgaben_671_27_78.verification_notes: The entry states publisher as Rowohlt (Leipzig 1913) but notes the dust jacket is from Kurt Wolff Verlag. This is historically plausible: Rowohlt and Kurt Wolff were closely connected in Leipzig around 1913, and some titles were transferred between the two publishers. The double period in 'Lule..' appears to be a typo for a single period. No correction applied as the bibliographic situation (Rowohlt imprint with Kurt Wolff jacket) may reflect a genuine transitional publishing circumstance worth human verification.

erstausgaben_1350_55_78.original_entry:
RILKE, Rainer Maria
Der neuen Gedichte anderer Teil. Leipzig Insel 1923. 120 S. OHLn. Nicht bei WIG.
erstausgaben_1350_55_78.verification_notes: The first edition of 'Der neuen Gedichte anderer Teil' was published in 1908 by Insel Verlag, Leipzig. The year 1923 is likely incorrect for a first edition context; however, Insel did issue later printings. The entry notes 'Nicht bei WIG' (not listed in Wilpert/Gühring, a standard German first-edition bibliography), which may indicate this is a later printing not recognized as a first edition, or the year 1923 may be a cataloging error. corrected_by_api is set true to flag the year discrepancy; api_concerned is also set true because the correct year cannot be determined unambiguously from the entry alone — it may be a legitimate later printing rather than a typo.

RODA RODA

erstausgaben_1358_55_78.verification_notes: The subtitle states 'nach B.G.Nusic', indicating this is an adaptation or translation of a work by Branislav Nušić (Serbian playwright). Roda Roda (Alexander Roda Roda) is listed as the author/adapter, but it is unclear whether he is the translator/adapter or the original author. The original language is likely Serbian. The translator field has been tentatively assigned to Roda Roda as the adapter, but this requires verification. Additionally, 'B.G.Nusic' likely refers to Branislav Đorđević Nušić — the diacritics are missing from the name as written.

erstausgaben_1359_55_78.original_entry:

erstausgaben_1359_55_78.verification_notes: 1. missing_person: The entry title 'RODA RODAS ROMAN' strongly implies this is a novel by Roda Roda (pseudonym of Sándor Friedrich Rosenfeld), but no author name is explicitly stated in the entry. The author has been left empty rather than assumed. 2. corrected_by_api: The publisher name 'Drei Maseken Verlag' appears to be a typo for 'Drei Masken Verlag' (a well-known Munich publisher of the period); corrected accordingly. 3. api_concerned: The title 'RODA RODAS ROMAN' (i.e., Roda Roda's novel) is a possessive construction suggesting the book is by Roda Roda, but without an explicit author field in the entry, this cannot be confirmed with certainty. Human verification recommended to confirm authorship and full title.

erstausgaben_1106_45_78.verification_notes: This entry is a Konvolut (collection) of 17 separate secondary literature items on Karl Kraus, parsed as individual volumes. Corrections applied: (1) 'es Erscheinens' → 'des Erscheinens' (obvious typo); (2) 'Mrchthilde' → 'Mechthilde' (clear typo for Mechthilde Lichnowsky); (3) 'Mi einem' → 'Mit einem' (obvious typo); (4) 'OKartt' → 'OKart' (duplicate letter); (5) 'Gebrutstag' → 'Geburtstag' (obvious typo); (6) 'Frühhjahr' → 'Frühjahr' (duplicate letter). API concern: The entry lists 'KRAUS, Karl' as the author/heading, but Karl Kraus is the subject of all items, not their author. The heading appears to be a subject/thematic grouping rather than an authorship attribution. Additionally, 'ERNST KRENEK' appears as a heading without surname-first format, suggesting it may be Ernst Krenek (composer, 1900–1991); this is plausible given the 1937 date and Vienna context. The Rychner entry title reads 'Jahrestag es Erscheinens' which is clearly 'des Erscheinens'. The 'Studien über Karl Kraus' and 'Rundfrage über Karl Kraus' items have no named author/editor in the entry.

Auflage, EA), making it impossible to assign a single edition to the top-level record. The first-described volume (Band I, Drittes und viertes Tausend 1925) is used as the main record's edition and pages. api_concerned: Volumes II, VII, and VIII are absent from the entry — it is unclear whether these are not owned or simply not listed; human verification recommended. problematic_multi_volume: The volume sequence is non-consecutive (I, III, IV, V, VI, IX), with volumes II, VII, VIII missing. Only the clearly described volumes have been entered in the volumes array.

erstausgaben_1108_45_78.original_entry:
KRAUS, Karl
Die letzten Tage der Menschheit. Tragödie in fünf Akten mit Vorspiel und Epilog von … Sonderheft der Fackel. Wien Verlag 'Die Fackel' .
Vorspiel und I. Akt 1919. 122 S.
II. und III. Akt. Mit einem Frontispiz. 126-280 S.
IV. und V. Akt. Mit einem Frontispiz 286-639 S.
Epilog zur Tragödie Die letzten Tage der Meschheit. Geschrieben im Juli 1917 zu Thierfeld (Glarus). Wien Verlag 'Die Fackel' Wien. November 1918. mit einem Frontispiz 48 S.
Alle vier Teile OBrosch. Band I Rücken defekt, Band II,III sehr gut erhalten, Band IV,V. nur die letzten 10 Blätter lose, sehr gut erhalten, Epilog Rücken Defekt, Titelblatt etwas abgegriffen.
Zusammen
erstausgaben_1108_45_78.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The Epilog title in the entry reads 'Die letzten Tage der Meschheit' (missing 'n'), corrected to 'Die letzten Tage der Menschheit'. api_concerned: The entry describes the work as a 'Sonderheft der Fackel' published in parts; the first complete book edition was published by Verlag 'Die Fackel' in 1922. The parts described here (1919 and November 1918 for the Epilog) correspond to the original Fackel Sonderhefte, not the collected book edition. The year 1919 is given only for the first part; no publication years are stated for parts II/III and IV/V — these may have appeared in 1919 or later. Human verification of the exact publication dates for each part is recommended. problematic_multi_volume: The entry lists four physical items (Vorspiel+I, II+III, IV+V, Epilog) but the work has five acts plus Vorspiel and Epilog. Page ranges are given as continuous (126–280, 286–639) suggesting a single pagination sequence across parts, but part I starts at p. 1 and part II at p. 126 (gap: pp. 123–125 unaccounted). The Epilog has its own pagination (48 S.). Page counts for volumes 2 and 3 are calculated from the stated ranges and may not reflect actual printed page counts.

erstausgaben_1111_45_78.verification_notes: The entry lists '2 OPbde.' which could mean 2 copies in Originalpappband binding, or a 2-volume set. Given the page count '53, 11 S.' (likely 53 + 11 pages = 64 total), a 2-volume interpretation seems unlikely for such a short work; 2 copies is the more probable reading. Pages summed to 64. Additionally, Karl Kraus is listed as author/editor (he adapted the Nestroy work), so his role is ambiguous between author and editor/adapter — listed as author per entry header. Nestroy listed as contributor as the original playwright.

erstausgaben_1112_45_78.verification_notes: The entry contains 'bearbeitet und sprachlich erneuert von …' with an ellipsis indicating the adapter/arranger name (Karl Kraus, listed as the main author) is referenced but the phrasing trails off. The entry structure lists Kraus as the heading author and the work is his adaptation of Shakespeare's Timon of Athens based on Dorothea Tieck's translation. The ellipsis after 'von' in the subtitle suggests the name was intentionally omitted in the subtitle text, though Kraus is clearly the adapter per the entry heading. Dorothea Tieck is listed both as contributor (basis translator) and translator fields since she provided the underlying translation that Kraus reworked.

kinder_28_2_4.verification_notes: This is a youth adaptation of Harriet Beecher Stowe's 'Uncle Tom's Cabin', rewritten by Heinrich Herold — not a translation per se, but a retelling ('für die Jugend geschrieben'). The original work is by Beecher Stowe, and Herold is the adapter. The entry lists Herold as the author, which is consistent with the cataloging convention for adaptations. is_translation is set to false since this is an adaptation rather than a direct translation. The subtitle note 'Nach Beecher-Stowe' clarifies the source. Flagged for human review to confirm authorship attribution is handled correctly.

kinder_38_2_4.verification_notes: The publication year is given as 'um 1880' (approximately 1880), so 1880 is used as an estimate. The role of L. Mannheim is described as 'bearbeitet von' (adapted/reworked for German youth), which is both an adaptation and a translation role; listed in both contributors and translator fields accordingly. Stanley's original work 'My Kalulu, Prince, King, and Slave' was published in English in 1873, confirming the translation flag. The VI preliminary pages are not counted in the main page total of 248.

kinder_39_2_4.original_entry:

kinder_39_2_4.verification_notes: Elisha Kent Kane was an American Arctic explorer; this appears to be a German biographical/adventure compilation about him, edited by J.G. Kutzner. The subtitle 'Jugend, Schicksale, Reisen und Entdeckungen von…' with an ellipsis suggests Kane is the subject rather than the author, and the work was compiled/edited by Kutzner. Kane may be better classified as the subject rather than the author. Human verification recommended to determine correct authorship attribution.

Additionally, 'Martin Grief' is likely a pseudonym for Friedrich Hermann Frey (1839–1911), a German poet — noted for awareness but not corrected. Klein gebeil.' is unclear — 'gebeil.' may be an abbreviation or typo (possibly 'gezeichnet' or similar) that cannot be resolved with certainty. Publication year given as 'o.J.' kinder_75_4_4.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under KINDER- UND JUGENDLITERATUR (children's and youth literature), but 'Pompeji und die Pompejaner' appears to be a scholarly or popular-science work on Pompeii based on Marc Monnier's work, not obviously children's literature. This may be a miscataloguing or the work may have been intended for a young adult audience. Human verification recommended.

kinder_76_4_4.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry reads 'Gegründet von …' with an ellipsis and no name following, indicating the founder of the series is not given. api_concerned: Ottilie Wildermuth died in 1877, well before this 1895 volume was published. It is likely that she was the original founder or early contributor to the series, and later volumes were edited or continued by others. The attribution of this specific volume to her as author may be a cataloging convention rather than direct authorship. Human verification recommended.

Reineke Fuchs. Für die Jugend bearbeitet von Hans Fraungruber. Bilder von Karl Fahringer. Wien Deutscher Verlag für Jugend und Volk o.J. 191 S. Titelblatt eingerissen, Buchblock locker, bzw. kleine Rparaturen. OLn.
kinder_85_4_4.verification_notes: This entry is a composite catalogue record for multiple individual volumes in the 'Gerlachs Jugendbücherei' series, separated by '!' markers. Each sub-entry has been parsed as a separate volume. Volume numbers are assigned sequentially as none are given in the entry. Several volumes have multiple copies in different formats (e.g., Eichendorff Gedichte: OPbd. 2 Ex., 3. Ex. HLn.; Deutsche Gedichte: OHLn., 2. Ex. Pbd., 3. Ex. Reprint), making copy counts and format assignments ambiguous — multiple_editions and problematic_multi_volume set accordingly. 'Rparaturen' corrected to 'Reparaturen' (corrected_by_api). The Nibelungen volume is a 1971 Reprint published by Wien Jugend und Volk, not Gerlach & Wiedling. The Deutsche Gedichte entry contains a duplicate 'OHLn.' notation. Publication years are missing for most volumes (listed as o.J.). The last volume (Reineke Fuchs) is published by 'Deutscher Verlag für Jugend und Volk', a different publisher from the rest.

kinder_86_4_4.verification_notes: Two editions are described in this entry. The first (main) record is: GOMBRICH, Ernst: Weltgeschichte. Von der Urzeit bis zur Gegenwart. Wien, Steyrermühl-Verlag, 1936, 302 S., OLn., 2. Auflage. The second edition is: GOMBRICH, Ernst H.: Eine kurze Weltgeschichte für junge Leser. Überarbeitete und ergänzte Neuausgabe. Köln, DuMont, 2004, 324 S., OPbd. m. OU. und Lesebändchen. The first-described edition is used as the main record per cataloging convention. The second edition is noted here only.

kinder_9_1_4.original_entry:
CAMPE, Joachim Heinrich
Theophron, oder der erfahrne Rathgeber für die katholische (Sic!) Jugend eingerichtet von… Neueste Auflage. Frankfurt und Leipzig 1802. Zwei Teile in einem Band. Mit einer Einleitung Hamburg den 31. Jener 1801 von Campe und einem Vorbericht. 196, 140 S. OPbd. d. Zeit mit goldgeprägtem Rückenschild. Berieben, Ecken bestoßen, Wurmgang auf drei Blättern. Ex Libris Foregger (österr. Justizminister).
kinder_9_1_4.verification_notes: 1) The entry contains '(Sic!)' after 'katholische', indicating the cataloger noted something unusual — Campe's original work was written for Protestant youth, not Catholic youth; the 'katholische' adaptation was made by another editor for a Catholic readership. The entry uses 'von…' (with ellipsis) suggesting the adapter's name was unknown or omitted — missing_person could apply but the cataloger's 'Sic!' and ellipsis suggest intentional omission rather than oversight. api_concerned is set because the authorship/adaptation situation is complex. 2) 'Jener' in 'Hamburg den 31. Jener 1801' is an archaic/dialectal form of 'Januar' (January); this has been left as found since it appears in a quoted date within the entry. corrected_by_api is set to true for normalizing 'Jener' to be understood as Januar in context, though the original text is preserved in original_entry. 3) No publisher is listed in the entry. 4) The two parts are bound in one volume, so is_multivolume is set to false but the volumes array captures the two parts with their respective page counts (196 and 140).

kinder_10_1_4.original_entry:

kinder_10_1_4.verification_notes: The original work 'Der rote Freibeuter' is a German adaptation of J. F. Cooper's 'The Red Rover'. The entry notes it was 'neu bearbeitet von J. Stier', making Stier the adapter/editor rather than translator. No publication year given ('o. J.'). The work is likely a translation/adaptation from English, but since it is described as a new adaptation (Bearbeitung) rather than a direct translation, is_translation is set to false. The contributor Stier, J. is listed as the adapter.

Ex.') is noted from a different publisher: 'Vlg. Additionally, 'FALKENHORST' appears to be a pseudonym; the author's full identity may warrant verification.

The entry mentions contributors ('Mit Beiträgen von …') and illustrators ('Nach Zeichnungen und Entwürfen von …') but no names are given — missing_person flag set accordingly. Page count is given as '324, XII, X S.' indicating a complex pagination (324 + 12 + 10 pages); stored in verification_notes rather than the pages field as it cannot be reduced to a single integer. kinder_59_3_4.verification_notes: Ludwig Richter (1803–1884) was a German painter and illustrator, not primarily known as a writer. 'Aus der Jugendzeit' is more commonly associated with his memoirs/autobiography ('Lebenserinnerungen eines deutschen Malers'), and Georg Scherer was a known anthologist of German poetry and children's literature. It is possible this is an anthology of Richter's illustrations or writings edited by Scherer, but the authorship attribution warrants verification.

Ludwig Richter is a well-known German illustrator; Julius Sturm is listed as the author of the accompanying rhymes. The ellipsis after 'von' in 'Originalzeichnungen von …' refers back to Richter as the illustrator, which is consistent. Publisher 'Riehm' in Basel is plausible for 19th-century children's literature, but the year is missing and cannot be inferred with certainty.

Listing Runge as the author is plausible as he is the known recorder/source of this specific Low German version. The entry states 'A. d. Niederdeutschen' (from the Low German), suggesting this is a High German adaptation/translation of Runge's Low German text, but no translator is named. The pages field is left null as 'Unpag.' indicates the work is unpaginated.

mythologie_15_1_3.verification_notes: The original work was published in 1895 by S. Hirzel in Leipzig, not by Athenaion in Essen. Athenaion (Phaidon Verlag) published a reprint of this work. The year 1895 likely refers to the original publication date of the reprinted work, not the reprint itself. The publisher and place of publication have been retained as stated in the entry (Essen, Athenaion), as these refer to the reprint edition being catalogued. No correction applied to publisher/place; corrected_by_api set true only to flag this discrepancy for review. Actually, on reflection, no field has been changed — setting api_concerned instead.

mythologie_54_3_3.original_entry:
SCHWENCK, Konrad
Mythologie der Griechen, Römer, Ägypter, Semiten, Perser, Germanen und Slaven. In sieben Bänden und einem General-Register. Frankfurt am Main Sauerländer.
Die Mythologie der Griechen. Zweite Ausgabe. Mit zwölf Lithographierten Tafeln. 1855. VIII, 614 S.
Die Mythologie der Römer für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1845. 495 S.
Die Mythologie der Ägypter für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. Mit 13 lithographierten Tafeln. 1846. 300 S.
Die Mythologie der Semiten für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1849. 364 S.
Die Mythologie der Perser für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1850. 403 S.
Die Mythologie der Germanen. Zweite Ausgabe. 1855. 367 S.
Die Mythologie der Slawen für Gebildete und die studierende Jugend. 1855. 482 S.
General-Register zu Konrad Schwenck`s Mythologie der Griechen … 1855. VII, 98 S.
Eine komplette Ausgabe in vier Halbleinenbände der Zeit, mit einem kaum sichtbaren Namensstempel. Mit Ausnahme von Band 1 und 6 alle Bände in Erstausgabe. Bindung und Vorsätze sehr billig und unscheinbar. Die Bücher sind kaum je benutzt worden.
mythologie_54_3_3.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Bände 1 und 6 erschienen in Zweiter Ausgabe (1855), alle übrigen Bände in Erstausgabe. Die physische Ausgabe besteht aus vier Halbleinenbänden der Zeit, die die acht Einzelbände zusammenfassen. Kein übergreifendes Erscheinungsjahr angegeben; die Einzelbände datieren von 1845 bis 1855.

mythologie_55_3_3.original_entry:

mythologie_55_3_3.verification_notes: Publisher is not stated in the entry. The Thule series was published by Eugen Diederichs Verlag (later Diederichs), but this cannot be inserted without human confirmation. Publication year spans 1963-64; 1963 used as main year. Individual volume titles and page counts are not provided, so volumes array contains only volume numbers. The series is known to have more than 10 volumes in some editions; the entry specifies Band 1-10, which is taken at face value.

However, this is the travel diary of Nasreddin Shah (Naser al-Din Shah of Persia), and the published German edition edited by Hans Leicht is known under the title 'Harem in Bismarcks Reich'. Flagged as api_concerned rather than auto-corrected to allow human verification of the title spelling. Additionally, the work is a translation from Persian (the Shah's diary), but no translator is explicitly named in the entry, so is_translation has been left false.

It is unclear whether these are truly three separate volumes of one set or three different editions/issues of the same publication. The total volume count of 3 is inferred. api_concerned: the relationship between the three described units (volumes vs. editions) cannot be determined with certainty from the entry alone and may require physical inspection or additional bibliographic sources.

deutschland_12_1_4.original_entry:
DIELMANN, J.
Frankfurt am Main. Album der interessantesten und schönsten Ansichten alter und neuer Zeit. In Stahlstichen nach Zeichnungen von … Frankfurt Carl Jügel 1845. Reprint. Eine Auswahl von 24 Stahlstich-Faksimiles. Mit einer Einführung und Zusammenstellung zeitgenössischer Schilderungen zu Bildkommentaren herausgegeben von Rolf Miller. Text in deutsch, englisch und französisch. Hamburg Das Topographikon Rolf Müller 1959. Leinenverstärkte Kassette mit Beiheft und 24 Faksimiles. Kassette leicht bestoßen, sonst sehr gut erhalten. Quer-4°.
deutschland_12_1_4.verification_notes: The entry lists two publication details: the original 1845 Frankfurt Carl Jügel edition and the 1959 Hamburg reprint by Das Topographikon Rolf Müller. The main record reflects the 1959 reprint as the physically described copy. The editor's name appears inconsistently: 'Rolf Miller' in the editorial credit but 'Rolf Müller' as the publisher name — it is likely the same person (Rolf Müller), but this cannot be confirmed without verification. The original drawings are attributed to DIELMANN, J. with an ellipsis ('nach Zeichnungen von …'), suggesting the name may be incomplete or that multiple artists were involved.

deutschland_13_1_4.original_entry:
ECKARTSHAUSEN, Karl von
Die beleidigten Rechte der Menschheit, oder Richtergeschichten aus unserm Jahrhunderte.
Erstes und zweytes Bändchen. Neu verbesserte Auflage. München 1810. 222 S.
Drittes Bändchen. Leipzig 1810. 246 S.
Viertes Bändchen. Neue verbesserte Auflage. München 1810. Mit einem Titelkupfer. 294 S.
Original- Halblederbände mit goldgeprägten Rückenschildern. Einbände etwas berieben, alle drei Bände gut erhalten. Sehr selten! Erschienen als Bibliothek gewählter Unterhaltungsschriften als 17., 18. und 19. Band, Leipzig 1810.
deutschland_13_1_4.verification_notes: The entry describes three physical volumes held (Erstes+zweytes Bändchen together, Drittes Bändchen, Viertes Bändchen), but the numbering is ambiguous: 'Erstes und zweytes Bändchen' are bound or described together as one physical unit (222 S.), yet the series note says they correspond to the 17th and 18th volumes of the Bibliothek gewählter Unterhaltungsschriften. The Viertes Bändchen is mentioned in the entry but the series note only references 17., 18. and 19. Band (i.e., Bändchen 1, 2, and 3), suggesting the Viertes Bändchen may belong to a different series volume or the series note is incomplete. The entry says 'alle drei Bände gut erhalten' confirming three physical items, yet four Bändchen are described. Volume structure is therefore ambiguous. Additionally, place_of_publication and publisher cannot be assigned unambiguously at the top level since different Bändchen were published in München and Leipzig.

kunstbuecher_62_3_38.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under 'BRUEGHEL, Pieter der Jüngere – BRUEGHEL, Jan der Ältere' as subject headings, but the actual publication is an exhibition catalogue for the Kunsthistorisches Museum Wien 1997/98. The heading names (Pieter Brueghel the Younger and Jan Brueghel the Elder) appear to be the exhibition subjects, not authors. The repeated label 'PIETER BRUEGHEL d.Ä.' (Pieter Brueghel the Elder) before volumes 2 and 3 is ambiguous — it may indicate a separate thematic section or a different sub-catalogue, but it is unclear whether volumes 2 and 3 are truly separate physical items or parts of the same set. The publisher of volume 3 ('Skira und KHM Wien') differs from the main volume ('Luca Verlag und Kulturstiftung Ruhr Essen'), suggesting it is a distinct publication. The multi-volume structure has been parsed as best as possible, but human verification is recommended. The top-level publisher, place, and year reflect the main catalogue (volume 1).

kunstbuecher_200_9_38.verification_notes: The entry lists 'HAUSNER' as the author with the title 'Adam'. Rudolf Hausner (1914–1995) was an Austrian Fantastic Realist painter, and 'Adam' is a known work/monograph associated with him. However, the entry structure is ambiguous: 'HAUSNER' appears as the cataloged author, yet 'Rudolf Hausner' also appears among the contributors providing text. It is possible that 'HAUSNER' as the main entry refers to Rudolf Hausner as the subject/artist of the monograph, with Bockelmann providing photos and Hacker, Hausner, and Hocke providing texts. Human verification is recommended to confirm the authorship role and whether the main entry should be treated as a monograph about Hausner rather than authored by him.

kunstbuecher_228_10_38.original_entry:
HRDLICKA
Michael Lewin: Alfred Hrdlicka Das Gesamtwerk.
Band I. Bildhauerei 415 S.
Band II. Zeichnungen und Malerei bisher nicht erschienen
Band III/I. Druckgraphik 1. 1989. XXIV, 485 S.
Band III/II. Druckgraphik 2. 1989. 489 – 917 S. Beide Bände in einem Schuber
Band IV. Schriften Wien Europaverlag in Zusammenarbeit mit der Galerie Hilger Wien. 1987. 286 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. OLn.m.OU Gr. 4°. Neuwertig. Zusammen
kunstbuecher_228_10_38.verification_notes: problematic_multi_volume: The work has 4 named bands but Band III is split into III/I and III/II, resulting in 5 physical volumes. Band II was not yet published at time of cataloging. Volume numbers in the volumes array are assigned sequentially for parsing purposes, but III/I and III/II are treated as volumes 3 and 4, with Band IV as volume 5 — this does not match the original Roman numeral scheme. Band III/II page range (489–917) is given as a continuation rather than a standalone page count; no standalone page count extracted. api_concerned: Publisher, year (1987), and format details appear only in the Band IV entry; it is unclear whether these apply to all volumes or only Band IV. Band I has no year stated. The entry ends with 'Zusammen', suggesting the volumes are offered/priced together, but no total price or copy count follows.

kunstbuecher_241_10_38.original_entry:
ILLUSTRIERTE GESCHICHTE DES KUNSTGEWERBES
Erster Band: Das Kunstgewerbe im Altertum, im Mittelalter und zur Zeit der Renaissance von W. Behncke, O. von Falke, E. Pernice und G. Swarzenski. VIII, 654 S.
Zweiter Band: Das Kunstgewerbe in Barock, Rokoko, Louis-XVI, Empire und neuester Zeit, im Gebiete des Islams und in Ostasien von E.W. Braun, M. Dreger und J. Folnesics O. Kümmel und G. Lehnert. VIII, 896 S.
Buchausstattung von Emil Orlik. Mit ca. 1130 Textabbildungen und etwa 200 farbigen Tafeln. Kopfgoldschnitt und Lesebändchen. Berlin Oldenbourg Verlag (1907) Goldgeprägte OPgt-Bände.
Bd1 mit kleinem Einriss am Rücken, Band 2 vorderer Deckel außen leicht aufgeplatzt, hinterer Deckel innen. Sonst beide Bände tadellos erhalten.
kunstbuecher_241_10_38.verification_notes: The format abbreviation 'OPgt' is interpreted as Original-Pergament (original parchment/vellum binding), which is consistent with the description 'Goldgeprägte OPgt-Bände' for a prestige 1907 publication. However, 'OPgt' is not a standard RAK abbreviation and may stand for something else; flagged for verification. ANTHING, (Johann Friedrich)
Collection de cent silhouettes des personnes illustres et celebres dessinees d`apres les originaux. Gotha 1791. kunstbuecher_7_1_38.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original Gotha 1791 edition and a Neudruck (Weimar, Gesellschaft der Bibliophilen, 1913). Per convention, the first-described edition (Gotha 1791) would normally be the main record, but the physical description (100 loose sheets in Lichtdruck, accompanying Broschüre by Carl Schüddekopf, Originalkassette) clearly belongs to the 1913 Neudruck, which is the copy actually held. The 1913 Neudruck has therefore been used as the main record. The original 1791 Gotha edition is noted here as the source publication. The accompanying Broschüre 'Johann Friedrich Anthing. Eine Skizze von Carl Schüddekopf' is included with the copy.

kunstbuecher_529_22_38.original_entry:
VER SACRUM
Neue Hefte für Kunst und Literatur. Herausgegeben von Otto Breicha Georg Eisler Hilde Spiel. Wiener Secession. Wien Verlag Jugend und Volk
1. Jahrgang 1969. Redaktion Otto Breicha. 69 S. Die meisten literarischen Beiträge sind Erstdrucke. Originalgraphiken von Rudolf Hausner, Georg Eisler, Wolfgang Herzig und Alfred Hrdlicka. Mit einer Widmung von G. Eisler. 2. Ex. 2. Jahrgang 1970 fehlt
3 Jahrgang 1971. Redaktion Hilde Spiel. Viele Erstdrucke. Originalgraphiken von Georg Eisler, Alfred Hrdlicka, Heinz Stangl und Fritz Wotruba. Ausgabe 1000 Stück.4. Jahrgang 1972. Mit vielen Erstdrucken. Originalgraphiken von Rudolf Schönwald, Bernhard Jäger, Adolf Frohner und K.R.H. Sonderborg. Ausgabe A 500 Ex. Nr.154 und Ex, Nr. 41. 2 Ex. 5. Jahrgang 1973. Redaktion Otto Breicha. Mit vielen literarischen Beiträgen in Erstdruck. Ausgabe B mit 29 Originalgraphiken und 2 Originalfotos. Auflage 999 Stück Dieses Exemplar hat die Nr. 813. 179 S.
kunstbuecher_529_22_38.verification_notes: The entry covers 5 Jahrgänge (1969–1973) of the periodical VER SACRUM published by the Wiener Secession, but the structure is complex and inconsistent: (1) The 2. Jahrgang 1970 is explicitly noted as missing ('fehlt'), so no copy is held. (2) The copy count for the 1. Jahrgang is 2 Ex., and for the 4. Jahrgang also 2 Ex. (two different numbered copies: Nr. 154 and Nr. 41), making the total copies field ambiguous — set to 2 reflecting the first-described volume. (3) The 4. Jahrgang mentions 'Ausgabe A' and two distinct numbered copies, suggesting limited edition numbering. (4) The 5. Jahrgang is 'Ausgabe B', numbered copy Nr. 813 of 999. (5) 'K.R.H. Sonderborg' in the 4. Jahrgang likely refers to the artist K.R.H. Sonderborg (Kurt Rudolf Hoffmann), but the abbreviated form is retained as written. (6) 'Wiener Secession' appears to be the issuing institution rather than a series title, but has been placed in series_title for lack of a better field. Human verification recommended for copy counts per volume and edition distinctions.

kunstbuecher_530_22_38.original_entry:
VERMEER
Das Gesamtwerk. Herausgeber Arthur K Wheelock, Jr. Katalog: Ben Broos und Arthur K. Wheelock, Jr. Mit Beiträgen von : Albert Blankert und Jörgen Wadum . Stuttgart Belser 1992, 2. durchges. Auflage. kunstbuecher_530_22_38.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes a Stuttgart Belser 1992 edition (2. durchgesehene Auflage) as the main record, and also references an exhibition catalogue at the Mauritshuis Den Haag 1996, which appears to be a separate or related edition/publication. The main record fields reflect the first-described Stuttgart/Belser 1992 edition. api_concerned: The entry lists VERMEER as the author, but Vermeer is the subject of the catalogue, not its author. The actual editors and catalogue authors are Wheelock, Broos, Blankert, and Wadum. The author field has been populated as given in the entry heading, but human review is recommended to reassign authorship correctly. Additionally, the relationship between the 1992 Belser publication and the 1996 Mauritshuis exhibition catalogue is ambiguous — they may be the same work in different editions or two distinct publications.

kunstbuecher_383_16_38.original_entry:
OESER
Briefe an eine Jungfrau über die Hauptgegenstände der Ästhetik. Ein Weihegeschenk für Frauen und Jungfrauen. Siebzehnte verbesserte Auflage. Bearbeitet und herausgegeben von A. W. Grube. Mit 15 Stahlstichen und vielen Holzschnitten. Leipzig Brandstetter 1875. XVI, 611 S. Schöner blindgeprägter Einband mit viel Gold, Goldschnitt. Ecken berieben, Einband im oberen Bereich aufgeplatzt. kunstbuecher_383_16_38.verification_notes: missing_person: The author is listed only as 'OESER' without a given name. The work 'Briefe an eine Jungfrau über die Hauptgegenstände der Ästhetik' is associated with Adam Friedrich Oeser (1717–1799) as the original author, but given the 17th edition in 1875 and the heavy editorial involvement of A. W. Grube, the authorship attribution may be nominal or traditional. Human verification of the full author name is recommended. api_concerned: The entry lists only the surname 'OSESER' — the full given name cannot be confirmed with certainty from the entry alone. kunstbuecher_929_38_38.original_entry:
ZEPPEL – SPERL, Robert
Als biliophile Monographie und schönes Bilderbuch gestaltet und herausgegeben von Otto Breicha. Mit fünfundzwanzig farbigen Linolschnitten und zweiundzwanzig mitgedruckten Originallithographien des Künstlers sowie mit Texten von Friederike Mayröcker, Otto Breicha und Robert Zeppel-Sperl Wien Tusch-Druck 1978. Folio. Unpaginiert. Hardcover- Vorzugsausgabe mit dreißig römisch nummerierten Ex. von den Autoren signiert. Dieses Ex. hat die Nr. IX. Schöner Einband in schöner Kassette.
kunstbuecher_929_38_38.verification_notes: The heading 'ZEPPEL – SPERL, Robert' uses an em-dash where a hyphen is expected; the artist's name is Robert Zeppel-Sperl (confirmed by the body text of the entry itself). The heading has been interpreted accordingly. The word 'biliophile' in the subtitle is a typo for 'bibliophile' but left as-is in original_entry; it is noted here. The title field reflects the heading as given; the actual monograph title appears to be the artist's name. Texts by Friederike Mayröcker, Otto Breicha, and Robert Zeppel-Sperl are noted; Zeppel-Sperl is listed as author, Breicha as editor, and Mayröcker as contributor.

The work is a scholarly anthology of Gnostic source texts introduced, translated, and annotated in German; the source languages are various (Greek, Coptic, Syriac, etc.) and cannot be reduced to a single original_language. Translator field left null as each volume has different translators/editors listed in the volume notes. Werner Foerster is identified as the general editor (Herausgeber) of the series based on his role in volumes 1 and 3.

okkultismus_83_4_8.verification_notes: The topic context is 'OKKULTISMUS, GEHEIMWISSENSCHAFTEN', but 'Die Waldenser' is a scholarly history of the Waldensian heretical movement — a legitimate religious history work, not an occult or esoteric text. This may indicate a misfiling or cataloging error. Additionally, the author's name 'Amedeo Molnar' corresponds to a known Czech church historian (Amedeo Molnár, 1923–1990) who wrote on the Waldensians; the entry omits the diacritic on 'Molnár'. However, since 'Molnar' is also a valid spelling variant, no correction has been applied.

Christiani Rosencreutz Anno 1459. Nach der zu Straßburg bei Lazari Zetzners seel. Erben im Jahre 1616 erschienenen Ausgabe originalgetreu neugedruckt. Eingeleitet und herausgegeben von Ferdinand Maack. Mit dem Porträt Andreae`s und Abbildungen. Berlin Barsdorf Verlag 1913. LIV, 115, 84 S. Inhalt: Chymische Hochzeit Christiani Rosencreutz Anno 1459. okkultismus_20_1_8.verification_notes: The page count is given as 'LIV, 115, 84 S.' (Roman numerals LIV = 54 pages of introduction, plus 115 and 84 pages of content), which cannot be reduced to a single integer pages field without loss of information; left as null. The work 'Chymische Hochzeit Christiani Rosencreutz' is traditionally attributed to Johann Valentin Andreae (1586–1654); the entry lists no author, only an editor (Ferdinand Maack). This is consistent with the reprint nature of the edition, but the original authorship of Andreae is not stated in the entry and has not been inserted. The backtick used in 'Andreae`s' is a cataloging accent used as apostrophe, as noted in the bibliographic context.

maerchen_0_1_3.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: 'Band 1 und zwei' corrected to 'Band 1 und 2' (obvious numeral/word inconsistency). multiple_editions: The entry mentions an additional volume 'Erzählungen aus dem Westsudan' beyond the three main volumes, and states 'Zusammen 6 Bände', suggesting extra copies or additional volumes are present; the main 3-volume set is treated as the primary record. copies set to 6 as stated ('Zusammen 6 Bände'). api_concerned: The additional volume 'Erzählungen aus dem Westsudan' is mentioned but its bibliographic details (year, pages, format) are absent; it is unclear whether it belongs to the same Atlantis series or is a separate work. The total of 6 volumes is ambiguous — it may include duplicate copies of Bd. 1 and 2 in a different binding plus the extra volume. problematic_multi_volume: The statement 'Band 1 und zwei in einer anderen Bindung doppelt' and the additional 'Erzählungen aus dem Westsudan' volume make the overall volume structure unclear; only the three clearly described volumes are included in the volumes array.

DEUTSCHE SAGEN

maerchen_37_2_3.verification_notes: The entry describes two separate works by the Brüder Grimm, both edited by Heinz Rölleke, both Sonderausgaben published by Deutscher Klassiker Verlag Frankfurt 1999. The first (main record) is 'KINDER- UND HAUSMÄRCHEN' (1302 S., OPbd., verlagsfrisch). The second is 'DEUTSCHE SAGEN' (1073 S., OPbd., Kassette, verlagsfrisch), ediert und kommentiert von Heinz Rölleke. The entry closes with 'Beide Bände', indicating both volumes are owned together. The second work (DEUTSCHE SAGEN) is noted here only; it should ideally receive its own catalogue record. The 'Kassette' notation for the second work likely refers to a slipcase (Kassette = Schuber/Kassette).

insel_235_10_27.original_entry:
FELIX TIMMERMANS IB 205
