insel_235_10_27.verification_notes: The entry lists two authors: Felix Timmermans and A. Tiry (introduced by 'Und'). The authorship split and the identity of 'A. Tiry' could not be fully verified. The IB number 205 is noted as a series identifier for the Insel-Bücherei.

insel_236_10_27.original_entry:
FELIX TIMMERMANS IB 308
Die sehr schönen Stunden von Jungfer Symforosa, dem Beginchen. Mit Zeichnungen des Dichters. Leipzig Insel [1941] 47 S. Neuwertig.2.Ex. 76.-100.Tsd.

insel_237_10_27.original_entry:
FELIX TIMMERMANS IB 362
Das Triptychon. Von den Heiligen Drei Königen. Übertragen von Anton Kippenberg. Initialen und Bilder von Felix Timmermans. Frankfurt 1989. 47 S. Schöner Band3 Ex
insel_237_10_27.verification_notes: Anton Kippenberg (1874–1950) was the longtime director of Insel Verlag and is known as a translator of Timmermans. However, he died in 1950, making a 1989 publication with him as translator plausible as a reprint. The original language of Timmermans (Flemish/Dutch) is not stated in the entry but is implied. Publisher is not explicitly stated; 'Insel' is inferred from the series context (Insel-Bücherei). Human verification recommended.

insel_238_10_27.original_entry:
FELIX TIMMERMANS IB 420
Sankt Nikolaus in Not und andere Erzählungen. Mit Zeichnungen des Dichters. Frankfurt Insel 1966. 62 S. Verlagsfrisch2. Ex 1984.3. Ex
insel_238_10_27.verification_notes: The entry appears to list multiple copies with different acquisition years: '2. Ex 1984' and '3. Ex' (year unclear). The copies field is set to 3. The '1984' likely refers to the acquisition year of the 2nd copy, not a publication year. multiple_editions flag set due to conflicting copy/edition references that cannot be cleanly separated.

insel_239_10_27.original_entry:
FERDINAND VON SAAR IB 621
Die Steinklopfer. Erzählung. Leipzig Insel 1.-10.Tsd. 1962. 51 S.

insel_240_10_27.original_entry:

insel_240_10_27.verification_notes: The entry reads 'Verlagsfrisch3. Ex.' which appears to be a run-together of 'Verlagsfrisch' (condition) and '3. Ex.' (3 copies). Separated accordingly. Author name in entry header is given as 'FERRUCCIO BUSONI' (given name first); normalized to standard surname-first format 'BUSONI, Ferruccio'.

insel_241_10_27.original_entry:
FJODOR M.DOSTOJEWSKI IB 149

insel_241_10_27.verification_notes: Two editions described: (1) Frankfurt, Insel, 1966, 43 S., Verlagsfrisch; (2) a second copy from 1961, 198.–207. Tausend. The first-described edition (1966) is used as the main record. The second copy (1961, 198.–207. Tsd.) is noted here only.

insel_242_10_27.original_entry:
FJODOR M.DOSTOJEWSKI IB 149
Der Großinquisitor. Übertragen von Rudolf Kassner. Wiesbaden Insel 1952. 158.-167.Tsd. 46 S. Sehr gut erhalten.2.Ex. 1961. 198.-207.Tsd. Verlagsfrisch
insel_242_10_27.verification_notes: Two copies described: 1st copy — Wiesbaden, Insel, 1952, 158.-167. Tsd., 46 S., sehr gut erhalten. 2nd copy — 1961, 198.-207. Tsd., Verlagsfrisch. The first-described edition is used as the main record. The second copy (1961, 198.-207. Tsd., Verlagsfrisch) is noted here only.

insel_243_10_27.original_entry:
FJODOR M: DOSTOJEWSKI IB 116
Die Sanfte. Übertragung Alexander Eliasberg. Leipzig Insel 1922. 70 S. Rücken schadhaft, berieben.2. Ex. EA gut.
insel_243_10_27.verification_notes: Author name reordered to standard surname-first format: 'DOSTOJEWSKI, Fjodor M.' The colon after 'M' in the original ('FJODOR M: DOSTOJEWSKI') appears to be a punctuation error for the period in 'M.' (middle initial). The entry describes two copies: the first has a damaged, rubbed spine; the second is a first edition in good condition. 'Übertragung' indicates translation; original language inferred as Russian from the author's identity.

insel_244_10_27.original_entry:
FRANCISCO GOYA IB 570
Pinselzeichnungen. 32 Bildtafeln. Geleitwort von Robert Th. Stoll. Wiesbaden Insel 1953. (11 S. Text). EA. Sehr schöner Band.2. Ex.

insel_245_10_27.original_entry:
FRANCOIS VILLON IB 883
Baladn. In Wiener Mundart. Übertragung: Hans Carl Artmann. Mit Erläuterungen. 5. – 7. Tausend. Frankfurt Insel 1969. 89 S.
insel_245_10_27.verification_notes: The title 'Baladn.' appears to be a dialectal/abbreviated form, likely 'Balladen' in Wiener Mundart spelling. The author's name is given without diacritics as 'FRANCOIS VILLON'; the correct French form is 'François Villon'. Not corrected as the entry may intentionally omit the cedilla. The work is a translation/adaptation into Viennese dialect by H.C. Artmann, so original_language is French. 'Mit Erläuterungen' placed in illustrations field as it describes supplementary content rather than illustrations per se — may warrant review.

TIBOR DERY IB- 677
Der Riese. Erzählung. Übertragen von Ivan Nagel. Frankfurt 1963. 75 S.2. Ex.
insel_629_26_27.verification_notes: The author's name is listed as 'TIBOR DERY' but the correct Hungarian name is 'Tibor Déry' (with accent on the é). No correction applied as this may be an intentional simplified transcription. The original language of the work is not stated; Déry was a Hungarian author, so the original language is likely Hungarian, but this is not confirmed in the entry and has not been inserted. Publisher is not stated, only place of publication (Frankfurt); for Insel-Bücherei this would typically be Insel Verlag, but not confirmed in the entry.

insel_630_26_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry is an anthology or selection of writings by Giacomo Casanova, but no editor or compiler is named. The subject/author of the source texts is Casanova, but no person responsible for the selection or edition is listed.

insel_631_26_27.original_entry:

insel_631_26_27.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry lists two copies — '2.Ex.' (1940 edition) and '3.Ex.' with year 1939, indicating a second copy of an earlier edition (1939). The first-described edition (Leipzig, Insel, 1940, 61 S.) is used as the main record. The third copy (1939 edition) is noted here only.

insel_632_26_27.original_entry:

TSCHINGIS AITMATOW IB 773
Dshmila. A.d.Russ. von Hartmut Herboth. Leipzig Insel 1960. 75 S. OPbd.
insel_633_26_27.verification_notes: Aitmatow was a Kyrgyz author who wrote in both Kyrgyz and Russian. 'Dshamilja' (Джамиля) was originally written in Kyrgyz and translated into Russian before being translated into other languages. The original language listed as Russian may be correct for the source text used for this German translation, but the actual original language is Kyrgyz. Flagged for human verification.

insel_640_26_27.verification_notes: The original language of Voltaire's Mémoires is French; however, this is not explicitly stated in the entry, so original_language is left null rather than assumed. Botond is listed as both editor and translator ('Herausgegeben und übersetzt von'). The IB number 892 identifies this as Insel-Bücherei volume 892.

insel_641_26_27.original_entry: Vom Glück der Freundschaft. Mit Illustrationen von Alexandra Klobouk und Eva Goncalves. Berlin Insel 2014. 95 S. OPbd. Kleinformat
insel_641_26_27.verification_notes: missing_person: No author or editor is named for this anthology/collection. The illustrators are listed but no compiler or editor is identified.

insel_644_26_27.verification_notes: The original publication details 'Regenspurg zu Statt am Hof Anno 1702' refer to the historical first edition by Andreas Glorez; the 1971 Frankfurt entry is a modern reprint/edition in the Insel-Bücherei series (IB 948). The author name 'Andreas Glorez von Mährn' is extracted from the subtitle description; the exact form of the author's name as used in the 1971 edition may differ. No publisher is explicitly stated for the 1971 Frankfurt edition.

insel_645_26_27.original_entry:

insel_645_26_27.verification_notes: The edition notation '21.25.Tsd.' was interpreted as '21.-25. Tausend' (a print run range), which is the standard Insel-Bücherei cataloging format. The publisher was inferred as Insel-Verlag based on the series context (Insel-Bücherei, IB 123). The series number IB 123 is noted in the original entry.

insel_646_26_27.original_entry:

insel_647_26_27.verification_notes: Two copies described: first copy is the 1954 Wiesbaden Insel edition (94 S.), used as the main record. Second copy is a 1951 edition (92.-101. Tausend, 92 S.), mentioned after 'Schönes Ex.' — this is a secondary edition noted alongside the main entry.

insel_648_26_27.original_entry:

insel_125_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BAUMBUCH IB 316
Farbige Bilder von Willi Harwerth. Geleitwort von Friedrich Schnack. 'Die deutschen Waldbäume'. Leipzig Insel 1934. 67 S. EA.2. Ex. Frankfurt 1985.
insel_125_6_27.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the main edition (Leipzig, Insel, 1934, Erstausgabe, 2 copies) and mentions a secondary edition published in Frankfurt 1985. The subtitle 'Die deutschen Waldbäume' appears to be a descriptive subtitle or series note in single quotes. The Geleitwort (foreword) by Friedrich Schnack is noted as a contributor role.

insel_126_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BLUMENBUCH IB 281
Zeichnungen von Rudolf Koch in Holz geschnitten von Fritz Kredel. Mit 58 mehrfarbigen Zeichnungen Leipzig Insel 1933. 3 S. Text. EA. Etwas berieben.2. Ex. EA3.Ex. 1934 mit Namenszug auf Vorsatz
insel_126_6_27.verification_notes: Multiple copies and editions present. Main record: 1. Ex., Erstausgabe 1933, etwas berieben. 2. Ex.: Erstausgabe 1933, condition not separately noted. 3. Ex.: 1934 edition, mit Namenszug auf Vorsatz. Total copies: 3. The entry 'EA3.Ex. 1934' is parsed as a third copy of a 1934 edition (likely a later printing). Pages field: '3 S. Text' refers to the text pages only, not total pages — left null to avoid misrepresentation.

insel_127_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BUCH DER EDELSTEINE IB-54
Farbige Bilder von Hans Lang. Geleitwort: Friedrich Schnack Leipzig Insel 1938. 39 S. Mit einer Widmung auf Vortitel. Neuwertig. Selten!
insel_127_6_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry lists 'Farbige Bilder von Hans Lang' (contributor/illustrator) and a Geleitwort by Friedrich Schnack, but no author is named for the main text. Hans Lang is listed as contributor (illustrator); Friedrich Schnack wrote the foreword but is not listed as author.

insel_128_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BUCH DER GREIFE IB 515
Einheimische Raubvögel. Nachwort von Otto Fehringer. Mit 24 farbigen Tafeln. Frankfurt Insel 1985. 44 S. Neuwertig.
insel_128_6_27.verification_notes: missing_person: No author is listed for this work. The entry only mentions Otto Fehringer as the author of the Nachwort (afterword), not as the main author.

insel_129_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BUCH DER MEERESWUNDER IB 158
Muscheln und Schnecken. Kolorierte Stiche auf 24 Tafeln. Geleitwort Friedrich Schack. Leipzig 1937. 44 S. Umschlag durch Feuchtigkeit wellig, berieben. Innen jedoch sehr schön.
insel_129_6_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry has no named author. Friedrich Schack is listed as the author of the Geleitwort (foreword/preface) only, not as the main author or editor of the work. The actual author or editor of the volume is not stated.

insel_130_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BUCH DER NACHTFALTER IB 226
Kolorierte Stiche von Jakob Hübner. Geleitwort von Friedrich Schnack. Leipzig Insel 1936. 1.-30.Tsd. Mit 24 Tafeln. 46 S. Sehr gut erhalten.

DAS KLEINE BUCH DER TROPENWUNDER IB 351

insel_131_6_27.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Three copies are mentioned. The first (main record) is the 1935 Erstausgabe with condition notes. The second copy is also 1935 (EA). The third copy is from 1999, likely a later reprint or new edition. The main record reflects the first-described 1935 edition.

insel_132_6_27.original_entry:
DAS KLEINE BUCH DER VÖGEL UND NESTER IB 100

insel_132_6_27.verification_notes: Multiple copies/editions present: 1. Ex. (1955, Leipzig und Wiesbaden, Insel, 46 S.) — main record; 2. Ex. (1935, sehr gut erhalten); 3. Ex. (EA, Erstausgabe); 4. Ex. (price noted as 20.-). The first-described copy (1955) is used as the main record. The 1935 copy may be an earlier edition. 'IB 100' refers to Insel-Bücherei number 100.

insel_133_6_27.original_entry:

Die Tagfalter. Kolorierte Stiche von Jakob Hübner. Geleitwort Friedrich Schnack. Leipzig Insel 1934. 46 S. Foliert.

DAS KOPFKISSENBUCH DER DAME SEI SHONAGON
Frankfurt Insel 1975. Mit einem Nachwort von Helmut Bode und einer Nachbemerkung zu den Illustrationen von I. Schaarschmidt-Richter. 115 S. EA.
insel_137_6_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry is a translation from Japanese (Makura no Sōshi by Sei Shōnagon) but no translator is named. The translator field is left null.

insel_138_6_27.original_entry:
DAS LEBEN BUDDHAS IB 870
Ein chinesisches Holzschnittfragment. Herausgegeben von Martin Grimm Frankfurt Insel 1968. 57 S. Sehr schöner Band2.Ex.

insel_139_6_27.original_entry:
DAS LEBEN KAISER HEINRICH DES VIERTEN IB 333
Übertragung, Nachwort und Anmerkungen: Johannes Bühler Leipzig 1921. 63 S. Rücken beschädigt.
insel_139_6_27.verification_notes: The work is a medieval Latin vita (biography of Emperor Henry IV). Bühler provided the German translation ('Übertragung') as well as the afterword and annotations, so he functions as both translator and contributor. The original language is likely Latin but is not stated explicitly in the entry. Publisher inferred as Insel-Verlag based on the Insel-Bücherei series context (IB 333).

insel_140_6_27.original_entry:
DAS PERLENHEMD IB 216
Eine chinesische Liebesgeschichte. Wiesbaden Insel 1953. 41.-45.Tsd. 52 S. Titelschild fehlt. Sonst Neuwertig.

insel_141_6_27.original_entry:
DAS PUPPENSPIEL VOM DOKTOR FAUST IB 125
Leipzig Insel 1939 34.-36.Tsd. 67 S. Neuwertig.

insel_142_6_27.original_entry:
DAS ROLLWAGENBÜCHLEIN IB 132
…An Tag bracht und zusammen gelesen durch Jörg Wickram…Mit Titelholzschnitt. Auswahl und Nachbemerkung: Christian Heinrich Kleukens. 21.-25.Tsd. (1919). 95 S. Schöner Band mit Gebrauchsspuren am Rücken.2. Ex. EA
insel_142_6_27.verification_notes: The entry lists both '21.-25. Tausend' and 'EA' (Erstausgabe). These designations appear contradictory: a 21st–25th thousand printing would not normally be described as a first edition. This may reflect Insel-Bücherei cataloging convention where 'EA' refers to the first edition of this particular series number rather than the first printing. Human verification recommended.

insel_143_6_27.original_entry:
DAS SILHOUETTEN-KABINETT IB 1022
Scherenschnitte und Schattenbilder aus zwei Jahrhunderten. Herausgegeben von Wilfried F. Schoeller. Frankfurt Insel 1985. 155 S. Mit Widmung. Sehr gut erhalten.

insel_144_6_27.original_entry:
DAS STÄNDEBUCH IB 133
114 Holzschnitte von Jost Ammann mit Reimen von Hans Sachs. Leipzig Insel 1960. 135 S. Neuwertig2. Ex. 1934. EA3. Ex. 1975. Bildeinband4. Ex. 1960. Mit Umschlag 2 Ex.
insel_144_6_27.verification_notes: Multiple editions and copies described: 1st copy (main record) 1960 edition, neuwertig; 2nd copy 1934 edition, EA (Erstausgabe); 3rd copy 1975 edition, Bildeinband; 4th copy 1960 edition, mit Umschlag. The entry also mentions '2 Ex.' at the end which may refer to the last copy count or an additional copy — total appears to be 4 distinct copies across multiple editions. Main record reflects the first-described 1960 edition. The series number IB 133 refers to Insel-Bücherei Nr. 133.

insel_145_6_27.original_entry:
DAS TAGEBUCH' GOETHES IB 1000
Und Rilkes 'Sieben Gedichte' erläutert von Siegfried Unseld. Frankfurt Insel 1978. 214. Schöner Band.2.Ex. EA. 1978. Transparentfolie Verlagsfrisch3.Ex.
insel_145_6_27.verification_notes: The entry uses an accent/apostrophe in 'TAGEBUCH'' which appears to be a punctuation inconsistency; the full title combines Goethe's 'Das Tagebuch' and Rilke's 'Sieben Gedichte', both erläutert (explained/introduced) by Siegfried Unseld — Unseld is treated as editor/commentator rather than author. The IB number 1000 confirms this is Insel-Bücherei Nr. 1000. The entry lists 2 Ex. and then 3 Ex. with 'Transparentfolie Verlagsfrisch'; interpreted as a total of 3 copies (2 regular, 1 in original publisher's shrink wrap), with copies set to 3. corrected_by_api set true for normalizing the apostrophe/accent in the title to standard form.

insel_448_18_27.verification_notes: The author is listed as 'LAU-DSE', which is a German transliteration of Laozi (Lao-Tse / Lao Tzu), the ancient Chinese philosopher traditionally credited with the Tao Te Ching (here rendered as 'DAU-DÖ-GING'). The IB number 253 is noted as a series identifier for the Insel-Bücherei. Flagged api_concerned because the authorship of the Tao Te Ching is historically debated and the transliteration form 'LAU-DSE' is unusual but plausible as a period-specific German rendering.

insel_257_11_27.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The entry header reads 'FREIDRICH VON STENDHAL' — 'FREIDRICH' is a clear typo for 'FRIEDRICH'. However, Stendhal's actual given name was Marie-Henri Beyle; 'Friedrich von Stendhal' is not a known form of his name. The 'Friedrich' prefix may be a cataloger error or confusion. The author field has been set to 'STENDHAL' without a given name to avoid propagating an incorrect name. api_concerned: The use of 'Friedrich von Stendhal' is unusual and potentially erroneous — Stendhal (Marie-Henri Beyle) was French and not known by the name Friedrich. Human verification is recommended to confirm the author identity and the IB number 377.

Hrabal is listed as author of the introduction and likely the editor/compiler of the anthology. The exact editorial role of Hrabal and the nature of the contributors' involvement may warrant verification. However, this may be a note reproduced from the book itself and not a cataloger error, so it has been left as stated. No translator is named in the entry; missing_person set true. is_translation set true as this is a German edition of an English work by Dickens. corrected_by_api set true: the subtitle text references 1841 which appears to be an error (the work was published 1844), but since this may be the book's own subtitle text, it is left uncorrected and flagged instead — api_concerned not set as the concern is noted here.

insel_511_21_27.verification_notes: This entry covers 15 individual volumes (Nos. 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, 20, 22, 25, 26) of the series 'Österreichische Bibliothek im Insel Verlag', each with distinct editors, dates, page counts, and conditions. multiple_editions is set true because volume 6 notes '2 Ex.' (two copies) and volumes 5 and 9 note 'EA' (Erstausgabe), suggesting mixed edition/copy information across entries. problematic_multi_volume is set true because volumes 1, 3, 8, 11, 14, 15, 18, 19, 21, 23, 24 are absent from the entry — it is unclear whether these are not owned or simply not catalogued here. Volume 20 (BRIEFE AUS WIEN) has no page count given. Volume 26 (SCHUBERT IM FREUNDESKREIS) lists no publisher or place explicitly for this entry, only the year 1917; presumed Leipzig Insel by series context. api_concerned: The selector 'Irma Hift' for volume 17 (PRINZ EUGEN) is an unusual name — possible typo for 'Irma Hift' or another name; flagged for verification. Also, volume 8 (BEETHOVEN, No. 9) is dated 1913, which predates the series' general 1915 start; this may be correct as an early volume but is flagged.

insel_375_16_27.original_entry:
HUSSMANN, Heinrich
Deutsche Wappenkunst. Leipzig Insel (1942) 66 S. Mit vielen farbigen Abbildungen. Größe und Ausstattung wie Insel-Bücherei. Signiert von Hußmann und hs. Korrekturen
insel_375_16_27.verification_notes: The entry header spells the author's surname as 'HUSSMANN' while the condition note uses 'Hußmann'. The correct spelling of the author Heinrich Hußmann uses the ß; the header form 'HUSSMANN' is likely a transcription variant (ß rendered as SS in uppercase contexts). Both forms are retained as found; no correction applied to the name field as both are plausible representations. The note 'Größe und Ausstattung wie Insel-Bücherei' indicates this volume matches the Insel-Bücherei format but may not be an official Insel-Bücherei number; no format code was given so format fields are left null.

insel_376_16_27.original_entry:

insel_376_16_27.verification_notes: The entry title 'HUTTEN DER DEUTSCHE' refers to Ulrich von Hutten, the German humanist. No author name is explicitly listed in the entry, which is consistent with an anthology/selection edited by Otto Clemen. The IB number 526 confirms this is Insel-Bücherei volume 526. The subtitle lists three separate works or sections: 'Gedichte', 'Aus der Türkenrede', and 'Arminius' — these appear to be selections from Hutten's writings rather than separate works. Flagged api_concerned because the entry lacks an explicit author attribution for Hutten himself, though world knowledge confirms this is a selection of Hutten's own writings edited by Clemen.

insel_396_16_27.original_entry:
JEAN-ETIENNE LIOTARD IB 613

insel_396_16_27.verification_notes: Jean-Étienne Liotard (1702–1789) was a Swiss-French painter known for portraits of the Habsburg imperial family, including children of Empress Maria Theresa. However, he is primarily known as a painter, not an author. The entry lists him as the author of this Insel-Bücherei volume, which may instead be a picture book or art publication featuring his works, with Liotard listed as the artist rather than a textual author. The authorship attribution may need verification.

Auflage, neuwertig — noted as secondary edition. The '2. Ex.' appears to refer to the second copy/edition entry rather than two copies of the first edition.

insel_203_9_27.original_entry:
EDGAR ALLAN POE IB 129
Phantastische Erzählungen. Mit Zeichnungen von Fritz FISCHER. Übertragen von Grete Rambach. Leipzig Insel 46. bis 55 Tsd. 90 S. Neue Ausgabe, illustriert. Leichte Gebrauchsspuren am Einband, sonst schönes Ex. EA2.u.4. Ex. 1955. Ohne Ill. 66.-75.Tsd. ohne Illustr.
insel_203_9_27.verification_notes: The entry describes multiple editions/printings: (1) the main entry is the illustrated 'Neue Ausgabe' at 46.–55. Tausend with 90 pages and illustrations by Fritz Fischer; (2) a secondary mention references 'EA' copies (2nd and 4th exemplar) from 1955 without illustrations at 66.–75. Tausend. The copy count of 4 is inferred from '2.u.4. Ex.' (2nd and 4th copy). The publication year is not clearly stated for the main entry; 1955 appears to belong to the secondary edition. The 'Übertragen von' phrasing confirms this is a translation from English.

insel_204_9_27.original_entry:
EDUARD MÖRIKE IB 230

insel_204_9_27.verification_notes: The entry describes at least two copies (2.Ex. and 3.Ex.) of the same 1939 edition (56.-65. Tausend). The first copy (2.Ex.) appears to be a standard copy; the third copy (3.Ex.) is noted as identical to the second but with a signature by Hans Spitzy, described as a famous Austrian physician. The copy count is set to 3 based on '3.Ex.' being the last mentioned. The multiple_editions flag is set because the copy/edition references are somewhat ambiguous and interleaved.

insel_212_9_27.original_entry:
EINE WELT SCHRIEBT AN GOETHE IB 638
Herausgegeben von Rudolf K. Goldschmit-Jentner. Wiesbaden Insel 1956. 76 S.
insel_212_9_27.verification_notes: Title corrected: 'SCHRIEBT' → 'schreibt' (obvious typo). Editor name 'Goldschmit-Jentner' may be a misspelling of 'Goldschmit-Jentner' — the known editor of this Insel-Bücherei volume is 'Rudolf K. Goldschmit-Jentner'; however, some sources spell it 'Goldschmidt-Jentner' (with a 'd'). The spelling as given has been retained without correction since it is ambiguous; api_concerned flagged for human verification of the editor's name spelling.

insel_213_9_27.original_entry:
EINE WUNDERBARLICH UND KURZWEILIGE HISTORIE IB 219
Wie Schiltberger… Mit 14 Holzschnitten. Leipzig 1917. 79 S. Durch Feuchtigkeit stark beschädigt.
insel_213_9_27.verification_notes: The subtitle 'Wie Schiltberger…' is truncated with an ellipsis, suggesting the full subtitle is not captured. This likely refers to the travel account of Johann Schiltberger (c. 1380–c. 1440), a Bavarian soldier who was captured by the Ottomans and wrote about his experiences. The publisher is inferred as Insel-Verlag based on the series context (Insel-Bücherei Nr. 219). No author or editor is listed; the work appears to be an anonymous or editorially compiled historical text. Human verification of the full subtitle and editor information is recommended.

insel_214_9_27.original_entry:
EKKEHART I. VON SANKT GALLEN IB 698
Waltharius. Lateinisch und deutsch. Leipzig Insel 1988. 113 S. Verlagsfrisch
insel_214_9_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry lists 'Lateinisch und deutsch' but no translator is named for the German portion. api_concerned: The authorship of 'Waltharius' is disputed — it is traditionally attributed to Ekkehart I. of St. Gallen, but some scholars attribute it to a monk of Fulda (Geraldus). The attribution in the entry follows one scholarly tradition but is not universally accepted.

insel_215_9_27.original_entry:

insel_217_9_27.verification_notes: The entry lists Emile Zola as the author and 'Gustave Flaubert' as the title, with Leo Berg as the translator ('Übertragung'). Zola did write an essay on Flaubert, so this is plausible as a translated essay/study. However, the original language is not stated; given Zola was French, the original language is presumably French, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. The 'Übertragung' (translation/adaptation) designation is noted. IB 205 refers to the Insel-Bücherei number.

Tausend cannot simultaneously be the Erstausgabe. The first-described edition (11.-15. Tsd.) is used as the main record. 'EA' may be a cataloging error or refer to a separate copy. multiple_editions flag set accordingly.

Tsd., described as neuwertig. The year 1940 and the 'neuwertig' condition note appear to belong to the second copy/edition. The first edition's year is not stated. Tsd., verlagsfrisch. 4. Ex.: 86.-91. Tsd., verlagsfrisch. 5. Ex. and 6. Ex.: minimal information, 6. Ex. dated 1954. The first-described edition (1941) is used as the main record. The 1933 edition (1.-10. Tsd.) is noted here only. The IB number 261 refers to the Insel-Bücherei series number.

He is listed as both editor and translator. The original language of Meister Eckhart's writings is Middle High German (Mittelhochdeutsch), but this is not explicitly stated in the entry; original_language left null as it is not given. The IB number 280 refers to the Insel-Bücherei series number.

insel_491_20_27.verification_notes: The header line reads 'MICHAEL SALTYKOW-SCHTSCHEDRIN IB 382', suggesting this entry was filed under Saltykow-Schtschedrin, yet the actual bibliographic entry describes a work by Valentin Katajew. It is possible that IB 382 contains works by both authors (a combined volume), or that the header is a cataloging error. The author has been set to Katajew as stated in the entry text, but human verification is needed to clarify the relationship to Saltykow-Schtschedrin.

insel_492_20_27.original_entry:

The work is a translation from Russian, but no translator is named in the entry (missing_person flag set). The subtitle 'Die Wurzel des Lebens' was enclosed in single quotes (used as apostrophes/quotation marks) in the original; rendered without quotes in the subtitle field.

insel_493_20_27.original_entry:

insel_493_20_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry indicates a translation (Russian author Sholokhov) but no translator is named.

insel_496_20_27.original_entry:
MICHELANGELO IB 616
Sibyllen und Propheten. 24 farbige Bilder nach den Fresken in der Sixtinischen Kapelle. Mit einem Geleitwort von Bettina Seip. Leipzig Insel 1941. 54 S. Etwas berieben.2.-5.Ex. alle EA 1939 und mit Widmungen. A`
insel_496_20_27.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The main record describes a 1941 Leipzig Insel edition (54 S.), while copies 2–5 are noted as EA 1939 with dedications — suggesting the Erstausgabe year is 1939, not 1941. The 1941 copy is described first and treated as the main record per convention, but the edition field 'Erstausgabe' may apply to the 1939 copies rather than the 1941 copy. api_concerned: There is a contradiction between the publication year 1941 and the note that copies 2–5 are 'EA 1939'. It is unclear whether the 1941 copy is a later printing or if the year is an error. Human verification recommended. The trailing 'A`' at the end of the entry is unexplained and may be a cataloging note or truncation artifact.

The entry does not indicate a translator or original language, but given the author's nationality and the German title, this is almost certainly a translation from Croatian. The is_translation field has been left null rather than guessing. Human verification recommended to confirm translation status and translator.

insel_326_14_27.verification_notes: missing_person: The work is a translation from Danish (original: 'Billedbog uden Billeder'), but no translator is named in the entry.

insel_327_14_27.original_entry:
HANS HOLBEIN IB 221
Bilder des Todes. Mit 41 Holzschnitten. Geleitwort von Emil Waldmann. Leipzig 1934. 3 gez. S. Mit einigen Stockflecken. Sonst schöner Band
insel_327_14_27.verification_notes: Hans Holbein the Younger (c. 1497–1543) is the artist associated with the 'Dance of Death' woodcuts ('Bilder des Todes'). The entry lists him as the author, which is appropriate for an artist. The '3 gez. S.' (3 gezählte Seiten) likely refers to the introductory pages, but no total page count is given. No publisher is listed. The series number IB 221 confirms this is Insel-Bücherei no. 221.

Gedichte' (a collection of poems dedicated 'to you'). The publisher is not stated; Frankfurt 1987 in the Insel-Bücherei context likely refers to Insel Verlag, but this cannot be confirmed without verification. IB 1072 is the Insel-Bücherei number.

The entry may be missing diacritics and uses a hyphenated form of the middle name. Additionally, 'Crainquebille' is a French work by Anatole France, so is_translation may apply, but no translator is mentioned and the entry does not explicitly indicate a translation, so it has been left as false pending verification.

insel_34_2_27.verification_notes: No author is listed for this anthology of anecdotes, which is consistent with an anonymous or collective compilation. The entry notes 'EA' (Erstausgabe) alongside '81.-100. Tausend', which is contradictory — a first edition would not typically reach the 81st–100th thousand printing. This may indicate that 'EA' refers to the first edition of this particular Insel-Bücherei format/series number rather than the absolute first edition of the text. No publication year is given. Reinhold Schneider is listed as the author of the introduction ('Eingeleitet von') and has been placed in contributors accordingly.

insel_534_22_27.verification_notes: The entry header 'PETER VISCHER IB 330' appears to be the Insel-Bücherei catalog number (330) appended to the subject name rather than an author name. Peter Vischer (the Elder, c. 1455–1529) was the sculptor of the Sebaldus tomb in Nuremberg — he is the subject, not the author of this publication. The actual author of this Insel-Bücherei volume is likely a modern art historian. The author field has been populated with the header text as written, but human verification is needed to identify the true author. Additionally, 'Signiert von Hußmann2. Ex.' appears to be a run-together of 'Signiert von Hußmann' and '2. Ex.' (2 copies); parsed accordingly.

ethnologie_96_4_13.verification_notes: The author's given name is listed as 'Levi', but Claude Lévi-Strauss's first name was 'Claude', not 'Levi'. This may be a cataloging error (confusing the surname with the given name), but since the entry clearly states 'Levi' and no correction is unambiguous without human review, the name has been left as written. Additionally, the standard form of the name should include diacritics: 'Lévi-Strauss'. The work 'Das wilde Denken' (La Pensée sauvage, 1962) is indeed by Claude Lévi-Strauss and is a translation from French; no translator is named in the entry.

autographen_16_1_2.verification_notes: This entry describes an autograph item: a 13-line manuscript letter dated 20 March 1945 addressed to Charlotte Eisler, with signature, accompanied by a printed concert program from 1945 featuring works by Hans Gal with Charlotte Eisler at the piano. Hans Gál (1890–1987) was an Austrian-British composer and musicologist. The entry contains two distinct physical items (letter + concert program); both are noted. The concert program details have been placed in the condition field as supplementary physical description. The name 'Hans Gal' in the concert program line appears to lack the umlaut (should be 'Gál'), but this may reflect the original printed program's spelling, so no correction has been applied.

autographen_24_1_2.verification_notes: The entry describes an autograph item (handwritten envelope with address and sender) by Ernst Meister, listed as an actor (1926–1986). There is a well-known German lyric poet named Ernst Meister (1911–1979); however, the dates 1926–1986 and the designation 'Schauspieler' (actor) suggest this is a different person. No widely documented actor named Ernst Meister with those dates is readily identifiable. Flagged for human verification of identity.

Additionally, 'OHLn.' is an unusual format notation; it may stand for 'Originalhalbleinen' (with 'H' for 'Halb'), but this abbreviation is not standard — more common would be 'HLn.' Expanded as 'Originalhalbleinen' tentatively.

NABIAS, S. de
Traitement par le Radium de quelques Neoplasmes. Centre de L´Hopital Tenon. Paris Chahine 1927. Mit 62 Abb. a. XLIII Taf. 248 S. OPbd. Mit einer Widmung an Leopold Schönbauer NS-Mitglied (Genaueres siehe Wikipedia !)
signierte_145_6_10.verification_notes: The condition note references Leopold Schönbauer as an NS-Mitglied and directs to Wikipedia for further details. Leopold Schönbauer (1888–1963) was a prominent Austrian neurosurgeon and rector of the University of Vienna; his relationship to National Socialism is historically documented and contested. The cataloger's note is a substantive annotation about the dedicatee's political history that warrants human review before any public use of this record.

signierte_34_2_10.original_entry:
CHRISTIANSEN, Friedrich und Carl (Kriegsverbrecher)
Die Kapitäne Christiansen. Nach Logbüchern erzählt. Dritte, erweiterte Auflage. Mit 55 Abbildungen. Berlin Mittler 1937. 222 S. OLn. Mit je einer Widmung auf dem Vortitel.
signierte_34_2_10.verification_notes: The entry lists 'CHRISTIANSEN, Friedrich und Carl' as a single author entry, but the parenthetical '(Kriegsverbrecher)' suggests these are two individuals. Friedrich Christiansen was a German aviator and later a Wehrmacht general convicted of war crimes. The authorship of two persons listed together is unusual and may indicate this is a work about them rather than by them, or a collaborative memoir. The note 'Mit je einer Widmung auf dem Vortitel' implies two copies, each with a dedication, which may also indicate copies: 2. Human review recommended.

The individual IB volumes have their own authors; HUßMANN may be the collector/signer. Additionally, 'Deutsche Wappenkunst' is noted as signed and hand-corrected by the author, but it is unclear whether HUßMANN is the author of that work or another person. Human verification recommended. problematic_multi_volume: The volumes listed are individual Insel-Bücherei numbers, not a single multi-volume work. Volume numbers are used as identifiers (IB series numbers), not sequential volume numbers of one work. The 'Deutsche Wappenkunst' item is a separate publication not part of the IB series proper. Structure is ambiguous and cannot be cleanly parsed as a standard multi-volume set.

geschichte_54_3_3.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The title 'Siecles de Louis XIV' is corrected to reflect Voltaire's known work; the full title is 'Le Siècle de Louis XIV' (singular), but the entry uses the plural 'Siecles' — this may be a variant title for this specific edition and has been left as written. The accent in 'd´apres' (using a backtick/apostrophe) has been normalized to 'd'apres' in the edition field. api_concerned: The entry states four volumes are present out of five, with page counts 252, 245, 218, 262 S., and notes that Bandes 2, 3, and 4 are from 1803, while the main publication year is 1815. It is unclear which volume number corresponds to the 1815 volume (Band 1 is the most likely candidate, but this is not stated explicitly). problematic_multi_volume: The volume numbering is ambiguous — four page counts are given but only three volume numbers (2, 3, 4) are explicitly identified as from 1803; the fourth page count (262 S.) likely belongs to Band 1 (1815), but this cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. Band 5 is missing entirely.

buch_55_3_8.original_entry:
DRYANDER
Practicierbüchlin außerlesener Artzeneystück. Frankfurt Egenolph 1589. 264 S. Nummerierte Auflage von 1000 Exemplaren. Mit einem Nachwort von Eberhard Tiefenthaler. Lindau Antiqua-Verlag 1979. Ex. Nr. 131. OPbd.
buch_55_3_8.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes two editions — the original Frankfurt/Egenolph 1589 edition and a 1979 reprint by Antiqua-Verlag, Lindau. Following convention, the 1979 edition (first described in detail with format and copy number) appears to be the physically owned copy; however, the 1589 edition is listed first. The 1979 Lindau edition has been used as the main record as it is the copy actually held (identified by Ex. Nr. 131 and format notation). The original 1589 Frankfurt/Egenolph edition is noted here for reference. api_concerned: 'DRYANDER' is likely Johannes Dryander (1500–1560), a German physician and anatomist, but the given name is absent from the entry. Additionally, the page count of 264 S. may belong to the 1979 reprint rather than the 1589 original — this cannot be determined with certainty. The contributor Eberhard Tiefenthaler (author of the Nachwort) has not been added to contributors as his role is postface author, but this could be noted.

The publication year 1962 for a work about Robinson Crusoe illustrations by DUMOULIN F.A.L. is unusual and may warrant verification — this could be a facsimile or reprint edition. The word 'e' in 'voyage e aventures' may be a typo for 'et', but left uncorrected as it may reflect the original title. Human review recommended.

buch_66_3_8.original_entry:
FAKSIMILES
|| SCHMÄLTZL, Wolfgang
Ein Lobspruch der hochlöblichen und weitberühmten Küniglichen Stadt Wienn. 1547. Mit Beiheft von ca. 1970. 64 S. OPbd. Von dieser Ausgabe wurden 500 Exemplare hergestellt.

Darinnen die edelste…Kräuter…Von Conrad Rosbach. Frankfurt Johann Spieß 1588. 294 S., 34 S. Register und 30 S. Vorrede. Hannover Edition 'libri rari'1982. Überreicht von Schaper & Brümmer Salzgitter-Ringelheim. Ill. OPbd.
DRYANDER
PRACTICIERBÜCHLEIN außerlesener Artzeneistück Frankfurt Egenolph.1589. 264 S. Mit zahlr.Abb. Nachwort von Eberhard Tiefenthaler. Lindau Antiqua-Verlag 1979. Num. Auflage von 1000 Ex. mit Nr. 314. OPbd.
OEKONOMISCHES HANDBUCH FÜR FRAUENZIMMER
Erster Band: Kochbuch. Stuttgart Steinkopf 1795. 636, 26 gez. S.(Register) und XXII S. Kommentar. Hrsg. v. Steinkopf Verlag Stuttgart 1977. OPbd. ||
buch_66_3_8.verification_notes: This entry groups four distinct facsimile publications under the heading 'FAKSIMILES'. Each is a separate work by a different author/source, reissued as a facsimile edition. They have been parsed as volumes for structural purposes, but they are not truly volumes of a single multi-volume work. Each volume entry captures the original publication details and the facsimile reprint details in the notes field. The 'DRYANDER' entry refers to Johannes Dryander (1500–1560), a German physician and anatomist — this appears consistent. The 'PARADEIßGÄRTLEIN' by Conrad Rosbach is a known herbal work. The grouping as a single record is structurally ambiguous and may warrant splitting into separate records. Additionally, the OEKONOMISCHES HANDBUCH entry mentions only 'Erster Band' but no further volumes are listed, so it is unclear if additional volumes exist.

Almanach auf das Jahr 48 des zwanzigsten Jahrhunderts. Wien Agathonverlag 1948. 320 S. Herausgegeben von L. W. Rochowanski. Einband von Josef Hoffmann. Mit zahlr. Abb. und erstmals veröffentlichten Beiträgen. OPbd.m.OU (U. an den Rändern etwas eingerissen) Tadelloser Band
Almanach auf das Jahr 46. 318 S. ohne OU. Sehr gut erhalten.
Almanach auf das Jahr 47. 235 S. m. OU. Sehr gut erhalten. Alles Erschienene.
buch_0_1_8.verification_notes: The entry describes three volumes of the Almanach series (years 46, 47, and 48), noted as 'Alles Erschienene' (all published volumes). The main record is built around the year 48 volume as the first described. The year 46 and 47 volumes are listed as additional copies/volumes without publisher or year details explicitly stated, though they are part of the same series. Volume numbers are treated as the almanac year numbers (46, 47, 48) rather than sequential volume numbers. The entry is flagged as multiple_editions because three distinct volumes are described together, and as problematic_multi_volume because publisher/year details for volumes 46 and 47 are not explicitly stated (assumed same publisher and approximate years by context).

buch_1_1_8.original_entry:
BAUMGARTEN, Siegm. Jac.
Nachrichten von merkwürdigen Büchern. Zweiter Band so das siebente bis zwölfte Stück enthält nebst einem doppelten Register. Halle Gebauer 1752. 548 S. und 7 Bll. Register in sechs monatlichen Nachrichten über Bücher in einem Band. Mit zahlreichen Vignetten und schönen Initialen in lateinischer hebräischer und griechischer Schrift. Originalhalbpergamentband. Sehr gut erhalten.

buch_2_1_8.original_entry:

Textband XVI, 552 S. Bildband 328 S. Heidelberg Edition Braus 1986. OPbde

buch_3_1_8.original_entry:
BIBLIOTHEK WEISSTEIN
Katalog der Bücher des verstorbenen Bibliophilen Gotthilf Weisstein. Herausgegeben von Fedor von Zobeltitz. Leipzig Gesellschaft der Bibliophilen 1913. In zwei Bänden. Wurde nur an Mitglieder der Gesellschaft weitergegeben. Dieser Band trägt die Nr. 247. XIII, 735, 371 S OLn. Mit schönem Vorsatzpapier. Sehr gut erhalten.
buch_3_1_8.verification_notes: The page count 'XIII, 735, 371 S' is ambiguous: it is unclear whether XIII and 735 belong to volume 1 and 371 to volume 2, or whether all three figures relate to a single volume. The entry states 'In zwei Bänden' but only one physical copy seems to be described (with a membership number 247). Volume assignment of page counts is uncertain and has been distributed tentatively (XIII+735 to vol. 1, 371 to vol. 2).

buch_4_1_8.original_entry:
COLLECTION GEORG HIRTH
Abtheilung. Deutsch Tanagra. Text. Porzellanfiguren des achtzehnten Jahrhunderts gesammelt von Georg Hirth. Mit zahlreichen Illustrationen, Vignetten, Leisten und Schlussstücken, ferner 109 Lichtdrucktafeln und 75 Autotypische Tafeln. (4 S.Vorwort), LXXXIV, 159 S.
Abtheilung. Kunstgewerbe – Ölgemälde – Graphische Künste. Diese Abteilung enthält im Katalog – Texte 90 Illustrationen, außer zahlreichen Vignetten, Leisten, Schlussstücken etc. ferner 39 Lichtdruck-Tafeln und 32 autotypische Tafeln. 158 S.
München Hirth´s Kunstverlag 1898. OBrosch. 2. Band nach Textteil Buchblock gebrochen, sonst sehr gut erhaltene Bände. Größe 4°.
buch_4_1_8.verification_notes: The entry labels both volumes as 'Abtheilung' without numbering them explicitly as Band 1 and Band 2; volume numbering as 1 and 2 is inferred from order of description. The page count for volume 1 is given in a mixed format '(4 S. Vorwort), LXXXIV, 159 S.' which cannot be reduced to a single integer and has been placed in the notes field instead. The publisher name appears as 'Hirth´s Kunstverlag' in the entry; normalized to 'Hirth's Kunstverlag'. It is unclear whether Georg Hirth is the author or the collector/subject; the entry states 'gesammelt von Georg Hirth', suggesting he is the collector, but he is also the publisher's namesake. Flagged for human review.

buch_5_1_8.original_entry:
DEUTSCH-ÖSTERREICHISCHES KÜNSTLER UND SCHRIFTSTELLERLEXIKON
Herausgegeben von Herm. Cl. Kosel.
Biographien der Wiener Künstler und Schriftsteller. Redigiert von Paul Gustav Rheinhardt. Wien Gesellschaft für graphische Industrie. 1902. 619 S.
Biographien und Bibliographie der deutschen Künstler und Schriftsteller in Österreich-Ungarn ausser Wien. (Mit Nachtrag für Wien.) Wien 1906. 388 S.
Illustrierte Originalleinenbände. Sehr guter Zustand.
buch_5_1_8.verification_notes: Two-volume work. Volume 1 (1902, 619 S.) covers Wiener Künstler und Schriftsteller, edited/redacted by Paul Gustav Rheinhardt. Volume 2 (1906, 388 S.) covers deutsche Künstler und Schriftsteller in Österreich-Ungarn ausser Wien. Overall editor is Herm. Cl. Kosel. Rheinhardt listed as contributor (Redigiert) for Volume 1. Top-level publication year and publisher reflect Volume 1 as first-described.

buch_6_1_8.original_entry:
FÜHRER DER DEUTSCHEN BUCHKUNST
Sonderdruck des Archivs für Buchgewerbe und Gebrauchsgraphik. Herausgegeben vom deutschen Buchgewerbeverein unter Mitwirkung der staatlichen Akademie für graphische Künste und Buchgewerbe zu Leipzig. 61. Jahrgang/Heft I. Leipzig Verlag des deutschen Buchgewerbevereins 1924. 105 S. Mit zahlreichen, teils montierten Abbildungen und einem Beiheft mit Werbung in 50 gez.S. OPbd. Gr.-4°. Sehr gut erhalten.

buch_7_1_8.original_entry:
GEIGY-HAGENBACH, K.
Autographen-Sammlung Basel. Als Manuskript gedruckt. (Nr.) 68 (gestempelt). Basel Gasser & Cie 1929.300. Tafel XXXIII.
Nachtrag III zur Autographen-Sammlung von K. Geigy-Hagenbach Basel. Als Manuskript gedruckt. Basel Gasser & Cie 1933. 304 - 376, Tafel XXXIV – LXXVII.
Nachtrag IV zur Autographen-Sammlung von K. Geigy-Hagenbach Basel. Als Manuskript gedruckt. Basel Gasser & Cie 1939. 378 – 412, Tafel LXXVIII – LXXXXVII.
Alle drei Teile in einem Band. OLn. Rückentitel. Einband stark gebraucht, sonst sehr gute Erhaltung. Wurde von Autor an Freunde verschenkt. Mit einer hs. Widmung des Autors.
buch_7_1_8.verification_notes: The entry describes three parts bound in one volume: the main work (1929) and Nachtrag III (1933) and Nachtrag IV (1939). Note that Nachtrag I and II are not present; the entry jumps directly to Nachtrag III, suggesting earlier supplements exist but are not included. Page numbers for volumes 2 and 3 are given as ranges (304–376 and 378–412) rather than total page counts, so the 'pages' field for those volumes is left null. The roman numeral 'LXXXXVII' in the last volume's plate range is unusual (typically written XCVII); it has been retained verbatim. The volume numbering assigned here (1, 2, 3) reflects the physical order of the three parts in the bound volume, not an original numbering scheme.

buch_9_1_8.original_entry:
KIRSCH, Adam Friederich
Abundantissimum Cornucopiae Lingue Latine et Germanicae Selectum…(etwa: Auswahl aus einem reichhaltigen Füllhorn der lateinischen und deutschen Sprache…) Nürnberg Buggel und Seitz 1731. Mi einem Titelkupfer und 1 gef. Tabelle. (20 Bll), 1152, 376, 92 S. OLn. mit zwei Schließen. Originalausgabe mit ausführlichen Angriffen auf die Raubdrucker dieser Zeit. Recht gute Erhaltung und vollständig.
buch_9_1_8.verification_notes: Typo corrected: 'Mi einem' → 'Mit einem' in the illustrations field. The page count is complex: (20 Bll), 1152, 376, 92 S. — this is a multi-part pagination typical of 18th-century works; no single integer is assigned to pages field. The known work by Adam Friedrich Kirsch is 'Abundantissimum Cornucopiae Linguae Latinae et Germanicae Selectum' (1718, with later editions); the 1731 Nürnberg edition by Buggel und Seitz is consistent with known bibliographic records.

buch_13_1_8.verification_notes: The original language is inferred as Czech based on the author's name (František Muzika was a Czech artist and typographer) and the fact that the entry states 'Deutsch von Max A. Schönwälder', indicating a translation into German. The original language field has been set to 'Tschechisch' accordingly, but this could not be verified with certainty from the entry alone. Milan Hegar is listed as contributor for graphical design ('Graphische Gestaltung').

buch_14_1_8.original_entry:
SCHELLER, Imm. Joh. Gerh.
Lateinisch-deutsches und deutsch-lateinisches Handlexicon vornehmlich für Schulen. Erster oder lateinisch-deutscher Theil. Leipzig Fritsch 1792. XX, 3214 Sp. Mit einem Frontispiz. OHLdr. mit geprägtem Rückenschild. Recht gut erhalten.
buch_14_1_8.verification_notes: The entry describes only 'Erster oder lateinisch-deutscher Theil' (the first/Latin-German part), suggesting this is one volume of a multi-part work. The second part (deutsch-lateinisch) may exist as a separate volume not catalogued here. The page count is given as 'XX, 3214 Sp.' (Spalten/columns), not pages — this has been left in the original_entry but not mapped to the 'pages' field since it refers to columns, not pages. The author 'Imm. Joh. Gerh. Scheller' is consistent with Immanuel Johann Gerhard Scheller (1735–1803), a known German classical philologist and lexicographer.

buch_25_2_8.original_entry:
ARNOLD, Theodor
Grammatica Anglicana concentrata oder kurzgefaßte Englische Grammatic, worinnen die richtige Pronunciation, und alle zur Erlernung dieser Sprache unumgänglich nöthigen Grundsätze aufs deutlichste und leichteste abgehandelt sind. Wobey I. Ein reiches Wörterbuch. II. Gewöhnliche Redensarten. III. Englische Idiotismi oder Anglicismi….Fünfte verbesserte Auflage von M.J.B. Rogler. Leipzig und Züllichau im Verlag der Waysenhaus- und Frommannischen Buchhandlung 1781. 314 S.
Vollständiges Deutsch-Englisches Wörterbuch als zweiter Theil zu Theodor Arnolds English Vocabulary ganz neu ausgearbeitet von M. Johann Bartholomäus Rogler. Erste Ausgabe. Leipzig und Züllichau bey N. S. Frommanns Erben 1790. 236 S. M. Exlibris.
Zwei Titel in einem OLdr. Band. Einband berieben. Gut erhalten.
buch_25_2_8.verification_notes: The entry describes two separately published works bound together in one Originalleder volume ('Zwei Titel in einem OLdr. Band'). Volume 1 is the 5th improved edition of Arnold's Grammatica Anglicana (1781), edited by M.J.B. Rogler. Volume 2 is the first edition of a companion German-English dictionary (1790) authored by M. Johann Bartholomäus Rogler, published by a different publisher (N. S. Frommanns Erben). The multiple_editions flag is set because two distinct editions/works with different publication years and publishers are described together. Top-level fields reflect the first-described work. The Exlibris note appears in the second volume's description but is applied to the physical condition of the bound volume as a whole.

buch_93_4_8.verification_notes: This entry lists five individual annual almanac volumes identified by their year number rather than sequential volume numbers. Volume numbers have been mapped to the almanac year (1909, 1918, 1940, 1986, 1990). For volumes 1986 and 1990, the subtitle and actual publication year differ from the almanac year (e.g., 1986 almanac published Frankfurt 1985; 1990 almanac published Frankfurt 1989). The structure is clear for all five volumes, but the use of almanac years as volume identifiers rather than sequential numbers is noted as potentially ambiguous.

The role of Chodowiecki (likely Daniel Chodowiecki, 18th-century German-Polish engraver) in relation to this work is unclear from the entry alone — the title suggests a work about his contemporaries, but no author or editor is named.

1919. X. Jahrgang Doppelheft 1/2 (1-74 S.)
1921. XII. Jahrgang IV, 127; 47 S. ||
buch_194_8_8.verification_notes: The entry lists multiple individual issues and annual volumes of the periodical 'Zwiebelfisch' across different years (1910, 1912, 1918, 1919, 1921), but the structure is inconsistent: some entries give page ranges (suggesting individual issues), others give total page counts (suggesting complete annual volumes). The main record fields reflect the 1912 fourth Jahrgang as the first-described entry. Page counts for individual volumes could not be cleanly parsed as single integers due to the range and compound notation formats used. The format and condition information in the entry appear to apply specifically to the 1912 volume.

renaissance_66_3_4.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The main record is the 1912 Diederichs first edition (Erster Band, XV + 244 S., OHPgt.). A secondary entry mentions an 'Erweiterte Neuausgabe aus 1978 mit Band 1 und 2' (XV, 244 und 389 S., OLn.), which is not parsed into the main fields per convention. problematic_multi_volume: The 1912 edition is described as 'Erster Band' only, suggesting a second volume may exist but is not described in the entry. The 1978 Neuausgabe contains two volumes (Band 1: XV, 244 S.; Band 2: 389 S.) but belongs to the secondary edition. The volume structure of the primary 1912 edition cannot be fully determined from the entry.

renaissance_71_3_4.verification_notes: The entry contains two works bound together: Machiavelli's 'Der Fürstenspiegel' (translated from Italian) and Friedrich der Große's 'Der Antimachiavell' (translated from French). No translator names are given for either work, hence missing_person is set. The main title is treated as Machiavelli's work; Friedrich der Große is listed as contributor for the second included text. api_concerned is set because this is a combined volume of two distinct works by different authors, and the cataloging structure is ambiguous as to whether Friedrich der Große should be treated as a co-author, contributor, or subject of a separate work within the same volume.

renaissance_14_1_4.verification_notes: No publisher is listed for this 2014 Breitenbrunn edition. The Hypnerotomachia Poliphili is a well-known Renaissance work originally written in Italian (with Latin and other languages interspersed), not purely in Latin as the entry states ('Übersetzung aus dem Lateinischen'). The original language field has been set to 'Lateinisch' as stated in the entry, but this may be an oversimplification or error. Human verification of publisher and original language description is recommended. Pages calculated as XVI (16) + 708 = 724.

IVANKA, Endre von (Hg.
Die letzten Tage von Konstantinopel. Der auf den Fall Konstantinopels 1453 bezügliche Teil des dem Georgios Sphrantzes zugeschriebenen 'Chonicon Maius' übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von …Graz Styria 1954. 101 S. Byzantinische Geschichtsschreiber. OBrosch.
mittelalter_57_3_7.verification_notes: The subtitle contains 'Chonicon Maius' which is likely a typo for 'Chronicon Maius' (missing 'hr'); corrected_by_api set true for this correction. The entry lists IVANKA, Endre von as editor (Hg.) and the subtitle phrase 'übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von …' trails off with an ellipsis and no name, indicating the translator/introducer name is missing; missing_person set true. Based on context (same person listed as Hg.), IVANKA is likely also the translator, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. The original language is inferred as Greek given the source text (Georgios Sphrantzes, Byzantine chronicle).

Im Reiche des goldenen Apfels. Des türkischen Weltenbummlers Evliya Celebi denkwürdige Reise in das Giaurenland und in Stadt und Festung Wien anno 1665. Übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von ...Graz Styria 1963. 292 S. Osmanische Geschichtsschreiber. Hg. v. Kreutel. OPbd. Einband stark berieben, sonst gutes Ex.
mittelalter_59_3_7.verification_notes: The entry states 'Übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von ...' with an ellipsis, which typically signals a missing person. However, given that KREUTEL, Richard F. is the named author/editor of the series and is the known translator of Evliya Çelebi's works, the translator is identified as Kreutel. The ellipsis appears to be a cataloging shorthand referring back to the main entry name. No missing_person flag set as the identity is unambiguous from context.

mittelalter_60_3_7.original_entry:
KREUZFAHRER EROBERN KONSTANTINOPEL

mittelalter_60_3_7.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry states 'Übersetzt eingeleitet und erklärt von Franz Grabler' — Grabler is the translator and editor/introducer, but no original author is listed. The work appears to be a collection of Byzantine chronicle sources about the Fourth Crusade (1204). api_concerned: The original language of the source texts is not stated; likely Greek or Latin given the Byzantine Geschichtsschreiber series context. No original author is named, which may be intentional for an anthology of sources. Grabler's role spans translator, introducer, and commentator, making it ambiguous whether he should be listed as editor, contributor, or translator — he has been placed in all relevant roles.

mittelalter_17_1_7.original_entry:
BYZANZ WIEDER EIN WELTREICH
Das Zeitalter der Makedonischen Dynastie. Teil I. Ende des Bilderstreites und Makedon.
Renaissance (Anfang 9. Bis Mitte 10. Jahrhundert). Nach dem Geschichtswerk des Johannes
Skylitzes. Übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von Hans Thurn. Graz Styria 1983. 308 S. OKart. Byzantinische Geschichtsschreiber Hrsg. v. Johannes Koder Band XV.
mittelalter_17_1_7.verification_notes: The entry lists Johannes Skylitzes as the source author (the work is based on his chronicle) and Hans Thurn as translator/editor of this volume. Johannes Koder is listed as series editor of 'Byzantinische Geschichtsschreiber'. The role of Skylitzes as 'author' vs. subject of the translation is clear from context. However, the entry structure places 'BYZANZ WIEDER EIN WELTREICH' as the series or overarching title, while the actual volume title refers to the Macedonian Dynasty. This dual-title structure may warrant verification. Additionally, 'OKart.' is expanded as 'Originalkartoniert' which is a reasonable inference but not a standard RAK abbreviation — it may simply mean 'Kartoniert'.

russland_83_4_17.verification_notes: The translation credit is complex: Alexander Herzen produced the original German translation, and Irene von Losow revised/edited it and added an afterword. Herzen is listed as translator and Losow as contributor accordingly. Additionally, 'Die Fundgrube' appears to be a series or collection name and has been placed in condition for now, but may belong in series_title — flagged for human review. The portrait mentioned is attributed to Diderot, which is plausible as Denis Diderot wrote a biographical portrait of Princess Dashkova.

Dostojewski' as the author's daughter who wrote this biographical work, but no full given name is provided — only the initial 'A.'. The daughter of Fyodor Dostoyevsky was Aimée (Lyubov) Dostoyevskaya, who wrote a biography of her father. api_concerned: The entry uses 'DOSTOJEWSKI' as the main heading without a given name, making it ambiguous whether this is a work by or about Dostoyevsky. Based on the subtitle 'Geschildert von seiner Tochter A.Dostojewski', this appears to be a biography of Fyodor Dostoyevsky written by his daughter. The author field has been left null and the daughter listed as contributor pending verification.

The work is a critical/biographical study of Dostoyevsky by Julius Meier-Graefe, not a work by Dostoyevsky himself. The author has been set to Meier-Graefe accordingly, but the filing under Dostoyevsky's name may reflect a cataloging convention (subject entry) that should be verified.

russland_92_4_17.original_entry:
DOSTOJEWSKI, F.M.
Gesammelte Werke. Zehnbändige revidierte Dünndruckausgabe. Übertragen von E. K. Rahsin
1. Der Doppelgänger. Frühe Romane und Erzählungen. 1961. 916 S.
2. Onkelchens Traum. 3 Romane. 1960. 997 S.
3. Aufzeichnungen aus einem Totenhaus und drei Erzählungen. 1964. 6.-8.Tsd. 861 S.
4. Der Spieler. Späte Romane und Novellen. 1959. 781 S.
5. Tagebuch eines Schriftstellers. Notierte Gedanken. 1963 666 S.
6. Rodion Raskolnikoff. Schuld und Sühne. 1964. 62.-65.Tsd. 762 S.
7. Der Idiot. 1973. 72.-74. Tsd. 979 S.
9. Der Jüngling. Roman. 1963. 43.-45. Tausend 892 S.
10. Die Brüder Karamasoff. Roman in vier Teilen und einem Epilog. 1959. 95.Tsd. 1303 S.
Der Band 8 Die Dämonen fehlt. Originalleinenbände. Bei vier Bänden Rücken verblasst, sonst tadellose Bände.
russland_92_4_17.verification_notes: Band 8 (Die Dämonen) fehlt physisch und ist daher nicht im volumes-Array enthalten. Publisher und Erscheinungsort fehlen im Eintrag vollständig; es handelt sich wahrscheinlich um die Piper-Ausgabe (München), kann aber nicht mit Sicherheit bestätigt werden. Die Bände haben unterschiedliche Erscheinungsjahre (1959–1973), kein gemeinsames Publikationsjahr angegeben.

Der Idiot. Roman. Mit einem Nachwort von Ilma Rakusa 981 S.
Die Dämonen. Roman. Mit einem Nachwort von Alexandar Flaker. 1033 S.
Der Jüngling. Roman. Mit einem Nachwort von Aage Hansen-Löve. 918 S.

russland_93_4_17.verification_notes: The entry lists 10 volumes but only 10 distinct volume entries are identifiable, matching the stated total. Volume 5 nachwort author is listed as 'Alexandar Flake' and volume 8 as 'Alexandar Flaker' — these appear to be two different spellings of the same person (likely Alexander Flaker, a known Slavicist), possibly one is a typo. Not corrected due to ambiguity between the two spellings across volumes. The translator 'E. K. Rahsin' is the known translator of Dostoyevsky into German (also spelled Rahsin/Rachsin); spelling retained as given. Volume numbers are inferred from order of appearance as the entry does not explicitly number them.

russland_144_6_17.verification_notes: The entry states 'A.d. Amerikan.' (translated from American English), but Gorbachev's original work 'Perestroika' was written in Russian, not English. The translation language designation is ambiguous and may be an error in the cataloging. No translator name is given.

russland_145_6_17.original_entry:

Judenmassakre. A.d.Russ. übersetzt von Sonja Wermer. Umschlag von Berthold Löffler. 6.-10.Tausend. Wien Wiener Verlag 1904. 106 S. Bunter OPbd.
russland_145_6_17.verification_notes: The author is catalogued as 'GORJKI, Maxim', which is a German transliteration variant of Maxim Gorky (Горький). The more common German spelling is 'Gorki' or 'Gorki', but 'Gorjki' is a known variant. No correction applied. Additionally, Berthold Löffler is listed as the cover designer (Umschlag); he has been placed in contributors. The title 'Judenmassakre' (Jewish Massacre) corresponds to Gorky's essay/pamphlet about the Kishinev pogrom of 1903, which is consistent with the 1904 publication date.

russland_343_14_17.original_entry:
TOLSTOI, Lew
Kreutzersonate. A.d.Russ. von Olga Radetzkaja. Sofja TOLSTAJA Eine Frage der Schuld. Aus anlass der 'KreutzersonateW von Lew Tolstoi. A.d.Russ von Alfred Frank. Nachworte von Olga Martynova und Oleg Jurjew. Zürich Manesse 2010. 428 S. Oln. m. OU und Lesebändchen.
russland_343_14_17.verification_notes: This entry appears to be a combined volume containing two works: (1) Lew Tolstoi's 'Kreutzersonate', translated by Olga Radetzkaja; and (2) Sofja Tolstaja's 'Eine Frage der Schuld' (a response to the Kreutzersonate), translated by Alfred Frank. Both works are bound together in one volume (428 S. total). The entry lists two translators for two distinct texts. The main title field captures the first work; Sofja Tolstaja is listed as contributor since her work is the secondary text in the volume. The string 'KreutzersonateW' in the original appears to be a typo for 'Kreutzersonate' (likely OCR or transcription artifact with 'W' appended). Nachworte by Olga Martynova and Oleg Jurjew are not separately captured. Human verification recommended to confirm the combined-volume structure and whether both authors should be listed as co-authors rather than author/contributor.

russland_290_12_17.verification_notes: The authorship of 'Zeugenaussage' (Testimony) is disputed. Solomon Volkow claimed to have recorded Shostakovich's memoirs, but the authenticity has been widely questioned by musicologists. The entry lists Schostakowitsch as author and Volkow as editor/recorder, which reflects the book's own presentation, but the true authorship is a matter of scholarly controversy.

russland_175_8_17.original_entry:
KENNAN, George
Sibirien! Deutsch von E. Kirchner. Zweite Auflage. Berlin Cronbach 1890. IX, 267 S.
Sibirien! Deutsch von E. Kirchner. Neue Folge. Vierte Auflage. Berlin Cronbach 1890. IV, 218 S. Zwei Bände als Band 1. Dritter Band als Reclam-Bändchen 102 S.
Zeltleben in Sibirien und Abenteuer unter den Korjäken und anderen Stämmen in Kamtschatka und Nordasien. Deutsch von E. Kirchner. Vierte Auflage. Berlin Cronbach 1890. VII, 355 S. Als Band 2.
2 Halbleinenbände. Jeder Band mit Bibliotheksstempel. Band 1 verbunden (zweiter vor erstem Band). Gut erhaltene Bände. Vollständig.
russland_175_8_17.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Band 1 enthält zwei separate Werke in unterschiedlichen Auflagen (Zweite Auflage und Neue Folge, Vierte Auflage) sowie ein Reclam-Bändchen (102 S.), was eine saubere Trennung erschwert. problematic_multi_volume: Die Struktur von Band 1 ist komplex — er vereint zwei eigenständige Titel (Sibirien! und Sibirien! Neue Folge) plus ein Reclam-Bändchen. Die genaue Seitenzahl für Band 1 als Ganzes ist nicht eindeutig angebbar (IX+267 + IV+218 + 102 S.). Band 2 (Zeltleben in Sibirien) ist klar mit VII, 355 S. beschrieben.

russland_4_1_17.verification_notes: The volume structure is complex and partially ambiguous. Band 1 appears to consist of two sub-volumes (Band 1.1 with X, 543 S. and Band 1.2 'Forschungen, Briefe und Dokumente' with 184 S.). Band 2 similarly has two sub-volumes (Erste Abteilung: VII, 615 S. and Zweite Abteilung: 376 S.). The notation 'Band 2. 1.2. Band 2.' is unclear and may indicate sub-volume numbering. Additionally, Band 2 was published by a different publisher (Verlag Siegfried Cronbach, Berlin, 1893), making this a multiple_editions / split-publisher situation. The main record uses the first-described publisher (Norddeutsches Verlags-Institut, 1891). The second publisher and year (Siegfried Cronbach, 1893) apply to Band 2 and are noted in the volumes array.

buddhismus_15_1_3.verification_notes: The entry contains the typo 'rvidiert' (missing 'e'), corrected to 'revidiert' in interpretation. The translation chain is complex: Burton and Arbuthnot produced an English translation from Sanskrit; Kolb and Weltmann then translated that into German. Both pairs are listed as contributors accordingly. Friedrich Ziehlke is listed as editor/reviser who compared against the original Sanskrit text and wrote the foreword.

buddhismus_18_1_3.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The author name 'LAO – TSE' has been normalized to 'LAO-TSE' (removing the spaces around the dash, which appear to be a typographic inconsistency). api_concerned: The title 'Die Bahn und der rechte Weg des …' is a known German rendering of the Tao Te Ching (Daodejing) by Lao-Tzu, translated by Alexander Ular. However, the title as given ends with an ellipsis ('des …'), which may indicate an incomplete transcription of the full title (possibly 'des Laotse' or similar). The phrasing 'Der chinesischen Urschrift nachgedacht' indicates this is a free rendering rather than a strict translation. No substantive concern about authorship or identity, but the truncated title warrants verification.

buddhismus_19_1_3.verification_notes: Laotse (Laozi) is the traditional attributed author of the Tao Te Ching, associated with Taoism, not Buddhism or Hinduism. The topic classification may be incorrect or the library uses a broad Eastern philosophy category. No correction applied.

The author 'LIÄ DSI' is the Wade-Giles romanization of Liezi (列子), a classical Daoist text — this entry appears under the topic BUDDHISMUS/HINDUISMUS but the work is Daoist; this may be a cataloging placement concern.

wien_23_1_10.original_entry:

wien_23_1_10.verification_notes: The original work by Wolffgang Schmeltzl dates to 1548. The entry states 'Wienn in Österreich, durch Mattheum Kuppitsch, Buchfürer 1849' and then 'Reprint aus Wien 1849 von Sommer (vormals Strauß)'. It is unclear whether the publisher is Kuppitsch or Sommer (vormals Strauß), and whether the 1849 date refers to the reprint or an earlier edition being reprinted. The relationship between Kuppitsch and Sommer/Strauß in this entry requires human verification. The author name 'Wolffgang Schmeltzl' is an archaic spelling variant and has not been corrected.

wien_59_3_10.original_entry:
KISCH, Wilhelm
Die alten Strassen und Plaetze von Wien´s Vorstädten und ihre historisch interessanten Häuser. Ein Beitrag zur Culturgeschichte Wien`s mit Rücksicht auf vaterländische Kunst, Architektur, Musik und Literatur. I. Band. Mit 201 Illustrationen. Wien Oskar Frank 1888. (4), IV, 576 S. Bunter OLn. Band mit schönen Vorsätzen und 3 fachem Goldschnitt. Sehr gut erhalten.
Band 2.: Die alten Strassen und Plätze Wiens und seine historisch interessantesten Häuser. Ein Beitrag zur Culturgschichte Wiens mit Rücksicht auf Vaterländische Kunst, Architektur, Musik, Literatur etc. In 20 Heften mit zahlreichen Illustrationen. Wien Selbstverlag des Verfassers 1879. XXXII S. Anschließend noch einmal das Titelblatt. Zusätzlich dazu: Mit einem Plane und 277 Illustrationen nach den Originalen von… Wien M. Gottlieb 1883. OLn. Band, sowie Band 1, ohne Goldschnitt. Einband vom Buchblock gelöst, durch Leinenstreifen leicht zu reparieren. Sonst neuwertig. Zus.
wien_59_3_10.verification_notes: missing_person: In Band 2, the illustrator/original artist is referenced with 'nach den Originalen von…' but the name is cut off with an ellipsis — the person is absent. multiple_editions: Band 2 appears to have two distinct publication records: one from Wien Selbstverlag des Verfassers 1879 and one from Wien M. Gottlieb 1883, which may represent different editions or parts of the same volume; these cannot be cleanly separated. problematic_multi_volume: The structure of Band 2 is ambiguous — it is unclear whether the 1879 and 1883 publications are sub-volumes, separate editions, or supplementary material. The top-level fields reflect Band 1 (the first-described volume) as the main record. api_concerned: The title and subtitle differ between Band 1 and Band 2 (Band 1 refers to 'Vorstädten', Band 2 to 'Wiens' more broadly), and the publishers and years differ significantly (1888 vs. 1879/1883), suggesting these may not be a straightforward two-volume set but rather related but distinct publications. Human verification recommended.

Wien im Jahre 1683. Geschichte der zweiten Belagerung der Stadt durch die Türken im Rahmen der Zeitereignisse. Aus Anlaß der zweiten Säcularfeier verfaßt im Auftrag des Gemeinderates des k. k. Reichshaupt- und Residenzstadt Wien von … Mit zahlr. Abbildungen im Text und mehrfach gefalteten Tafeln. Wien Waldheim 1883. XVII, 487 S. OLn. m. goldgepr. Deckel und Rücken. wien_93_4_10.verification_notes: The entry contains 'verfaßt im Auftrag des Gemeinderates … von …' followed by an ellipsis, indicating the author's name was left incomplete in the cataloging. However, the author 'RENNER, Victor v.' is given at the top of the entry and is consistent with the known historical work. The ellipsis likely refers to the author being named in the subtitle phrase but left out there; the main author field is populated from the heading. missing_person flag set due to the trailing ellipsis after 'von' in the subtitle description.

wien_124_5_10.original_entry:
TIETZE, Hans
Alt-Wien in Wort und Bild. Vom Ausgang des Mittelalters bis zum Ende des XVIII. Jahrhunderts
Herausgegeben von… Zweite vermehrte Auflage. Mit 230 Abbildungen auf 144 Tafeln und 8 farbigen Kunstbeilagen. Wien Schroll 1926. 72, 144 S.
Das vormärzliche Wien in Wort und Bild. Herausgegeben von Hans Tietze. Mit 247 Abbildungen und einem farbigen Titelbild. Wien Schroll 1925. 116, 132 S.
2 Titel in einem Band. OLn. Gr.-4° mit goldgeprägtem Deckel und Rücken. In bestem Zustand. Zus.
wien_124_5_10.verification_notes: missing_person: The first title 'Alt-Wien in Wort und Bild' is introduced with 'Herausgegeben von…' but no editor name follows — the name is missing (ellipsis only). Hans Tietze is named as editor only for the second title. multiple_editions: The entry describes two distinct titles bound together in one physical volume ('2 Titel in einem Band'). The first title (main record) is 'Alt-Wien in Wort und Bild', Wien Schroll 1926, 72+144 S., Zweite vermehrte Auflage, with 230 illustrations. The second title is 'Das vormärzliche Wien in Wort und Bild', herausgegeben von Hans Tietze, Wien Schroll 1925, 116+132 S., with 247 illustrations and a coloured frontispiece. Both share the same physical binding (OLn. Gr.-4° mit goldgeprägtem Deckel und Rücken). The abbreviation 'Zus.' likely indicates a combined/zusammengebunden note.

Band: Vom Römischen Imperium zum Byzantinischen Reich. Herausgegeben von Paul Veyne. A. d. Französ. 1994. 5. Auflage: 19. Tausend.

neuzeit_27_2_4.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Each volume has a different edition and print run noted (e.g., Band 1: 5. Auflage 19. Tausend; Band 2: 5. Auflage 18.–20. Tausend; Band 3: 4. Auflage 15.–17. Tausend; Band 4: 3. Auflage 14.–16. Tausend; Band 5: 1. Aufl. 1993), making it impossible to assign a single edition or year to the overall set. These are recorded per-volume in the volumes array. api_concerned: The entry spells the series editor's name as 'Georges Duby' in the header but 'George Duby' (without the final 's') in the description of Band 2. The correct form is 'Georges Duby'; the Band 2 spelling appears to be a typo, but no correction has been applied since it is within a volume note. Additionally, no translator name is given despite the work being translated from French; translator field left null. The entry also includes 'Lesebändchen' (ribbon bookmarks) as a physical feature, noted here as it does not fit neatly into any schema field.

Der Schlüssel zu den grossen Mysterien nach Henoch, Abraham, Hermes Trismegistos und Salomon. Mit Abb. Interlaken Ansata-Verlag 1981. 303 S. OBrosch.
esoterik_23_1_3.verification_notes: Éliphas Lévi (1810–1875) was a French occultist who wrote in French. This appears to be a German translation of his work 'La Clef des grands mystères' (1861), but no translator is named in the entry. The original language is almost certainly French, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. Additionally, the author's name is commonly spelled 'Lévi' with an accent; the entry omits the accent, which may be a cataloging simplification rather than an error.

esoterik_59_3_3.verification_notes: This entry is a thematic grouping under the heading TAROT containing six separate bibliographic records by different authors. No single main record can be identified. All six are listed here for reference: (1) DOUGLAS, Alfred: 'Ursprung und Praxis des Tarot', transl. from English, Köln: Diederichs 1986, 253 S., OBrosch., series: Diederichs Gelbe Reihe DG 062. (2) STEINER-GERINGER, Mary: 'Tarot als Selbsterfahrung', Köln: Diederichs 1985, 236 S., OBrosch., series: Diederichs Gelbe Reihe DG 055. (3) TEGTMEIER, Ralph: 'Tarot. Geschichte eines Schicksalsspiels', Köln: DuMont 1986, 181 S., mit zahlr. Abb., series: Du Mont Taschenbücher. (4) PAPUS: 'Tarot der Zigeuner', Interlaken: Ansata-Verlag 1985, transl. from French, 3. Auflage, mit zahlr. Abb., 313 S., OBrosch. (5) KAPLAN, Stuart R.: 'Der Tarot. Geschichte Deutung Legesysteme', München: Hugendubel 1985, 3. Auflage, 255 S., mit zahlr. Abb., OBrosch. (6) POLLACK, Rachel: 'Tarot. 78 Stufen der Weisheit', transl. from English, mit 100 Abbildungen, München: Knaur 1985, 396 S., TB. The multiple_editions flag is set because this entry contains multiple distinct works that cannot be collapsed into a single record.

sonstiges_1_1_4.original_entry:
ATLAS MAIOR
Johannes Bläu Atlas Maior of 1665. Köln Taschen 2006. In zwei Bänden.
Germania 215 S.
Germania Austria Helvetia 223-404 S.
Originalleinenbände in Original Schuber 4° Neuwertig
sonstiges_1_1_4.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The author name 'Bläu' is likely a typo for 'Blaeu' (Joan Blaeu, the Dutch cartographer responsible for the Atlas Maior of 1665). The Taschen 2006 reprint is well documented. Correction applied conservatively as 'Bläu' is the form given in the entry and 'Blaeu' is the standard form. api_concerned: The Taschen 2006 edition of the Atlas Maior is known to exist in multiple thematic volumes; the two volumes described here ('Germania' and 'Germania Austria Helvetia') appear to be the same or overlapping volume. The first volume is titled 'Germania' with 215 pages, and the second is 'Germania Austria Helvetia' with pages 223-404 (182 pages in continuous pagination). This continuous pagination suggests these may be two parts of a single volume rather than two independent volumes, or the entry may reflect a specific two-volume subset of the larger Taschen series. Human verification recommended.

sonstiges_9_1_4.original_entry:
FALKENBUCH KAISER FRIEDRICH DES ZWEITEN
De arte venandi cum avibus. Zwölf Faksimile-Drucke aus des Codex MS.Palatinus Latinus 1071 der Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana. Einführung und erläuternde Beschreibung von Carl Arnold Willemsen. ADEVA 1973. 51 S. Folio OLn.m.OU. Sehr gut erhalten.
sonstiges_9_1_4.verification_notes: The place of publication for ADEVA (Akademische Druck- und Verlagsanstalt) is Graz, Austria, but it is not stated in the entry and has not been inserted. Carl Arnold Willemsen is listed as providing the introduction and descriptive commentary ('Einführung und erläuternde Beschreibung'), so he has been placed in contributors rather than authors or editors. The original work 'De arte venandi cum avibus' is attributed to Emperor Frederick II; no author field has been populated since this is a facsimile edition of a medieval manuscript.

Verlagsfrisch.
sonstiges_10_1_4.verification_notes: The work is a reprint (1980, BEGO Bremen) of the original 1733 Berlin edition (Johann Andreas Rüdiger). The original was a translation from French into German; no translator is named in the entry. missing_person is set true because the translator is absent. The original publication data (Berlin, Johann Andreas Rüdiger, 1733) is noted here for reference but the main record reflects the reprint edition as the physically described copy.
