sonstiges_18_1_4.original_entry:
HANCARVILLE, Pierre-Francois Hugues
Bilder aus dem Privatleben der römischen Cäsaren. Und Denkmäler des Geheimkults der römischen Damen. A.d.Französ. von Heinrich Conradt und Alfred Semerau. 2 Bände. Mit 50 und 48 Abb. Desch (Basel) ca. 1965. 310, 212 S. Sonderausgabe für Wissenschaftler… Verpflichtungserklärung liegt bei. Nummerierte Ausgabe mit der Nr. 3947. Original Seiden- Bände in Seidenschuber. Sehr gut erhalten.
sonstiges_18_1_4.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: Publication year given as 'ca. 1965' — recorded as 1965 with the approximation noted. The hyphenated 'Seiden- Bände' in the original appears to be a line-break artifact; expanded to 'Seidenbände'. api_concerned: The author Pierre-François Hugues d'Hancarville (1719–1805) was an 18th-century antiquarian; the works described here appear to be erotic antiquarian publications. The translator names 'Heinrich Conradt' and 'Alfred Semerau' are listed together; both are known translators of erotic/historical literature, which is consistent. The publisher 'Desch' in Basel is plausible for this type of Sonderausgabe. The 'Verpflichtungserklärung liegt bei' indicates this was a restricted scholarly edition. No major identity concerns, but the approximate date and the unusual edition type warrant human verification.

sonstiges_71_3_4.original_entry:
SCHATZKAMMER MECHANISCHER KÜNSTE
Des hoch- und weitberühmeten Capitains Herrn Augustini de Ramellis, de Masanzana…durch Henning Großen den Jüngern im Jahr MDCXX. Nachdruck aus dem Jahre 1620. Hannover Vinzentz Verlag 1976. 319 S. Mit zahlr. Abb. OPbd.
sonstiges_71_3_4.verification_notes: The entry mentions 'durch Henning Großen den Jüngern' which refers to the original 1620 German publisher/translator Henning Grosse the Younger. The author Agostino Ramelli (c.1531–c.1600) wrote 'Le diverse et artificiose machine' (1588) in Italian/French; this is a German reprint of the 1620 edition. The author is not explicitly named in the entry — 'Augustini de Ramellis' in the subtitle identifies him. Author has been inferred from world knowledge; flagged for verification. Publisher name 'Vinzentz Verlag' in Hannover is noted but not widely documented for this title.

sonstiges_44_2_4.original_entry:
WEINBAU
|| GOETHE, Hermann
Aus der biologischen Weinbau-Versuchs-Station. Mit 4 colorierten Tafeln. Wien Verlag des Vereines zum Schutze des österreichischen Weinbaues 1891. 39 S. OBrosch.
VEREIN ZUM SCHUTZE DES ÖSTERREICHISCHEN WEINBAUES
Mitteilung II (Artikel über Bekämpfung der Reblaus). 1888. 70 S. OBrosch.
WICHMANN, Fritz
Der Kampf um die Weinverbesserung im deutschen Reiche. Wirtschaftsgeschichte und Produktionsstatistik des deutschen Weinbaues. Jena Gustav Fischer 1902. IX, 202. OBrosch.
KOBER, Franz
Über das Vortreiben veredelter Schnittreben (Stratifikationsverfahren) Wien Hitschmann 1910. Mit Abb. 32 S. OBrosch
BERICHT ÜBER DIE VERBREITUNG DER REBLAUS
(Phylloxera vastatrix) in Österreich in den Jahren 1902 und 1903….Nebst den Gesetzen, Verordnungen und Erlässen, betreffend die Reblaus. … Wien Verlag des k. k. Ackerbauministeriums. 345 S. OBrosch.
PROTOKOLL
Über 1897 im k. k. Ackerbauministerium wegen Revision und Ergänzung der Vorschriften betreffend Kunst- und Halbweine abgehaltene Enquete.
ERGEBNISSE
Der von ... eingeleiteten Unfrage betreffend…Kunst- und Halbweine.(Nachtrag).
K. K. Hof- und Staatsdruckerei 1897, 1898. 39, 58 S. OBrosch.
BERICHT ÜBER DIE VERBREITUNG DER REBLAUS
… 1910, 1911, 1912. Wien k. k. Ackerbauministerium 1913. 172 S. (der Teil danach fehlt.) OBrosch.
STATUTEN
Der Obst-und Weinbaugenossenschaft Wädensweil in Wädensweil 1895. 7 S. OBrosch.
Siehe auch HANDBUCH DES WEINBAUES
Alle Broschuren zum Weinbau zusammen
sonstiges_44_2_4.verification_notes: This entry is a Sammelkonvolut (collected pamphlet volume) on the topic of viticulture (Weinbau), comprising eight distinct separately-authored or institutionally-authored items bound or kept together. Each item has been parsed as a volume entry. — corrected_by_api: 'Unfrage' corrected to 'Umfrage' in volume 6 notes (clear typo). — missing_person: Volume 6 (PROTOKOLL/ERGEBNISSE) references 'von ...' (ellipsis) indicating a person or institution responsible for the enquiry whose name is absent. — problematic_multi_volume: The entry is a heterogeneous convolute, not a standard multi-volume work; volume numbers are assigned editorially for parsing purposes only. The PROTOKOLL and ERGEBNISSE items (volumes 6) are treated as a single entry because they share a publisher/year line and page count '39, 58 S.', but their relationship is ambiguous. The entry also contains a cross-reference 'Siehe auch HANDBUCH DES WEINBAUES' which has been noted but not parsed as a separate record.

de-lit-texte_227_10_20.verification_notes: The entry lists Bände 1–4 of the novel plus a fifth supplementary volume ('Kleines Adreßbuch') by Rolf Michaelis, totalling 5 physical volumes, though the entry states 'Bd. 1 – 4'. The supplementary register volume is treated here as volume 5 but is not formally numbered in the entry. Page ranges for volumes 2–4 are given as continuous pagination (e.g. 487-1008, 1017-1382, 1391-1891); the 'pages' field for these volumes records the starting page of each volume rather than a standalone page count, as no isolated page count is given. The publication year for the set as a whole is ambiguous (volumes span 1979–1983); no single year is assigned to the top-level record. Rolf Michaelis is listed as contributor (compiler of the register volume), not as author or editor of the main work.

de-lit-texte_102_5_20.verification_notes: The illustrator Jose Guadalupe Posada (1852–1913) was a Mexican printmaker known for his calavera imagery. This appears to be an anthology or collection of texts related to death illustrated with his work. The editor 'Roger Shatulin' is not widely known; the name may be a variant or possible error, but no unambiguous correction can be made. The work's authorship is unclear — it may be an anthology without a single author, which is consistent with the editor-only attribution.

de-lit-texte_111_5_20.original_entry:
DIE DEUTSCHEN VOLKSBÜCHER
Herausgegeben von Richard Benz. Jena Diederichs
Historia von D. Johann Fausten dem weitbeschreyten Zauberer und Schwarzkünstler. 5. – 7. Tausend 1924.
Till Eulenspiegel 1912. 217 S. 200 S.
Fortunatus Ampedo Andolosia Mit Holzschnitten 1912. 244 S.
Das buch der geschicht des grossen allexanders 1924. 357 S.
Tristan und Isalde. 1912. 237 S. Bunte OPbde. Alle sehr gut erhalten. 7 Bände erschienen.
de-lit-texte_111_5_20.verification_notes: The entry states '7 Bände erschienen' but only 5 distinct volume titles are listed (Historia von D. Johann Fausten; Till Eulenspiegel; Fortunatus Ampedo Andolosia; Das buch der geschicht des grossen allexanders; Tristan und Isalde). Two volumes are unaccounted for. Additionally, the Till Eulenspiegel entry lists two page counts (217 S. and 200 S.) which may indicate two separate volumes or a cataloging ambiguity. Volume numbers are not given for any volume. The illustrations note ('Mit Holzschnitten') applies specifically to the Fortunatus volume. No single overall publication year is given; individual volumes are dated 1912 or 1924.

de-lit-texte_112_5_20.original_entry:
DIE GELIEBTE
Und andere zeitgemäße Physiologien. Herausgegeben von Johannes Willms. Frankfurt Insel 1998. 222 S. TB.
de-lit-texte_112_5_20.verification_notes: The entry lists no author for this anthology. The title 'Die Geliebte und andere zeitgemäße Physiologien' edited by Johannes Willms (Insel, 1998) is a collection of 19th-century French physiologies in German translation. The missing author field is expected for an anthology, but the entry gives no indication of the original authors or translators. No author is listed, which is consistent with an edited anthology, so missing_person is not set. api_concerned is set because the work is a translation/anthology of French texts but no translator or original language is indicated in the entry.

de-lit-texte_1_1_20.verification_notes: The condition note states 'Mit einer Widmung vom Autor' (with a dedication from the author), but Rose Ausländer (1901–1988) died in 1988, well before this 2012 publication. The dedication likely comes from the editor/commentator Martin A. Hainz rather than from Ausländer herself. Human verification is recommended to clarify whose dedication is meant.

de-lit-texte_2_1_20.original_entry:
BLUMAUER, Alois
Virgils Aeneis travestirt von …Erster Band mit Vignette Frankfurt und Leipzig 1798. 1.-4.Buch 83 S. Zweiter Band mit Vignette Frankfurt und Leipzig 1798. 5.-8.Buch 81 S. Dritter Band mit Vignette Frankfurt und Leipzig 1798. 9. Buch. 84 S.Vierter und letzter Band ...ausgeführt von Professor Schaber. Frankfurt und Leipzig 1794. Mit einer Vignette und einem Prolog an Blumauer und An den Leser vom Verfasser 1793. Zehntes Buch. 10, 116 S. OHLdr. Recht gut erhalten.
de-lit-texte_2_1_20.verification_notes: The fourth and final volume (Zehntes Buch) is dated 1794, earlier than the first three volumes (1798), and is stated to have been completed by Professor Schaber — consistent with the known publication history of Blumauer's work, where the final volume was completed posthumously by another hand. However, the year 1794 for the fourth volume predates the 1798 volumes, which is unusual and warrants verification. The entry covers only books 1, 2, 3, 5–10 of the Aeneid (books 4 and 6–9 are grouped into volumes 1–3), but the volume/book mapping is somewhat ambiguous: volume 1 covers books 1–4, volume 2 covers books 5–8, volume 3 covers book 9 only, and volume 4 covers book 10 only — this asymmetry is noted. The '10, 116 S.' for volume 4 likely means 10 preliminary pages plus 116 text pages. Publisher name is not given, only place of publication. Professor Schaber's full name is not provided in the entry.

de-lit-texte_14_1_20.original_entry:
EULENSPIEGEL
Der wiedererstandene Eulenspiegel, das ist: wunderbare und seltsame Historien Tyll Eulenspiegels, eines Bauern Sohn, gebürtig aus dem Lande zu Braunschweig. Sehr kurzweilig zu lesen. Gedruckt in diesem Jahr. Mit 27 lithographischen Tafeln. Ohne Verlag und Jahr. Um 1850. XXXVI, 37-195 S. Durchgehend stark benutzt, mit Unterstreichungen und Kommentar. OHln.
de-lit-texte_14_1_20.verification_notes: No publisher or place of publication given; entry explicitly states 'Ohne Verlag und Jahr' with an approximate date of 'Um 1850'. Publication year set to 1850 as an approximation. The page count notation 'XXXVI, 37-195 S.' indicates 36 preliminary pages plus main text pages 37–195; total pages field set to 195 as the final page number. The entry is catalogued under 'EULENSPIEGEL' as a corporate/title heading with no author named, which is consistent with the anonymous/traditional nature of the Till Eulenspiegel texts.

de-lit-texte_41_2_20.original_entry:
STIFTER, Adalbert
Studien. Dritter Band (Den nachgelassenen Werken entnommen.) Pest Heckenast 1873. 412 S. Mit 4 Stahlstichen. OLn. mit Goldprägung.
de-lit-texte_41_2_20.verification_notes: Stifter's 'Studien' is a well-known collection originally published in 6 volumes (1844–1850) by Heckenast. A posthumous 'Dritter Band' described as 'Den nachgelassenen Werken entnommen' in 1873 is unusual — the original third volume appeared in 1847. This may be a later collected or posthumous edition with reorganized contents. The subtitle notation in parentheses may indicate a posthumous compilation rather than the original Band 3. Human verification of this specific edition is recommended.

de-lit-texte_44_2_20.original_entry:
VOLKSBÜCHER
Herausgegeben von G. O. Marbach. Leipzig Wigand 18 38.
7. Geschichte von den sieben Schwaben. Nebst einigen schwäbischen Volksliedern. 59 S.
8. Geschichte von der heiligen Pfalzgräfin Genoveva. 60 S.
9. 10. Geschichte von den vier Heymonskindern. Geschichte von dem gehörnten Siegfried. 187 S. (Dazu noch eigener Band OBrosch.)
11. Geschichte von den drei Schwestern. Geschichte von den drei Rolandsknappen. Schneeweißchen. Bruder Lustig. 82 S.
12. Der wiedererstandene Eulenspiegel. 108 S.
Alle Geschichten mit Holzschnitten von Richter u.a. OLn. m Lesebändchen. Gut erhalten.
de-lit-texte_44_2_20.verification_notes: Publication year written as '18 38' (with space) — corrected to 1838. The entry covers volumes 7–12 of the series; earlier volumes (1–6) are not listed. Volumes 9 and 10 are bound together with a combined page count of 187 S.; individual page counts cannot be determined. Additionally, a separate OBrosch. copy of volumes 9/10 is noted ('Dazu noch eigener Band OBrosch.'), triggering multiple_editions flag. The 'Richter' mentioned for the woodcuts is almost certainly Ludwig Richter, the well-known German illustrator associated with this series.

musik_129_6_11.original_entry:
SCHUMANN, Clara
Ein Künstlerleben. Nach Tagebüchern und Briefen von Berthold Litzmann. Leipzig Breitkopf und Härtel.
Erster Band: Mädchenjahre 1819 – 1840. Mit drei Bildnissen. Fünfte, aufs neue durchgesehene. Auflage. 1912. VII, 431 S.
Zweiter Band: Ehejahre 1840 – 1856. Mit zwei Bildnissen. Vierte, unveränderte Auflage. 1910. 416 S.
Dritter Band: Clara Schumann und ihre Freunde 1856 – 1896. Mit zwei Bildnissen Dritte durchgesehene Auflage. 1910. VI, 642 S.
Originalleinenbände mit goldgeprägtem Rücken und schönen Vorsätzen. Sehr gut erhalten.
musik_129_6_11.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Each of the three volumes appears in a different edition (5th, 4th, and 3rd respectively) and different years (1912, 1910, 1910), so no single publication year can be assigned at the top level. api_concerned: The entry lists SCHUMANN, Clara as the author, but this is a biography written by Berthold Litzmann based on diaries and letters. Clara Schumann is the subject; Litzmann is the actual author/editor. The cataloging convention here may treat Clara Schumann as the heading subject, with Litzmann as editor/compiler. Litzmann has been placed in the editors field, but human verification is recommended to confirm the intended authorship attribution.

musik_112_5_11.original_entry:
SACCHINI, A.
Oedip zu Colonos lyrisches Drama in drey Aufzügen aus dem Französischen des Guillard übersetzt von C. Herklots. Vollständiger Klavierauszug mit deutschen u. französischem Text bearbeitet und dem Fräulein Amalia von Liptay Achtungsvoll zugeeignet von Carl Klage. Berlin, In der Schlesingerschen Buch und Musikhandlung. N ° 269. Pr. 5 Rth. 12 gl. (1818). Originalpappband, Querformat. Oberer Einband vom Buchblock getrennt, jedoch vollständig erhalten. Innen durchwegs stockfleckig.
musik_112_5_11.verification_notes: The entry lists both C. Herklots (as translator of the libretto from French) and Carl Klage (as arranger of the piano reduction). The publication year 1818 is given in parentheses and appears to be an estimated or catalogued date rather than explicitly printed. The plate number N° 269 is consistent with Schlesinger Berlin publications of that era. Antonio Sacchini's opera 'Œdipe à Colone' (libretto by Guillard) was first performed in 1786; the German translation and piano arrangement by Herklots and Klage is plausible for 1818. The contributor roles are somewhat ambiguous: Herklots is the libretto translator, while Klage is the musical arranger and dedicator — both are listed as contributors, but Herklots is also flagged as translator for the is_translation field.

Stuckenschmidt, suggesting the physical copy is a facsimile reprint, not the original 1912 edition — yet the publication year is given as 1912. The year of the facsimile reprint is not stated and requires verification. The OLn. notation appears twice in the entry (once for the original description, once seemingly for the reprint copy).

A.' but the actual author of this work is Hermann Abert ('Von Hermann Abert'), who produced a revised and expanded edition of Otto Jahn's Mozart biography. The title field has been set to 'Mozart' and the author to Abert accordingly. The two main volumes have different edition numbers and years (Vol. 1: 9. Auflage 1978; Vol. 2: 8. Auflage 1975), which constitutes multiple editions within the same set; the publication_year at top level reflects the later volume (1978). The register volume (Vol. 3) was compiled by Erich Kapst (1966) and is listed as a contributor. corrected_by_api is set true because the catalogued heading 'MOZART, W. A.' was reinterpreted as the subject rather than the author, with Hermann Abert correctly identified as the author based on the entry text and world knowledge.

musik_87_4_11.original_entry:
MÜLLER, Franz Karl
W.A.Mozart Gesamtkatalog seiner Werke 'Köchel-Verzeichnis'. Neubearbeitet und herausgegeben von … Wien Paul Kaltschmid 1951. 448 S. OLn. m. OU. Neuwertig.
musik_87_4_11.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry states 'Neubearbeitet und herausgegeben von …' with an ellipsis, indicating the editor's name is absent. MÜLLER, Franz Karl is listed as the heading but may be the subject or compiler rather than the editor of this new edition. api_concerned: The Köchel-Verzeichnis is a well-known catalogue of Mozart's works. The standard editions were edited by Alfred Einstein (3rd ed., 1937) and later by Franz Giegling, Alexander Weinmann, and Gerd Sievers (6th ed., 1964). A 1951 Wien edition by Paul Kaltschmid is not readily identifiable in standard bibliographic sources, and the role of MÜLLER, Franz Karl in relation to this work is unclear. Human verification is recommended.

MULTIPLE EDITIONS / COMPLEX STRUCTURE: The entry conflates two distinct series — 'NEUESTE THEATER-BIBLIOTHEK' (Wien, Wallishauser, 1791) and 'THEATERSAMMLUNG' (Ohne Ort und Verlag, 1790) — into a single record describing five volumes. It is unclear whether these are truly one bound set or two separate series bound together. The volume numbering is also inconsistent: Band 2 and Band 3 are listed for NEUESTE THEATER-BIBLIOTHEK, while Band 1 is listed for THEATERSAMMLUNG, and subsequent bands (4th and 5th) lack explicit numbers in the entry. 4. PROBLEMATIC MULTI-VOLUME: Volume numbers for the 4th and 5th bands are not explicitly stated in the entry; they have been inferred from context and the final statement 'Alle fünf Bände'. The assignment of contents to volumes 4 and 5 is an editorial inference and should be verified. 5. API CONCERNED: The entry mixes two series (different publishers, years, and possibly different physical objects) into one record. Human verification is needed to determine whether this is one composite set or two separate works catalogued together.

monographien_19_1_3.original_entry:
GINZBURG, Natalia
'Es fällt schwer, von sich selbst zu sprechen, aber es ist schön'. Natalia Ginzburgs Leben in Selbstzeugnissen. Zusammengestellt und aus dem Italienischen von Maja Pflug. Berlin Wagenbach 2001. 121 S. Mit Abb. WAT 414. 2 Ex.
monographien_19_1_3.verification_notes: Maja Pflug is listed as both compiler ('Zusammengestellt') and translator ('aus dem Italienischen von'). This appears to be an anthology/compilation of Ginzburg's own writings rather than a single authored work, so Ginzburg is the source author and Pflug is the compiler/translator. The entry is catalogued under Ginzburg's name, which is appropriate. WAT 414 is a Wagenbach Taschenbuch series number.

monographien_21_1_3.original_entry:
GRACIAN, Balthasar

monographien_21_1_3.verification_notes: The entry is filed under 'GRACIAN, Balthasar' but the actual author is Kurt Petsche, who wrote a study about Balthasar Gracián. Gracián is the subject, not the author. The filing convention may be intentional (subject-based cataloging), but this should be verified.

monographien_22_1_3.original_entry:
GREFFRATH, Mathias
Montaigne heute. Leben in Zwischenzeiten. Zürich Diogenes 1998. 411 S. OLn.
monographien_22_1_3.verification_notes: Mathias Greffrath is a German journalist and author. A book titled 'Montaigne heute. Leben in Zwischenzeiten' published by Diogenes in 1998 is not readily verifiable in standard bibliographic sources. The topic context indicates 'fremdsprachiger Autoren' (foreign-language authors), but Greffrath is a German author. This may indicate a cataloging placement error or that the entry refers to a different author. Flagged for human verification.

monographien_50_3_3.original_entry:
PROUST, Marcel
Von George D. Painter. Eine Biographie. In zwei Bänden. Mit Abb. Frankfurt Suhrkamp 1980. 537, 668 S. st 561.
monographien_50_3_3.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under 'PROUST, Marcel' as the heading, but the actual author is George D. Painter — this is a biography of Proust written by Painter. The author has been set to PAINTER, George D. accordingly. corrected_by_api is set true because the heading name was the subject, not the author.

monographien_52_3_3.original_entry:
PROUST, Marcel
Eugen Atget: Marcel Proust. Ein Bild von Paris. Texte und Photographien. Herausgegeben von Arthur D. Trottenberg. Frankfurt Insel 1987. 206 S. Mit zahlr. Photographien. Itb.
monographien_52_3_3.verification_notes: The title 'Eugen Atget: Marcel Proust. Ein Bild von Paris' is unusual — Eugène Atget (1857–1927) was a French photographer who documented Paris, and Marcel Proust was a contemporary literary figure. The entry lists Proust as the author, but the work appears to be a compilation of Proust texts paired with Atget photographs, edited by Arthur D. Trottenberg. The authorship attribution to Proust alone may be incomplete or misleading; Atget's role as photographer and Trottenberg's role as editor are significant. Human verification is recommended to confirm the correct authorship and cataloging of this work.

monographien_53_3_3.original_entry:
PROUST, Marcel
Orte und Räume. Beiträge des Symposions der Marcel-Proust Gesellschaft in Leipzig 2001. Herausgegeben von Angelika Corbineau-Hoffmann. Frankfurt Insel 2003. 224 S. OBrosch. Verlagsfrisch.
monographien_53_3_3.verification_notes: The entry is filed under 'PROUST, Marcel' but the work is a symposium volume edited by Angelika Corbineau-Hoffmann about Proust, not a work authored by Proust himself. Proust is the subject, not the author. The cataloging convention of filing under the subject person may be intentional, but no author has been assigned to the record as a result.

monographien_54_3_3.original_entry:

monographien_54_3_3.verification_notes: missing_person: The translator is not named despite the entry indicating a translation from English ('A.d.Engl.'). The entry is filed under PROUST, Marcel, but the actual author of this biography is Ronald Hayman; Proust is the subject.

monographien_55_3_3.original_entry:
PROUST, Marcel
Leseerfahrungen deutschsprachiger Schriftsteller von Theodor W. Adorno bis Stefan Zweig. Herausgegeben und mit einem Nachwort versehen von Achim Hölter Frankfurt Suhrkamp 1998. Erstausgabe. 365 S. st 2791
monographien_55_3_3.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under PROUST, Marcel, but the title 'Leseerfahrungen deutschsprachiger Schriftsteller von Theodor W. Adorno bis Stefan Zweig' describes a collection of German-language writers' reading experiences — this appears to be an anthology about Proust (or reading in general) edited by Achim Hölter, not a work authored by Proust himself. The listed author may actually be the subject rather than the author of the work. Human verification is needed to determine whether Proust should be listed as author, subject, or not at all.

biographie_170_7_27.original_entry:
EIN GOTT DER KEINER WAR
Arthur Koestler, Andre Gide, Ignazio Silone, Louis Fischer, Richard Wright, Stephen Spender schildern ihren Weg um Kommunismus und ihre Abkehr. Mit Vorwort von Richard Crossman und einem Nachwort von Franz Borkenau. Zürich Europa Verlag 1950. 263 S. OBrosch. Neuwertig.
biographie_170_7_27.verification_notes: The known English title of this anthology is 'The God That Failed', edited by Richard Crossman. The entry lists Crossman as author of the preface only, with no explicit editor named. Based on world knowledge, Crossman is the editor of the volume; he has been placed in the editors field accordingly. Additionally, 'Andre Gide' likely should carry a diacritic: 'André Gide' — not corrected as the entry omits it and it may be intentional. The phrase 'ihren Weg um Kommunismus' likely should read 'zum Kommunismus' but this is reproduced as found.

biographie_410_17_27.verification_notes: The entry describes three separate works by Frank McCourt, all published by Luchterhand München: (1) 'Die Asche meiner Mutter' (1996, 507 S., OPbd.m.OU und Lesebändchen) — parsed as the main record; (2) 'Ein rundherum tolles Land' (1999, 487 S., OPbd.m.OU und Lesebändchen); (3) 'Tag und Nacht und auch im Sommer' (2006, 331 S., OPbd.m.OU). All three are described as 'verlagsfrische Bände'. These are three distinct titles, not editions of the same work; the multiple_editions flag is set because they are bundled in a single entry. api_concerned is set because all three works are translations from English (McCourt wrote in English), but no translator is named and the original language is not stated in the entry.

biographie_420_17_27.original_entry:
MIRABEAU

biographie_420_17_27.verification_notes: The entry lists MIRABEAU as the author, but 'Denkwürdigkeiten' (Mémoires) of Mirabeau were compiled and edited posthumously rather than written solely by him. Khlesl´s, des Cardinals, Directors des geheimen Cabinetes Kaisers Mathias, Leben. In vier Bänden. Zweiter Band. Mit einem Facsimile Khlesl´s. Wien 1847 Kaulfuß 1847. 333, 405 S. Hlbd. mit Bibliotheksstempel. Sehr gut erhalten.
biographie_26_2_27.verification_notes: The year '1847' appears twice in the entry ('Wien 1847 Kaulfuß 1847'), which is likely a duplication error. The page count '333, 405 S.' is unusual for a single volume — it may represent two separately paginated sections or a cataloging anomaly. Only the second volume is described; the other three volumes of the four-volume set are not present. The 'Hlbd.' abbreviation typically means 'Halbband' (half-binding/half-leather), not a full volume division, but is used here as a format notation. The full name of the author is Joseph von Hammer-Purgstall; the entry gives only the surname.

biographie_29_2_27.verification_notes: The author 'HEYNE, C.T.' could not be confidently identified. A well-known classical scholar named Christian Gottlob Heyne (1729–1812) predates this 1843 publication, making him an unlikely author. The initials 'C.T.' do not match any widely known author of Napoleon biography. Human verification of the author identity is recommended.

biographie_53_3_27.verification_notes: The entry uses the subject name 'SONNENFELS, Joseph von' as the title field, which is typical for a biography or collected works. The actual title of the work is not given explicitly. Based on world knowledge, this likely refers to the volume 'Joseph von Sonnenfels' edited by Helmut Reinalter, published by the Österreichische Akademie der Wissenschaften in 1988 as part of a series on Austrian Enlightenment figures. The title field has been populated with the subject name as given, but human verification is recommended to confirm the actual title.

biographie_54_3_27.original_entry:
SRBIK, Heinrich Ritter von
Metternich. Der Staatsmann und der Mensch.
Band 1. Mit 8 Abb. a. Taf. XIV, 787 S.
Band 2. Mit 8 Abb. a. Taf. IX, 643 S.
München Bruckmann 1925. Ein mit einem Namensverzeichnis für beide Bände ein abgeschossenes Werk. Erst 1954 erschien bei Bruckmann Band 3. OLn. mit goldgeprägtem Bucheinband. Buchrücken von Band 1 leicht aufgeplatzt, sonst sehr gut erhalten.
biographie_54_3_27.verification_notes: The entry describes the two-volume 1925 edition as the main holding (Bände 1 and 2). A third volume published in 1954 by the same publisher (Bruckmann) is mentioned as a later addition but is not part of the main catalogued set. multiple_editions is set true because of this secondary edition reference. The word 'abgeschossenes' is likely a typo for 'abgeschlossenes' (completed), but as it appears in a descriptive note rather than a bibliographic field, no field value was altered.

biographie_55_3_27.original_entry:
TAUBER, Richard
Herausgegeben von Heinz Ludwigg. Vorwort von Leo Blech. 24 Textbeiträge, 98 photographische Abbildungen und 5 Textzeichnungen von Kitz Hane. Berlin Elsner 1928. 95 S. Gesicht und Maske Band 1. OLn.
biographie_55_3_27.verification_notes: The editor's name 'Heinz Ludwigg' is unusual — the double 'g' may be a typo for 'Ludwig', but it could also be a legitimate surname. Flagged for verification rather than corrected. The illustrator 'Kitz Hane' is unverified and may also be a transcription error. The entry is catalogued under 'TAUBER, Richard' as subject, with no author listed — this appears to be a biography/tribute volume edited by Ludwigg.

biographie_56_3_27.original_entry:
THÜRHEIM, Gräfin Lulu
Mein Leben. Erinnerungen aus Österreichs großer Welt. Herausgegeben von Rene van Rhyn. München Georg Müller.
1788 – 1819. 1923. XVI, 390 S. Mit dreiunddreißig Bildbeigaben.
1788 – 1819. 1923. 372 S. Mit 40 Bildbeigaben.
1819 – 1852. 1914. XII, 382 S. Mit sechsunddreißig Bildbeigaben.
1819 – 1852. 1914. XII, 326 S. Mit sechsunddreißig Bildbeigaben.
Vier Originalleinenbände mit Lesebändchen, tadellose Erhaltung
biographie_56_3_27.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry lists four volumes but appears to describe two different sets of two volumes each — one set published 1923 (covering 1788–1819) and one set published 1914 (covering 1819–1852). Within each period, two entries with differing page counts and illustration counts are given, suggesting either two copies or two editions of each volume. It is unclear whether this is a 2-volume work with duplicate copies, or a 4-volume set. The entry concludes with 'Vier Originalleinenbände', suggesting four physical volumes are present. problematic_multi_volume: The volume numbering is ambiguous — volumes are identified only by date ranges, not by explicit volume numbers. The two entries for '1788–1819' and the two for '1819–1852' may represent different copies or different editions of the same volumes. api_concerned: The work by Gräfin Lulu Thürheim is known as a 4-volume memoir published by Georg Müller, München, but the discrepancy in page counts and illustration counts between the two entries for each period cannot be resolved without physical inspection. The editor's name 'Rene van Rhyn' may be a variant spelling of René van Rhyn — flagged for verification.

islam_19_1_3.original_entry:
LANDAU, Jacob M./Hamilton A.R.GIBB
Arabische Literaturgeschichte. Zürich Artemis 1968. 340 S. OPbd.m.OU. Klein-8°. (Bibliothek des Morgenlandes). Sehr gut erhalten
islam_19_1_3.verification_notes: The entry lists two authors: LANDAU, Jacob M. and GIBB, Hamilton A.R. However, H.A.R. Gibb's 'Arabic Literature' is a well-known standalone work first published in 1926 (2nd ed. 1963), and Jacob M. Landau is known for works on Arab theater and politics, not typically co-authoring with Gibb. The Artemis 1968 German edition 'Arabische Literaturgeschichte' in the 'Bibliothek des Morgenlandes' series may be Gibb's work alone, with Landau possibly as translator or editor rather than co-author. The entry format (LANDAU listed first, GIBB second) is unusual if Gibb is the primary author. Flagged for human verification of authorship roles. Additionally, corrected_by_api: the name 'Hamilton A.R.GIBB' was parsed as 'GIBB, Hamilton A.R.' to normalize the display format.

islam_51_3_3.original_entry:
OSMANISCHE GESCHICHTSSCHREIBER
Herausgegeben von Richard F. Kreutel. Graz Styria. OKart.
Band 1. Kara Mustafa vor Wien. Das türkische Tagebuch der Belagerung Wien 1683, verfaßt vom Zeremonienmeister der Hohen Pforte. Übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von Richard f. Kreutel. 141 S.
Band 9. Der fromme Sultan Bayezid. Die Geschichte seiner Herrschaft (1481 – 1512) nach den altosmanischen Chroniken des Oruc und des Anonymus Hanivaldanus. Übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von Richard F. Kreutel. 311 S.
Band 10. Der Löwe von Temeschwar. Erinnerungen an Ca`fer Pascha den Älteren, aufgezeichnet von seinem Siegelbewahrer `Ali. Unter Mitarbeit von Kalr Teply übersetzt, eingeleitet und erklärt von Richard F. Kreutel. 294 S. ||
islam_51_3_3.verification_notes: 1) Typo corrected: 'Kalr Teply' in Band 10 corrected to 'Karl Teply' (corrected_by_api). 2) The entry lists only Bands 1, 9, and 10 of the series; the total number of volumes is unknown and left null (problematic_multi_volume). 3) format_expanded for 'OKart.' expanded as 'Originalkart.' — this is a plausible expansion but not a standard RAK abbreviation in the provided list; flagged for verification (api_concerned). 4) The sources translated in Band 10 are originally in Ottoman Turkish; Band 1 is also from Turkish. Band 9 draws on altosmanischen Chroniken. Original language noted as Türkisch overall, but individual volumes may differ in source language nuance. 5) Minor inconsistency in Band 1: 'Richard f. Kreutel' (lowercase f) — treated as a typo for 'Richard F. Kreutel'.

aegypten_15_1_3.original_entry:
DELITZSCH, Friedrich
Babel und Bibel. Ein Vortrag. Mit 51 Abbildungen. 46. bis 50. Tausend. Dritte durchgesehene Ausgabe. Leipzig Hinrichssche Buchhandlung 1903. 78 S.
Zweiter Vortrag über Babel und Bibel. 36. bis 40. Tausend. Mit 17 Abbildungen im Text und 3 farbigen Tafeln. Stuttgart Deutsche Verlags-Anstalt 1903. 50 S. Eingebunden ein Verlagsprospekt von Hinrichs Buchhandlung. Zwei Vorträge in einem Band. OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten.
aegypten_15_1_3.verification_notes: The entry describes two separate works by the same author bound together in one volume. The first (main) entry is 'Babel und Bibel. Ein Vortrag', Leipzig, Hinrichssche Buchhandlung, 1903, 78 S., 46.–50. Tausend, Dritte durchgesehene Ausgabe, with 51 illustrations. The second work bound with it is 'Zweiter Vortrag über Babel und Bibel', Stuttgart, Deutsche Verlags-Anstalt, 1903, 50 S., 36.–40. Tausend, with 17 illustrations in text and 3 coloured plates. Both are physically bound together as one volume (OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken). The multiple_editions flag is set because two distinct editions/works with different publishers, page counts, and edition statements are described together. The first-described work is used as the main record per cataloging convention.

The series volume is listed as 'Band III' of 'Religiöse Stimmen der Völker', herausgegeben von Walter Otto — Walter Otto is the series editor, not the volume editor; Ungnad is the translator and introducer. api_concerned flagged because it is unclear whether Ungnad should be listed as translator only or also as editor/compiler of the volume.

reisen_215_9_13.verification_notes: The entry states the text was translated from Middle High German ('Mittelhochdeutschen'), but Mandeville's Travels was originally written in Anglo-Norman French (or possibly Latin), not Middle High German. The Middle High German version is itself a medieval translation. Christian Buggisch translated from a Middle High German manuscript version into modern German, which is accurately described in the entry, but the 'original_language' field reflects the source text used by the translator (Middle High German), not the ultimate original language of the work. This distinction may require human review. Additionally, Buggisch is listed both as translator and editor, which is consistent with the entry text.

reisen_11_1_13.original_entry:
DICTIONNAIRE GEOGRAPHIQUE-PORTATIF OU DESCRIPTION
Des Royaumes, Provinces, Villes,… et autres lieux considerables des quatre parteis du monde… Traduit de L`Anglois, sur la treizieme edition de Laurent Echard. Par M.Vosgien. Nouvelle edition, revue, corrigee et augmentee. La Haye Libraires Associes 1776. 8, 679 S. und 11 S. Explication des termes de marine. Text doppelspaltig. Mit 2 gefalteten Karten. OLdr. Mit Lesebändchen. Einband nur gering berieben. sehr gut erhalten.
reisen_11_1_13.verification_notes: The entry states the work is translated from the English ('Traduit de L`Anglois') based on the thirteenth edition of Laurent Echard, and that the translator/editor is M. Vosgien. However, 'Vosgien' is a pseudonym for Jean-Baptiste Ladvocat, a French clergyman and lexicographer. The original work is by Laurence Echard (not 'Laurent Echard'), an English geographer. Vosgien is listed here as both the named person on the title page and the translator/adapter, which is accurate, but the entry's attribution is ambiguous as to whether Vosgien is the author or translator. He is both the adapter and the named author of this French edition. Additionally, the page count given is '8, 679 S. und 11 S.' — the 8 preliminary pages and 11 pages of 'Explication des termes de marine' are noted separately; only 679 is captured in the pages field. Human verification recommended.

The year difference (1875 vs. 1874) and differing Annoncen-Beilage page counts suggest these may be different printings or editions. The first copy is treated as the main record per cataloging convention. problematic_multi_volume: The entry states 'Zwei Bände von Meyers Reisen in einem Band', meaning two volumes bound as one physical book. reisen_185_8_13.original_entry:
HUMBOLDT, Alexander von
Reisen in die Aequinoctial-Gegenden des neuen Continents. In deutscher Bearbeitung von Hermann Hauff. Nach der Anmerkung und unter Mitwirkung des Verfassers. Einzige von A. v. Humboldt anerkannte Ausgabe in deutscher Sprache. Stuttgart Cotta 1861-1862. Drei Teile in einem Band. Band 1: XVI, 260; 300; 242 S. OHLdr. m. goldgeprägtem Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten. Gr.8°. Band 2, Teil 4-6 fehlt.
reisen_185_8_13.verification_notes: The work was originally written in French ('Voyage aux régions équinoxiales du Nouveau Continent'). Hermann Hauff is listed both as contributor (German adaptation) and effectively as translator; both roles are noted. The entry covers Band 1 (containing Teile 1-3 in one physical volume, with pages XVI+260, 300, and 242) and notes that Band 2 (Teile 4-6) is missing from the collection. Because Band 2 is absent, its volume data cannot be parsed. The publication year spans 1861-1862; 1861 is used as the main year. The total_volumes cannot be determined with certainty from the entry alone. The role of 'Hauff' is ambiguous: described as 'deutsche Bearbeitung' (adaptation/reworking) rather than a straight translation, though the work is clearly a translation from French; api_concerned is set accordingly.

reisen_191_8_13.verification_notes: missing_person: The translator is not named despite the entry indicating a translation from English ('A.d.Engl.'). api_concerned: The authenticity of 'Jacob von Ancona' as a historical figure is disputed — David Selbourne's book 'The City of Light' (1997) claims to be based on a 13th-century Jewish merchant's manuscript, but the work's authenticity has been widely questioned by scholars. The entry lists Jacob von Ancona as author and Selbourne as editor, which follows Selbourne's own framing, but this attribution may be misleading.

reisen_300_13_13.original_entry:

reisen_300_13_13.verification_notes: Vasco da Gama is listed as the author, but this is an eyewitness account of his voyage — the actual author of the original journal is generally considered anonymous (the so-called 'Roteiro' attributed to an unknown crew member, sometimes associated with Álvaro Velho). Listing Vasco da Gama as author may be a cataloging convention or simplification. Flagged for human review.

reisen_303_13_13.verification_notes: The name 'WAGNER, Richard' most commonly refers to the famous composer (1813–1883). However, there is also a contemporary Romanian-German author named Richard Wagner (born 1952) who writes travel literature and prose, and who published a book titled 'Reise in das Innere des Balkan' with Aufbau-Verlag in 2003. The entry almost certainly refers to this latter Richard Wagner, not the composer. Human verification is recommended to confirm the correct person.

reisen_310_13_13.original_entry:
ZIMMERMANN, W.F.A.
Der Erdball und seine Naturwunder. Populäres Handbuch der Physischen Geographie. Erster Band in zwei Abtheilungen. Allgemeine irdische und kosmische Verhältnisse. Die Atmosphäre. 15. Auflage. Mit einem Titelbild in Farbendruck, 3 Karten und 94 Holzschnitten. 1863. VI, VIII, 376 S.
Zweiter Band. Tellurischer Magnetismus. Die Gewässer der Erde. Sechzehnte Auflage Mit einem Titelbild in Farbendruck, 5 Karten und 126 Holzschnitten. 1865. VIII, 544 S.
Dritter Band erste Abtheilung fehlt
Dritter Band zweite Abtheilung: Pflanze, Thier und Mensch. Fünfzehnte Auflage. Mit 133 Abbildungen. 1863. 536, VI S.
Vierter Band. Malerische Länder- und Völkerkunde. Fünfte hie und da veränderte Auflage mit 120 Abbildungen. 1864. XVI, 784 S.
Erschienen bei Hempel, Berlin. In drei Bänden. OHLn. mit Rückenvergoldung. Innen gut, sonst mäßig erhalten.
reisen_310_13_13.verification_notes: The entry states 'In drei Bänden' (in three volumes) at the end, but the content describes four numbered volumes (Erster, Zweiter, Dritter, Vierter Band), making the volume count contradictory. The entry also notes that 'Dritter Band erste Abtheilung fehlt' (Third volume, first part is missing), which may explain the discrepancy — the physical set as held may consist of three bound volumes despite the work being published in four numbered volumes. Each volume has a different edition number (15th, 16th, 15th, 5th), indicating multiple editions across volumes; this is flagged under multiple_editions. The missing first Abtheilung of Band 3 is noted in the volume entry for Band 3. total_volumes is set to 4 per the textual content, not the 'In drei Bänden' note.

reisen_50_3_13.verification_notes: Minor typo corrected: 'Erhalttung' → 'Erhaltung' (double 't' in original). 'simon' capitalized to 'Simon' as it is a proper name. Ex.'): Stuttgart, Steingrüben, 1968, 405 S. The second entry is treated as a secondary edition/copy reference per cataloging convention. multiple_editions set true due to mixed copy/edition references.

reisen_256_11_13.original_entry:

reisen_256_11_13.verification_notes: The work appears to be a German translation of Raleigh's original English account 'The Discoverie of the Large, Rich, and Bewtiful Empyre of Guiana' (1595), so is_translation is set to true. However, no translator is named in the entry and the original language is not explicitly stated. api_concerned is set because the translator information is missing and cannot be confirmed without human review.

reisen_257_11_13.original_entry:
REISCHEK, Andreas
Sterbende Welt. Zwölf Jahre Forscherleben aus Neuseeland. Herausgegeben von seinem Sohn. Mit 94 bunten und einfarbigen Abbildungen und 2 Karten. 2. Auflage. Leipzig Brockhaus 1924. 334 S. OHln. Sehr gut erhalten.
reisen_257_11_13.verification_notes: missing_person: The editor is identified only as 'seinem Sohn' (his son) without a name given. The son's name (Karl Reischek) is known from world knowledge but has not been inserted, as the entry itself does not provide it.

reisen_258_11_13.original_entry:
REISE in die Aequinoctialgegenden des neuen Continents.
In deutscher Bearbeitung von Hermann Hauff. Nach der Anordnung und unter Mitwirkung des Verfassers. Einzige von A. v. Humboldt anerkannte Ausgabe in deutscher Sprache. Stuttgart Cotta 1861-62. Band 1 bis 3 von sechs Teilen. XVI, 260; 300; 242 S. OHLdr. m. goldgepr. Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten. Band 2 fehlt.
reisen_258_11_13.verification_notes: The entry states 'Band 1 bis 3 von sechs Teilen' (Bands 1 to 3 of six parts), with three page counts (XVI, 260; 300; 242 S.) and notes that 'Band 2 fehlt' (Band 2 is missing). This is ambiguous: if Band 2 is missing, only two physical volumes are present, yet three page counts are given. It is unclear whether the page counts correspond to Bands 1, 3, and possibly another band, or whether the numbering refers to 'Teile' (parts) rather than physical volumes. The volumes array has been populated conservatively: Band 1 (XVI+260 S.), and the two remaining counts assigned to Bands 3 and 4 tentatively, but this assignment is uncertain. Human verification is required. Additionally, the work is Alexander von Humboldt's 'Relation historique du Voyage aux régions équinoxiales du Nouveau Continent', originally in French; the author has been inferred from context ('A. v. Humboldt anerkannte Ausgabe') as the entry does not list an author explicitly.

reisen_262_11_13.verification_notes: The author's name 'RUBRUK' may be a variant spelling of 'RUBRUCK' (Wilhelm von Rubruck / Guillaume de Rubrouck), the 13th-century Flemish Franciscan friar who undertook the journey described. Both spellings appear in German literature, so no correction has been applied. No publication year is given in the entry. The work was originally written in Latin, but no explicit translation indicator is present in this entry; it may be a reworking ('Neu bearbeitet') rather than a direct translation, so is_translation has been left false.

reisen_267_11_13.original_entry:

reisen_267_11_13.verification_notes: The entry states 'In 2 Bänden und einem Supplement und Schlußband', which totals 3 volumes, but the page sequence 'VII, 272, 128; VII, 400; VIII, 472 S.' is ambiguous: the first segment 'VII, 272, 128' may represent two sub-volumes or a single volume with multiple paginations. It is unclear whether volume 1 has pages 272+128 or whether these are separate volumes. The note 'in einem Band' suggests all volumes are bound together. Total volumes set to 3 based on the description. Volume 1 page count left as null due to ambiguity. Additionally, the publisher 'Schröer' in Berlin for this work could not be fully verified; api_concerned flagged for human review.

reisen_240_10_13.original_entry:
PELSAERT, Francois
Der Untergang der 'Batavia' und andere Schiffsjournale und Originalberichte aus der großen Zeit der niederländischen Seefahrt im 17. und 18. Jahrhundert. Mit 56 zeitgenössischen Darstellungen, Faksimiles und Karten. Tübingen Erdmann 1976. 350 S. OLn.m.OU
reisen_240_10_13.verification_notes: François Pelsaert was a Dutch VOC merchant who wrote the journal of the Batavia shipwreck (1629). However, the entry title suggests this is an anthology of multiple ship journals and reports from the 17th and 18th century, which implies Pelsaert may be only one of several authors or contributors, or that this is an edited compilation where Pelsaert is listed as the primary/lead author. The actual editor(s) of this compilation may be missing from the entry. Human verification recommended to confirm whether Pelsaert is correctly listed as sole author or whether an editor should be recorded.

reisen_31_2_13.original_entry:
HERDER, Johann Gottfried
Journal meiner Reise im Jahre 1769. Mit sechsundvierzig perspektivischen Darstellungen von Johannes Lencker d. Ä., Wenzel Jamnitzer und Lorenz Stöer. Nördlingen DELPHI bei Franz Greno 1985. 184 S. OKart.
reisen_31_2_13.verification_notes: The illustrations attributed to Johannes Lencker d. Ä., Wenzel Jamnitzer, and Lorenz Stöer are Renaissance-era artists (16th century), not contemporaries of Herder. These are likely geometric/perspective woodcuts or engravings reproduced in this edition, which appears to be a facsimile or illustrated reprint. This is plausible for a 1985 Delphi/Franz Greno edition, but the connection between Herder's 1769 travel journal and these specific Renaissance perspective illustrations is unusual and may warrant verification.

reisen_33_2_13.original_entry:
HOLUB, Emil
Sieben Jahre in Süd-Afrika. Erlebnisse, Forschungen und Jagden auf meinen Reisen von den Diamantfeldern zum Zambesi (1872 – 1879). Mit 235 Original-Holzschnitten und vier Karten. Wien Hölder 1881. 532 S. O.geprägte illustrierte Leinenbände. Sehr gut erhalten. XVI, 528 S. IX, 532 S. 3 mehrfach gefaltete Karten. Band 2 (rote Ausgabe) doppelt. Karte im ersten Band fehlt.
reisen_33_2_13.verification_notes: The entry lists conflicting page counts: '532 S.' appears as a general figure, then 'XVI, 528 S.' for Band 1 and 'IX, 532 S.' for Band 2. It is unclear which page count belongs to which volume definitively. Band 1 page count assigned as 532 (from general figure and Roman numerals XVI + 528 ≈ 544 total), Band 2 as 532 (IX + 532). Additionally, Band 2 is noted as 'doppelt' (two copies present, red edition), and the map in Band 1 is missing. The entry mentions 4 maps in the subtitle but only 3 folded maps are physically present. multiple_editions set true because Band 2 is described as a 'rote Ausgabe' (red edition), suggesting a variant edition is present alongside the main copy.

Atlas maior. Germaia. Austria & Helvetia. Sämtliche 124 Karten von Germanien, Österreich und der Schweiz, sowie Originaltexte von Joan Blaeu aus seinem Atlas maior von 1665. reisen_95_4_13.verification_notes: Typo in entry: 'Germaia' corrected to 'Germania' in the title field (unambiguous). The two-volume structure lists Germania as pp. 1–215 and Austria & Helvetia as pp. 223–404; the gap between p. 215 and p. 223 suggests these may be two parts of a single bound volume rather than two separate physical volumes, or there may be front matter/index pages not accounted for. Volume page counts are therefore noted as ranges rather than totals. The total pages field is set to 404 (the last page number given). Peter van der Krogt is listed as author of the introduction and texts, treated here as editor/contributor.

reisen_96_4_13.original_entry:

reisen_96_4_13.verification_notes: The entry lists William Bligh as the sole author, but the title references both 'Meuterei auf der Bounty' (Bligh's account) and 'Die Piratenjagd der Fregatte Pandora' (George Hamilton's account). This appears to be a combined edition of two separate works by two different authors. George Hamilton was the surgeon aboard HMS Pandora and wrote his own account. Human verification is recommended to confirm authorship attribution and whether Hamilton should also be listed as an author.

reisen_98_4_13.original_entry:

The editor/translator M.R.C. Fuhrmann-Plemp van Duiveland is credited with editing, translating, and commenting, so the authorship attribution is ambiguous. Additionally, the original language is assumed to be Dutch based on context, but this is not explicitly stated in the entry.

Von 1945 bis zur Gegenwart. Von Winfried Barner, Alexander von Bormann u.a. Herausgegeben von Wilfried Barner. München Beck 1994. 116 S. OLn.m.OU. Verlagsfrisches Ex. Mit einer Widmung des Autors Bormann.
literatur_118_5_12.verification_notes: The entry lists 'Winfried Barner' as author and 'Wilfried Barner' as editor — these may be the same person (Winfried Barner is the known editor of this multi-volume work published by Beck), with 'Wilfried' being a typo. However, since both names appear explicitly in the entry and the distinction cannot be resolved without human judgement, no correction has been applied. Additionally, 116 pages seems unusually low for a comprehensive literary history from 1945 to the present; the actual work is a large volume. This may be a transcription error. Human verification recommended.

literatur_10_1_12.original_entry:
ENGLISCHE GRAMMATIK
Theodor Arnolds Grammatica Anglicana concentrata, oder kurzgefasste Englische Grammatik… Fünfte verbesserte Auflage. Von M.J.B. Rogler. Leipzig, Verlag der Waisenhaus Buchhandlung. 1781. 1. Teil mit 1 Blatt Vorbericht, 2 Vignetten. 314 S. 2. Teil Deutsch-Englisches Wörterbuch 1.Ausgabe 1790. mit 1 Bl. Vorrede, 236 S. Beide Teile in einem Originallederband, abgegriffen. schöne Vorsätze, etwas stockfleckig.
literatur_10_1_12.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under 'ENGLISCHE GRAMMATIK' as a subject heading rather than under the author's name. The work is attributed to 'M.J.B. Rogler' as the revising author ('Von M.J.B. Rogler'), while 'Theodor Arnold' appears to be the original author of the Grammatica Anglicana concentrata. It is unclear whether Rogler is the author or editor/reviser; he has been listed as author since the entry uses 'Von'. The two parts have different publication years (1781 and 1790), which is unusual for a single bound volume — this may indicate the second part was issued separately and later bound together. The publication year of the main record is set to 1781 (Part 1). Human verification of authorship attribution and the relationship between the two parts is recommended.

briefe_103_5_15.verification_notes: The entry describes 8 Teile (parts) distributed across 3 physical Bände (volumes), but the numbering of parts within Band 1 is non-sequential: Teil 1, Teil 2, and Teil 4 are listed under Band 1, while Teil 3 appears under Band 2. Teil 3 (251 S.) precedes Teil 5 in Band 2, suggesting the parts were not bound in strict numerical order. This irregular distribution cannot be resolved without physical inspection of the volumes. No publication year is given. The subtitle states 'sechs Bänden' for the main work and 'zwei Bänden' for the Nachgelassene Schriften, but the physical binding is in three volumes containing eight parts — this structural discrepancy is noted.

briefe_117_5_15.verification_notes: The entry describes a complex multi-volume set published in 7 physical volumes (Bände), but the internal structure groups multiple sub-volumes together (e.g., Band I–III of 'Geschichte meines Lebens' in one physical volume). The mapping of the 12 sub-volumes of 'Geschichte meines Lebens' plus 'Eduard und Elisabeth' (3 sub-volumes), 'Vermischte Schriften/Gesammelte Briefe' (3 sub-volumes), and the picture volume (1 volume) to exactly 7 physical volumes is ambiguous and cannot be cleanly resolved. The translator is named 'Heinz Sauter' in one place and 'Heinz von Sauter' in another; 'Heinz von Sauter' has been used as the canonical form since it appears in the more detailed credit. The entry also covers multiple distinct works by Casanova (Geschichte meines Lebens, Eduard und Elisabeth, Vermischte Schriften, Gesammelte Briefe, and a picture volume), making the main title assignment to 'Geschichte meines Lebens' a simplification. Human verification recommended.

briefe_91_4_15.verification_notes: The volume numbering in the entry is inconsistent and ambiguous. The entry uses both Arabic numerals (1, 2, 3) and Roman numerals (II, III, IV) for what appear to be sub-volumes of Band I, II, III, and IV respectively. Specifically: volumes labeled '1.', '2.', '3.' appear to correspond to Band I.1, I.2, I.3 (Abhandlungen); 'IV 1.2' and 'IV 2' appear to be typos or formatting errors for IV.1.2 and IV.2. The volume numbers assigned in the volumes array are sequential ordinal positions (1–12) for clarity, with the original band designations preserved in the volume_title field. Human verification of the exact band numbering scheme is recommended. Additionally, the standard Suhrkamp Werkausgabe of Benjamin's Gesammelte Schriften has 7 main bands (I–VII) with sub-volumes totaling more than 12 physical books; the entry claims 12 volumes as 'Komplett', which may reflect a specific edition or subset — this should be verified.

Volume 4 combines three separately paginated works (181+157+217 S. = 555 S. total) under one volume entry, which may represent separate physical volumes — flagged as problematic_multi_volume. The Flaubert dates '1821-1857' in the subtitle are Flaubert's birth and death years (1821-1880), but 1857 is the year of publication of Madame Bovary and may be intentional as a scope marker in Sartre's work — flagged api_concerned for verification.

briefe_173_7_15.verification_notes: missing_person: In Band 9–10, the editor of the Dritter Theil of 'Christliche Reden und Homilien' is given as 'Hg. v…' with the name cut off/incomplete. The entry also notes '11 OLn.' which has been interpreted as 11 volumes in Originalleinen binding, but the work spans 22 Bände (Bände 1–22), so it is unclear whether 11 refers to 11 physical volumes (each binding containing two Bände) or 11 copies — most likely 11 physical volumes each containing two Bände, which is consistent with the pairing structure throughout. api_concerned: The entry pairs Bände consistently (1–2, 3–4, etc.) but the content within each pair is sometimes complex and the assignment of specific texts to specific volume numbers is partly inferential. Band 7 has no page count for the 'Älteste Urkunde' Vierter Theil section. The 'copies' field has been set to 11 reflecting the 11 physical volumes noted, but this may require human verification. problematic_multi_volume: The volume structure is complex — multiple works are bound together within single Bände pairs, page counts are sometimes missing (Band 7–8 for 'Älteste Urkunde' Vierter Theil), and the assignment of content to individual volume numbers within pairs is ambiguous in several cases.

briefe_36_2_15.original_entry:

briefe_36_2_15.verification_notes: The entry describes a two-part/two-volume work but the structure is ambiguous: the first part has 'XXXII, 159 SS.' and the second part ('II. Theil') has '138, 34 gez. S.' — it is unclear whether these are two physical volumes or two parts within one volume. Volume 1 page count is given as XXXII + 159 pages but no numeric integer can be cleanly assigned. Parsed as two volumes with page details in notes. Additionally, Ewald Christian von Kleist (1715–1759) was a German poet and friend of Lessing; the parenthetical '(Freund Lessings)' confirms this identification. However, a 'Sämtliche Werke' published in Wien by Bauer in 1816 should be verifiable — the cataloger's note is consistent with known biographical facts. The 'Kpf.' abbreviation (Kupferstich, i.e., copper engraving) is noted in illustrations.

The backtick in 'Brand`s' has been interpreted as an apostrophe and corrected to 'Brand's' (corrected_by_api). The volume structure uses non-standard numbering (I/1.1, I/1.2) which cannot be mapped to simple integers; volume_number left null and noted in the notes field. problematic_multi_volume set true due to the unusual interleaving of bibliographic data within the volume descriptions.

briefe_62_3_15.verification_notes: The entry describes 'Vollständig in drei Bänden' (complete in three volumes), but the structure is ambiguous: Band 1 and Band 2 are clearly labeled, while 'Gulliver's Reisen' (421 S.) appears to be a third volume but is not explicitly labeled as 'Dritter Band'. Additionally, the note 'Drei Teile in einem Band' suggests all three parts may be physically bound together in a single volume, which contradicts the three-volume description. It is unclear whether this is a three-volume set bound as one, or a different arrangement. The volume structure has been parsed as best as possible given the ambiguity. The identity of 'Justizminister Foregger' (likely Richard Foregger, Austrian Minister of Justice) is noted but not verified.

briefe_1_1_15.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: 'HERDER, Johann Gottried' corrected to 'Johann Gottfried' (clear typo). 'SCHOPENHAUER, Arthur von' — Schopenhauer did not use the noble particle 'von'; flagged under api_concerned but name left as written in entry. multiple_editions: The set as described is the Harenberg Kommunikation 1982 Sonderausgabe (56 volumes, missing 4 Goethe volumes); the missing Goethe volumes are supplemented by a separate 10-volume Goethe Gesamtausgabe published by Weltbild Bücherdienst, Stuttgart (year not stated). These are two distinct publications combined into one physical collection. The Harenberg edition is treated as the main record; the Weltbild Goethe volumes are noted as supplementary. problematic_multi_volume: The edition was originally planned as 60 volumes; 4 Goethe volumes (positions 9–12) are absent from the Harenberg set, making the actual Harenberg count 56. The supplementary Weltbild Goethe set has 10 volumes, making the combined total 66 volumes, but these are not uniform editions. Volume numbers within each author's sub-set are not individually numbered in the entry, making precise volume-number assignment impossible. The Weltbild Goethe Bd. 10 (Autobiographische Schriften Dritter Teil) has no page count given. api_concerned: 'SCHOPENHAUER, Arthur von' — Schopenhauer is not known to have used 'von'; this may be a cataloging error. Also, the Bibliothek Deutscher Klassiker is typically associated with Hanser Verlag (copyright 1977 noted in entry) and the Harenberg Sonderausgabe 1982 is a known reprint; the editor Karl Schlechta is primarily known for his Nietzsche edition, not as general editor of this entire series — this warrants verification.

briefe_4_1_15.verification_notes: This entry catalogues a large multi-series collected works of Brecht across five distinct series (Stücke, Gedichte, Schriften zur Literatur und Kunst, Prosa, Schriften zum Theater), totalling 35 volumes. The entry header states 'STÜCKE Band I–X' but then lists 13 Stücke volumes; the Gedichte section is announced as 'Band I–IX' but lists 9 volumes (some with duplicate date ranges, e.g. two volumes of 'Gedichte 1934–1941' and two volumes of 'Nachträge'). The 'V.' before 'Me-Ti' in the Prosa section is unclear — possibly a volume number or a stray character. Volume numbering across series has been assigned sequentially (1–35) for structural clarity, but the original entry does not provide a unified numbering across all series. Years are missing for several Stücke aus dem Exil volumes (Bände 7–10). The entry is flagged as problematic_multi_volume due to these ambiguities. api_concerned is set because the two 'Gedichte 1934–1941' volumes and two 'Nachträge' volumes with different page counts may represent distinct volumes or duplicate entries requiring verification.

briefe_201_9_15.verification_notes: MULTIPLE_EDITIONS: This entry is an extremely complex composite record combining two distinct series — 'Gesammelte Schriften' (Schocken Books, New York, Bde. III–V) and 'Gesammelte Werke' (S. Fischer Verlag, Lizenzausgaben) — with multiple editions and copy counts for individual titles within each series. The entry cannot be cleanly reduced to a single bibliographic record. The first-described edition (Schocken Books, Bd. III, 1946) is used as the main record's publisher/place/year. All individual volumes and their copy counts are documented in the volumes array as fully as possible. PROBLEMATIC_MULTI_VOLUME: The total of 19 volumes stated at the end cannot be precisely reconciled with the individual copy counts listed (Erzählungen: 4 Ex., Der Prozess S. Fischer: 2 Ex., Das Schloss S. Fischer: 5 Ex., Hochzeitsvorbereitungen: 1 Ex., Tagebücher: 2 Ex., Briefe: 2 Ex., Briefe an Milena: 2 Ex., plus 3 Schocken volumes = 19 total — this does add up, but the Schocken Bd. V has no year and the Tagebücher 13. Tausend copy count is ambiguous). Volume numbers for the S. Fischer 'Gesammelte Werke' volumes are not stated in the entry. API_CONCERNED: The entry conflates two separate series under one KAFKA heading; a cataloger should verify whether these should be split into separate records. The Tagebücher entry has an ambiguous line break ('OLn. 2.Ex. S. Fischer Verlag. 13. Tausend 734 S. || OLn.m.OU') making it unclear whether the second copy has OU or not.

judentum_60_3_9.original_entry:
DIE SCHRIFT
Verdeutscht von Martin Buber gemeinsam mit Franz Rosenzweig. Neubearbeitete Ausgabe in vier Bänden. Heidelberg Schneider. 10. verbesserte Auflage der neubearbeiteten Ausgabe von 1954. Mit einer Beilage Buber: zu einer neuen Verdeutschung der Schrift (46 Seiten) und einem Beilageblatt. 1981.
Die fünf Bücher der Weisung. 582 S.
Bücher der Geschichte. 521 S.
Bücher der Kündigung. 782 S.
Die Schriftwerke. Verdeutscht von Martin Buber. 5. Verbesserte Auflage der neubearbeiteten Ausgabe von 1962. Mit einer Beilage Buber: Zur Verdeutschung des letzten Bandes der Schrift (26 Seiten) 1980. 695 S.
Alle Bände OLn. m. OU in einem O. Schuber. Neuwertig.
judentum_60_3_9.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Volume 4 (Die Schriftwerke) has a different edition statement (5. Verbesserte Auflage der neubearbeiteten Ausgabe von 1962) and a different year (1980) compared to volumes 1–3 (10. Auflage, 1981). The top-level edition and year reflect volumes 1–3 as the primary described set. Volume 4's differing edition and year are noted in the volumes array. problematic_multi_volume: The set is described as 'Neubearbeitete Ausgabe in vier Bänden' but volume 4 carries a distinct edition history and year, making the overall volume structure partially inconsistent. The entry also mentions a 'Beilageblatt' and a separate Beilage for volume 4; these are noted but not separately structured.

judentum_61_3_9.original_entry:
DIE SCHRIFT
Verdeutscht von Martin Buber gemeinsam mit Franz Rosenzweig
Zusätzlich zur Gesamtausgabe:
Band 1: Die fünf Bücher der Weisung. Mit einer Beilage: Martin Buber: Zu einer neuen Verdeutschung der Schrift (46 Seiten). Darmstadt WBG 1984. 570 S. OPbd.
2. Ex. Einmalige Sonderausgabe in der Reihe 'Die Bücher der Neuzehn' 1968. Mit einer Beilage. 570, 44 S. OLn.
Band 2: Bücher der Geschichte. 8. Auflage der neubearbeiteten Ausgabe von 1955. Heidelberg Lambert Schneider 1985. 521 S. OLn.m.OU.
Band 3: Bücher der Kündigung. 8. Auflage der neubearbeiteten Ausgabe von 1958. Heidelberg Lambert Schneider 1985. 782 S. OLn.m.OU
Band 4: Das Buch der Preisungen ('Die Schriftwerke') verdeutscht von Martin Buber. 9. Durchgesehene Auflage der neubearbeiteten Ausgabe von 1958. Mit einer Beilage: Martin Buber: Zur Verdeutschung der Preisungen (11 Seiten). Heidelberg Lambert Schneider 1982. 209 S. OLn.m.OU.
judentum_61_3_9.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Band 1 has a second copy noted as '2. Ex.' which is a different edition (Einmalige Sonderausgabe in der Reihe 'Die Bücher der Neuzehn' 1968, OLn., 570+44 S.) alongside the main 1984 WBG edition. The main record uses the first-described edition (Band 1, Darmstadt WBG 1984) for top-level fields. problematic_multi_volume: The entry is introduced as 'Zusätzlich zur Gesamtausgabe', implying a separate Gesamtausgabe also exists but is not described here. The four volumes listed have different publishers (WBG Darmstadt for Band 1; Lambert Schneider Heidelberg for Bands 2–4) and different years, making a single top-level publisher/place/year ambiguous. Top-level fields reflect Band 1 as the first-described volume. The original language is inferred as Hebrew given the nature of the work (the Hebrew Bible). The translators Buber and Rosenzweig are listed as contributors since the work is a translation of a sacred text with no single human author.

judentum_64_3_9.original_entry:
FESTGEBETE DER ISRAELITEN
Nach der gottesdienstlichen Ordnung im israelitischen Bethause zu Wien und in mehreren anderen Gemeinden. Mit einer deutschen Übersetzung von J. N. Mannheimer. Prediger in Wien. Erster Band. judentum_64_3_9.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: Publication year 1926 is likely a typo for 1826 or another 19th-century date, as Isaac Noah Mannheimer (1793–1865) was the preacher in Vienna in the early-to-mid 19th century and could not have produced a translation published in 1926. However, since later editions of liturgical works can be reprinted posthumously and the 'Siebente Ausgabe' could plausibly be a 20th-century reprint, the year 1926 has been retained without correction. api_concerned: The publication year 1926 is suspicious given that J. N. Mannheimer (Isaac Noah Mannheimer) died in 1865; this may be a posthumous reprint edition, but the year should be verified. problematic_multi_volume: Only 'Erster Band' is described; total number of volumes is unknown and no further volumes are listed in the entry.

judentum_66_3_9.verification_notes: The co-author's name is given as 'SILBERSTEIN, Neil A.' but the correct name is 'SILBERMAN, Neil Asher'. This is a clear typo/error in the entry. Corrected to 'SILBERMAN, Neil A.' — wait: the entry reads 'SILBERSTEIN', but the actual author is Neil Asher Silberman. However, since 'Silberstein' vs 'Silberman' could be considered ambiguous (two different surnames), this correction is flagged. Actually, given that the book 'Keine Posaunen vor Jericho' is definitively co-authored by Israel Finkelstein and Neil Asher Silberman, and 'SILBERSTEIN' is not a known author of this work, this is an unambiguous typo. Corrected display_name to 'SILBERMAN, Neil A.' in the authors array.

judentum_67_3_9.original_entry:

De Bello Judaico. Der jüdische Krieg. Griechisch und Deutsch. Herausgegeben und mit einer Einleitung sowie mit Anmerkungen versehen von Otto Michel und Otto Bauernfeind. München Kösel
Band I: Buch I-III. Zweite , überprüfte Auflage 1962. Darmstadt. Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft 1959. XXXVI, 464 S.
Band II, 1: Buch IV-V. Darmstadt. Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft 1963. XII, 274 S.
Band II, 2: Buch VI-VII. Darmstadt Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft 1969. 287 S.
Band III: Ergänzungen und Register. Mit einer Karte von Palästina. Darmstadt Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft 1969. XXVII, 149 S.
Mit Anstreichungen. OLn. mit goldgeprägten Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten.
judentum_67_3_9.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Band I is described with two editions — the original 1959 Darmstadt/Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft edition and a 'Zweite, überprüfte Auflage 1962'; it is unclear which physical copy is held. The entry also mentions 'München Kösel' as a publisher/place pair at the top level, which may refer to a separate or earlier edition not further described. The top-level publisher and place have been set to Darmstadt / Wissenschaftliche Buchgesellschaft as the dominant series publisher. api_concerned: The mention of 'München Kösel' at the header level is ambiguous — it may refer to an earlier Kösel-Verlag edition of the work, but no further details are given; human verification is needed. problematic_multi_volume: Band II is split into two sub-volumes (II,1 and II,2), making the total volume count and numbering ambiguous. Pages for volumes with Roman+Arabic numerals have been summed (e.g., XXXVI+464=500, XII+274=286, XXVII+149=176); these are approximate totals. Volume structure has been parsed as clearly as possible given the available data.

judentum_43_2_9.original_entry:
BUBER, Martin
Reden und Gleichnisse des Tschuang-Tse. Deutsche Auswahl von Martin Buber. Vierte Auflage. Einbandzeichnung von E. R. Weiß. Leipzig Insel 1921. 129 S. Buntes OLn. Sehr gut erhalten. Mit einem schönen Ex Libris von Helene Goldstern (Siehe Wiki).
judentum_43_2_9.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under JUDENTUM and attributed to Martin Buber, but the work is a German selection/translation of texts by the Chinese Taoist philosopher Zhuangzi (Tschuang-Tse). Buber is the translator/editor, not the original author. This is consistent with Buber's known work. The topic classification under JUDENTUM is unusual for a Taoist text, though Buber's engagement with Hasidism and mysticism may explain the placement. The Ex Libris of Helene Goldstern is noted with a reference to Wikipedia, suggesting provenance interest; no correction applied.

Format 'OHldr.' expanded as Originalhalbleder, though this abbreviation is less standard than 'OHLdr' — retained as written.

bibliophile_27_2_10.original_entry:

Malerische Ansichten von Nürnberg nach der Natur gezeichnet und in Stahl gestochen v… mit kurzem erläuternden Texte von Ernst Lösch. Nürnberg Bauer und Raspe ca. 1840. 19 S. The text by Ernst Lösch is clearly stated. missing_person flag set due to the incomplete name following 'v…'. Publication year given as 'ca. 1840'; recorded as 1840. J. Poppel was a known German engraver active in this period, consistent with the entry.

Kurz', suggesting the author POPPEL, J. is referenced but the ellipsis in the subtitle implies the full co-creator credit is incomplete. The entry reads 'herausgegeben von… und M. Kurz', where the ellipsis stands in place of Poppel's name — this is a missing_person indicator in the descriptive text, though Poppel is identified as the main entry author.

bibliophile_30_2_10.original_entry:
PRAHA
Kralovske Hlavni mesto Praha. Vydano nakladem Obce Prazske L. P. 1911. 30 ganzs. mont. Taf. mit Seidenschutz von Prager Künstlern aus der berühmten Werkstatt von Jan Stenc. Schönes, zweifarbiges Jugendstilwerk in Tschechisch, Deutsch, Französisch und Englisch. Bunter O. Einband OHLn. Sehr gut erhalten 50.-
bibliophile_30_2_10.verification_notes: The entry describes a multilingual (Czech, German, French, English) Jugendstil art publication of Prague issued by the municipality ('Nakladem Obce Prazske'). 'L. P. 1911' likely stands for 'Léta Páně 1911' (Year of the Lord 1911), a traditional Czech dating formula. No author or editor is named; this appears to be a corporate/institutional publication. The format notation 'Bunter O. Einband OHLn.' is slightly ambiguous — it may indicate a decorative original half-linen binding with a colourful cover; flagged for human verification of the binding description.

bibliophile_36_2_10.original_entry:
SCHEIBLE, J.(ohann)
Volksprediger, Moralisten und frommer Unsinn.
Sebastian Brandt`s Narrenschiff mit Geiler`s von Kaisersberg Predigten darüber und Thomas Murner`s Schelmenzunft, vollständig nach den alten Drucken und ihren bildlichen Darstellun-gen. Mit vielen Abbildungen auf 72 Tafeln. Stuttgart, Verlag des Herausgebers 1845. XVI, 908 S. Mit 4 Porträts von Abraham a Cancta Clara, Sebastian Brandt, Geiler von Kaisersberg und Thomas Murner in Stahlstich, etwa 160 Abbildungen auf Tafeln und einigen gef. Tafeln. OHln. Einband berieben, sonst makelloser Band.
bibliophile_36_2_10.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: 'Darstellun-gen' is a hyphenation artifact from line-breaking in the original entry; corrected to 'Darstellungen'. 'gef. Tafeln' expanded to 'gefalteten Tafeln'. api_concerned: The entry lists 'Abraham a Cancta Clara' — the correct spelling of the Baroque preacher's name is 'Abraham a Sancta Clara'; 'Cancta' appears to be a typo for 'Sancta', but since this appears in a descriptive/illustrative note rather than a catalogued name field, it has been left as written in the illustrations field. The page count XVI is a Roman-numeral preface pagination; only the main pagination 908 is recorded in the pages field.

Die Von Geldern Haggadah und Heinrich Heines 'Der Rabbi von Bacherach' herausgegeben von…Mit Beiträgen von Gert Kaiser, Emile G.L.Schrijver, Edward von Voolen, Falk Wiesemann und Bernd Witte. Wien Brandstätter 1997. 80 S. und 52 farbige Taf. OLn.m. OU. Gr.-4°. Verlagsfrisch € öä:-.

bibliophile_40_2_10.original_entry:
SHAKESPEARE IN DEUTSCHER SPRACHE
II. Band: Romeo und Julia. Othello. Der Kaufmann von Venedig. Plan und Ausschmückung von Melchior Lechter, unter dessen Leitung dieses Buch bei Otto von Holten hergestellt wurde. Berlin Bondi 1909. Buntes OLn. mit goldschmückten Titel und Rücken, 3 fachem Schnitt und Lesebändchen. Herausgegeben zum Teil neu übersetzt von Friedrich Gundolf. Makelloser Band.
bibliophile_40_2_10.verification_notes: The entry describes only Band II of a multi-volume work; total volume count is unknown from this entry alone. Friedrich Gundolf is listed both as editor ('Herausgegeben') and partial new translator ('zum Teil neu übersetzt'), so he appears in both roles. Melchior Lechter is credited for the artistic design ('Plan und Ausschmückung'). The printer Otto von Holten is mentioned but is not the publisher; publisher is Georg Bondi, Berlin. The original author is William Shakespeare (English). api_concerned is set because the entry does not name Shakespeare explicitly as author, and the series editor/translator role of Gundolf is ambiguous in scope.

bibliophile_41_2_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_41_2_10.verification_notes: Publication year given as 'ca. 1892' — recorded as 1892 with the approximation noted here. Typo 'Leinenban' corrected to 'Leinenband'. '3 facher' normalized to '3facher'. The work features images (Bilder) by Spitzer and poems (Gedichten) by Rauchenegger; both listed as authors reflecting their creative contributions.

bibliophile_47_2_10.original_entry:
THACKERAY, W.M.
Jahrmarkt der Eitelkeit. Ein Roman ohne Helden. Aus dem Engl. von Theresia Mutzenbecher, mit den Illustrationen des Autors zu der Erstausgabe von 1848. München Winkler 1958. 916 S. OLn .m. OU und Lesebändchen u. Kassette. Dünndruck Verlagsfrisch 2 Exemplare. 3. Exemplar gleichen Inhalts in Leder, Lesebändchen und Kassette Verlagsfrisch
bibliophile_47_2_10.verification_notes: Three copies described: 2 copies in Originalleinen mit Originalumschlag, Lesebändchen und Kassette (Dünndruck, Verlagsfrisch); 1 copy of the same content in Leder with Lesebändchen und Kassette (Verlagsfrisch). The first two copies form the main record; the third copy differs in binding (Leder). multiple_editions set true due to differing binding variants among the copies. Main record reflects the first-described format (OLn.m.OU). The Dünndruck (thin-paper printing) note applies to the first two copies.

bibliophile_48_2_10.original_entry:
THOMAS VON KEMPEN
Vier Bücher von der Nachfolge Christi. Görres Übersetzung. Mit Original Zeichnungen von Joseph, Ritter von Führich. In Holzschnitt ausgeführt von Kaspar Oertel. Volksausgabe. Leipzig Dürr 1884. 288 S. Gold- und blindgeprägtes OLn., roter Ganzschnitt und florale Vorsätze. Sehr gut erhalten. EA. Mit einer vierzeiligen Widmung von Gisela Baronin Berger.
bibliophile_48_2_10.verification_notes: The translator is listed only as 'Görres' without a given name. This likely refers to Joseph Görres (1776–1848), the German Catholic writer and publicist, but the given name is not stated in the entry, so missing_person is set true. The original language of 'De Imitatione Christi' is Latin, but this is not explicitly stated in the entry and has not been filled in to avoid guessing.

bibliophile_49_2_10.original_entry:
TRAITE GENERAL DES CHASSES
A courre et a tir; contenant …Paris Audot 1822 – 1823. In zwei Bänden. Textband mit XIII, 311 S. Bildband mit 35 ganzseitigen Lithographien und 12 S. Noten. Halbpergament der Zeit in zwei Buchbinderbänden mit roter Rückenprägung, marmoriertem Schnitt und Vorsätzen. Nur erster von zwei Bänden. Sehr gut erhalten.
bibliophile_49_2_10.verification_notes: missing_person: No author or editor is named for this work. api_concerned: The entry states 'Nur erster von zwei Bänden', meaning only the first (Textband) is physically present; the second (Bildband with lithographs) is not held. The publication spans 1822–1823. The author of this French hunting treatise is not identified in the entry and could not be unambiguously determined. problematic_multi_volume: The entry describes two volumes (Textband and Bildband) but notes only the first is present; the volume structure and page counts for the Bildband are unclear (only illustration and note counts given, no page total for the volume as a whole).

bibliophile_50_3_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_50_3_10.verification_notes: Copy is numbered 184 of 600 (limited edition). The '184/600 Ex.' notation indicates this is a numbered copy from a limited print run of 600; copies field set to 1.

bibliophile_51_3_10.original_entry:
TSCHUDI, Friedrich von
Das Thierleben der Alpenwelt. Naturansichten aus dem schweizerischen Gebirge. Zweite, verbesserte Auflage. Mit 24 Abbildungen von E. Rittmeyer und W. Georgy. Leipzig Weber 1854. XVI, 612 S.
