bibliophile_52_3_10.original_entry:
ULENHART, Niclaus
Sonderlich curieuse Historia von Isaac Winkelfelder und Jobst von der Schneidt: Wie es diesen Beyden Gesellen/ in der Stadt Prag Ergangen…Faksimile Ausgabe von 1923 in 400 Ex. Dieses mit der Nr. 15 von der Gesellschaft deutscher Bücherfreunde in Böhmen. Mit 7 (3 gef.) Abb. (8) 144 S. OKart.
bibliophile_52_3_10.verification_notes: The entry states 'Faksimile Ausgabe von 1923' but does not specify the original publication year of the work. The original work by Ulenhart dates to the early 17th century (c. 1617). The place of publication for the 1923 facsimile edition is not stated in the entry. The page count '(8) 144 S.' suggests 8 preliminary pages plus 144 main pages; only 144 has been recorded. The format 'OKart.' has been expanded as 'Originalkarton' though it could also stand for 'Originalkartonage' — flagged for verification.

WILDE, O.
Die Ballade von Zuchthause zu Reading. Von C.3. 3. In memoriam…. Nach dem Englischen von Wilhelm Schölermann. Leipzig Insel 1905. 43 S. 3. Aufl. OLn. m. Lesebändchen Sehr gut erhalten.
bibliophile_56_3_10.verification_notes: The subtitle 'Von C.3. 3. In memoriam…' is ambiguous. Oscar Wilde's 'The Ballad of Reading Gaol' was originally published under the pseudonym 'C.3.3.' (his prison cell number). The entry appears to conflate this pseudonym with a subtitle or dedication phrase. The exact rendering 'Von C.3. 3. In memoriam….' may be a cataloging artifact or partial transcription error; human verification is recommended.

bibliophile_71_3_10.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry reads 'gesammelt von…' with an ellipsis and no following name, indicating the collector/editor name is absent. api_concerned: Ernst Benz is known as a theologian and historian of religion; his authorship or editorship of a collection titled 'Alte deutsche Legenden' published by Diederichs in 1910 cannot be confirmed with certainty — the listed person may be the subject heading rather than the author/editor. bibliophile_180_8_10.original_entry:
RABELAIS, Francois
Meister Franz Rabelais der Arzeney Doctoren Gargantua und Pantagruel aus dem Französischen verdeutscht durch Gottlob Regis. München Georg Müller 1906.
1. Erstes und zweites Buch mit einem Bildnis des Autors und zwei Beilagen. 70, 315 SS
2. Drittes, viertes und fünftes Buch mit einer Beilage. 322 – 915 S.
Original Pergamentbände mit reichlicher Einbandverzierung und Lesebändchen. Im ersten Band Einband im unteren Teil geplatzt, zweiter Band mit geringen Schäden am Einband. Erschienen in 555 Ex., dieses Exemplar mit der Nr. 484.
bibliophile_180_8_10.verification_notes: Volume 1 has a dual page count '70, 315 SS' — likely 70 pages of prefatory material and 315 pages of main text; only the main page count (315) is stored in pages. Volume 2 uses a continuous pagination (322–915), so no standalone page count can be cleanly assigned; pages left null for that volume. The translator's name 'Gottlob Regis' is well attested as the German translator of Rabelais, so no correction needed.

Total volume count and first volume details are unknown. The page range 226–412 suggests this is a continuation, but no top-level page count can be assigned. Additionally, the roles of Führich (illustrator) and Deveri (text author/Pfarrer) are clear but neither is a conventional author or editor in the strict sense; they are listed as contributors. api_concerned flagged because it is unclear whether this is a standalone second volume or part of a larger set not otherwise catalogued.

bibliophile_7_1_10.original_entry:
DANTE DEUTSCH VON RUDOLF BORCHARDT
Drei Teile in einem Band. München Bremer Presse und Berlin Rowohlt 1923 bis 1930. 522 S. OLn. mit goldgeprägtem Rückentitel. Tadellose ErhaltungDANTE DEUTSCH …
Fegefeuer… wie oben. S. 164-312. OPbd. mit Deckel- und Rückenschild. Neuwertig.
bibliophile_7_1_10.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes two copies/editions of the same work. The first is the complete three-part volume (München, Bremer Presse und Berlin Rowohlt, 1923–1930, 522 S., OLn., tadellose Erhaltung). The second is a separate copy of the 'Fegefeuer' (Purgatorio) section only (S. 164–312, OPbd. mit Deckel- und Rückenschild, neuwertig), described as 'wie oben' (same publisher/year). The main record reflects the first-described complete volume. api_concerned: The publication year range '1923 bis 1930' spans multiple years; the three parts of Borchardt's Dante translation were indeed published separately over this period by Bremer Presse. The dual publisher listing 'München Bremer Presse und Berlin Rowohlt' is unusual and may reflect different publishers for different parts. Human verification of the exact publication details is recommended. Rudolf Borchardt is both the translator and the named author in the title.

GOETHE, Johann Wolfgang
Der Zauberflöte zweiter Teil. Fragment. Textgestaltung nach der bei Cotta erschienenen Jubiläumsausgabe. Jahresgabe der Wiener Bibliophilen Gesellschaft im Goethejahr 1949. Gedruckt in 200 Exemplaren. Mit einem Beitrag Goethe und Wien und das "Zauberflöte"-Fragment des Dichters von Michael Maria Rabenlechner. Wien 1949. 58 S. Mit einem kleinen Eignerstempel auf innerem Einband. OPbd. in Seidenpapier und Schuber, dieser in schlechtem Zustand. Sonst sehr gutes Exemplar.

bibliophile_12_1_10.original_entry:
GOETHE, Johann Wolfgang von
Das römische Carneval. Mit 20 farbigen ganzs. Abb. v. Georg Schütz, handkoloriert von Georg Schauer. Nach dem ersten Druck von Johann Friedrich Unger von 1789. Leipzig Insel (1965). 70 S. OPbd. m. OSchuber. NeuwertigDas römische Carneval. Facsimile und Kommentarband 'Untadeliche Schönheit'. Rudolfstadt Hain 1993. OHLdr. 69 S. und 20 Farbtaf. Kommentarband OLn. 79 S.Das römische Carneval. München Recht-Verlag (Bruckmann) ca. 1922. Mit einem Titelkupfer und 20 handkolorierten Farbtaf. 1000 num. Ex. mit der Nr. 464. OPbd. Einband berieben, sonst gutes Ex.
bibliophile_12_1_10.verification_notes: Three editions of the same work are described in sequence without clear separators. The first-described edition (Leipzig, Insel, 1965) is used as the main record. Second edition: Facsimile und Kommentarband 'Untadeliche Schönheit', Rudolfstadt, Hain, 1993, OHLdr., 69 S. und 20 Farbtaf., Kommentarband OLn., 79 S. Third edition: München, Recht-Verlag (Bruckmann), ca. 1922, mit einem Titelkupfer und 20 handkolorierten Farbtafeln, 1000 nummerierte Exemplare mit der Nr. 464, OPbd., Einband berieben, sonst gutes Ex.

bibliophile_13_1_10.original_entry:
GRUBER, Robert
Schopenhauers Geliebte in Berlin. Urkundliches, gesammelt von…Mit vier Bildnissen und mit Nachbildungen von Briefen und Urkunden. Wien Österreichische Druck- und Verlags-gesellschaft 1934. OHLn. m. interessantem Exlibris. Sehr gut erhalten.
bibliophile_13_1_10.verification_notes: The subtitle contains 'Urkundliches, gesammelt von…' with an ellipsis indicating a collector/editor name that is missing from the entry. The author GRUBER, Robert may be the compiler, but the trailing ellipsis suggests a name was omitted. missing_person flag set accordingly.

bibliophile_14_1_10.original_entry:
GÜTERSLOH, Paris
Kain und Abel. Eine Legende. Wien Jugend und Volk 1969. 73 S. Einmalige num. Auflage von 999 und L Exemplaren mit der Nr. 139. Mit ganzseitigen Illustrationen des Autors. OHPgt. Schuber. 4°. Verlagsfrisch.

bibliophile_15_1_10.original_entry:
HAMERLING, Robert
Der König von Sion. Epische Dichtung in zehn Gesängen. Illustriert von Adalbert von Rößler und Hermann Dietrichs. Hamburg Richter o.J.(1886) 355 S. Mit zahlreichen z. T. ganzseitigen Illustrationen in Holzstich. Ein prächtiger Folio-Band aus der Gründerzeit. Grüner Leinenband, dreifacher Goldschnitt, Blindprägung mit viel Gold, Vorsätze mit Stoffmuster, Lesebändchen, OPbd. mit O.Kartonschuber. Makellose Erhaltung.

bibliophile_16_1_10.original_entry:
HARTMANN VON AUE
Der arme Heinrich. Besorgt von Rudolf Borchardt. München Bremer Presse 1925. 88 S. Titel und Initialen von Anna Simons. PD
bibliophile_16_1_10.verification_notes: Format abbreviation 'PD' does not match any standard RAK format pattern (OLn, OPbd, HLn, Pp, OBrosch, etc.). It may stand for 'Pergamentdruck', 'Pappdeckel', or another binding type specific to the Bremer Presse edition. Human verification of the format field is recommended. Additionally, 'Besorgt von' (prepared/edited by) Rudolf Borchardt is treated as editor; Anna Simons is listed as contributor for the title design and initials.

bibliophile_17_1_10.original_entry:
HAUPTMANN, Gerhart
Das Hirtenlied. Ein Fragment. Mit 17 Holzschnitten von Ludwig von Hofmann. Nach der Liebhaberausgabe, die O.v.Holten als 3. Buch in der für ihn von Marcus Behmer gezeichneten Schrift gedruckt. Dresden Reissner 1924. 48 S. OLn. Sehr gut erhalten.

bibliophile_18_1_10.original_entry:
HEINE, Heinrich
Aus den Memoiren des Herrn von Schnabelewopski. Mit Zeichnungen von Julius Pascin. Berlin Cassirer 1920. Zweite Auflage. 94 S. Die Lithographien dieser Ausgabe sind photographisch verkleinert. OHLn. Neuwertig.

bibliophile_19_1_10.original_entry:
JOYCE, James
Finnegans Wehg. Kainnäh ÜbelSätZung des Werkeß fun Schämes Scheuß von Dieter H. Stündel. Verlag Jürgen Häusser. 1.Auflage 1993. 628 S. Originalleinenband mit Transparentfolie, darin Bildnis von Joyce. Zweisprachig englisch deutsch. In Originalpappkassette . Gr. -4°.Verlagsfrischer Folio-Band
bibliophile_19_1_10.verification_notes: The subtitle 'Kainnäh ÜbelSätZung des Werkeß fun Schämes Scheuß' is an intentional wordplay/pun title (a German homophonic translation of 'Finnegans Wake' by James Joyce, translated by Dieter H. Stündel). The unusual capitalization and spelling are deliberate features of this well-known bibliophile edition. The work is bilingual (English-German). Place of publication not stated in entry (publisher was based in Darmstadt). Format noted as Gr.-4° (Großquart/Folio).

bibliophile_20_1_10.original_entry:
LASKER-SCHÜLER, Else
Hebräische Balladen in der Handschrift von … Herausgegeben und mit einem Nachwort von Norbert Oellers. Frankfurt Jüdischer Verlag im Suhrkamp Verlag 2000. 60 S. OPbd. mit Transparentfolie. Neuwertig. €
bibliophile_20_1_10.verification_notes: The title contains 'in der Handschrift von …' with an ellipsis, suggesting a person's name is missing — likely the name of the person whose handwriting is featured. This is flagged as missing_person.

bibliophile_21_1_10.original_entry:
MADAME GUILLOTINE
Revolutionsgeschichten. Herausgegeben von Curt Moreck. Mit acht Bildbeigaben von Wilhelm Thöny. München Georg Müller 1919. 1.-4.Tausend. 397 S. Auflage von 4000 Exemplaren. Umschlagzeichnung von W. Thöny. OPbd. mit farb. Deckeltitel. Rücken etwas berieben, sonst ein sehr schöner Band

bibliophile_22_1_10.original_entry:
MALERISCHE UND ROMANTISCHE DEUTSCHLAND
Die Veduten des Reisewerks aus den Jahren 1836 – 1841. Ein Reprint für die Bibliophilen Taschenbücher Mit einem Nachwort von Marianne Bernhard. Harenberg. Mit ca. 410 Abb. auf 428 S. Bibliophile Ausgabe in Leder. Mit einem eingesetzten Medaillon mit Metallrahmen. Lesebändchen. Schöner makelloser Band.
bibliophile_22_1_10.verification_notes: No place of publication or year is given. The original work 'Das malerische und romantische Deutschland' was a multi-volume series published by Georg Wigand (Leipzig) in the 1830s–1840s; this is a reprint by Harenberg but no reprint year is stated. The author of the original work is not named in the entry — the series had multiple contributors. Marianne Bernhard is credited only with the afterword (Nachwort). Human verification recommended to confirm publication year and whether an author or editor should be listed.

bibliophile_23_1_10.original_entry:
MAUTNER, Stephan
Farbige Stunden. Mit Bildbeigaben nach Aquarellen, Bleistift- und Federzeichnungen des Verfassers. Wien Verlag der Wiener Graphischen Werkstätte 1921. 143 S. Eines der 1000 nummerierten und vom Autor eigenhändig signierten Ex. mit der Nr. 3. Mit einem Ex Libris von Egon Wellesz. OHLn. mit einem kleinen Einriss am Rücken. Schönes Ex.

bibliophile_24_1_10.original_entry:
MÜLLER, Karl
Praktische Pflanzenkunde für Handel, Gewerbe und Hauswirtschaft. Mit 140 Abbildungen aus 24 fein kolorierten Tafeln und begleitendem Text. Zweite Auflage. Regensburg Stahl 1900. 591 S. OLn.

bibliophile_225_10_10.original_entry:
ZEICHEN DER LIEBE
Wie man lieben und die Liebe dichten soll. Ein indisches Lehrbuch für Liebende und Dichter Stuttgart Hatje 1949. A. d. Sanskrit und einem Nachwort von Heinrich Zimmer. 3. und 4. Tausend Mit sechs Tafeln in Lichtdruck 115 S. OPbd. in Schuber. Neuwertig.

bibliophile_226_10_10.original_entry:
ZIEGLER, Charlotte
Das Stundenbuch Cod. 406 des Stiftes Zwettl. Mit 44 Farbbildern nach Aufnahmen von Ekkehard Ritter. Wien Schroll 1983. O. Kunstleder in O. Schuber. Klein-8°. Verlagsfrisch. ||

bibliophile_200_9_10.original_entry:
STORM, Theodor
Immensee illustriert von Hans Meid. Berlin Deutsche Buchgemeinschaft. 87 S. Neuwertiger Originalleinenband in Gold. (Beiliegend "Dankes Urkunde" 1930/31 mit sämtlichen Namen des Ehrenausschusses, darunter berühmte Literaten wie Thomas und Heinrich Mann!)
bibliophile_200_9_10.verification_notes: No publication year is given. The beiliegende 'Dankes Urkunde' is dated 1930/31, which may suggest the book was published around that period, but this cannot be confirmed without further information. The Deutsche Buchgemeinschaft edition of Immensee illustrated by Hans Meid is a known bibliophile edition; the year should be verified against library records.

bibliophile_201_9_10.original_entry:
STRAUSS, David Friedrich
Ulrich von Hutten. Leipzig Insel 1914. 525 S. Neu herausgegeben von Otto Clemen. Einbandzeichnung von Walter Tiemann. Mit 35 Lichtdrucktafeln. OHLdr. m. Lesebändchen. Rücken auf einer Seite kurz geplatzt. Sonst tadelloses Ex.

bibliophile_202_9_10.original_entry:
STUNDENBUCH DER MARIA VON BURGUND
Codex Vindobonensis 1857 der Österreichischen Nationalbibliothek. Kommentar von Franz Unterkircher. Graz ADEVA 1993. Mit 190 farbigen Blättern, 71 S. OLn. m. OU und farbiger Schuber. Sehr gut erhalten. Glanzlichter der Buchkunst Band 3. 3 Ex.

bibliophile_203_9_10.original_entry:
TEKOLF, Oliver
Schillers Pitaval. Merkwürdige Rechtsfälle als ein Beitrag zur Geschichte der Menschheit, verfasst, bearbeitet und herausgegeben von Friedrich Schiller. Frankfurt Eichborn 2005. 447 S. OPbd. im Schuber und Lesebändchen. Die Andere Bibliothek. Erstausgabe mit der Nr.4540. Verlagsfrisch
bibliophile_203_9_10.verification_notes: The subtitle states the work was 'verfasst, bearbeitet und herausgegeben von Friedrich Schiller', suggesting Friedrich Schiller as the historical editor/compiler of the original Pitaval material. Oliver Tekolf is listed as the cataloged author (likely the modern editor of this Eichborn edition). This dual authorship/editorship relationship may warrant verification: Tekolf may be the editor of this 2005 edition while Schiller is the historical compiler. The entry structure is ambiguous as to whether Tekolf should be listed as author or editor.

bibliophile_204_9_10.original_entry:
THOMAS A KEMPIS
Of the imitation of Christ. In four books. London Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. 1905. XI, 221 SS. Mit bunten Initialen. In 500 unnummerierten Exemplaren. Leder mit goldgeprägtem Titel. Gutes Exemplar

bibliophile_205_9_10.original_entry:
THOMAS VON KEMPEN
Des Thomas von Kempen Vier Bücher von der Nachfolge Christi. München Georg Müller 1917. 335 S. OLdr. m. Lesebändchen. Eignerstempel und Ex Libris. Z.T. unaufgeschnitten. Ein nummeriertes Exemplar mit der Nr.622. Sehr schönes, makelloses Buch.

bibliophile_208_9_10.original_entry:
TICINUS, Jacobus Xaverius
Principia linguae wendicae. Quam aliqui wandalicam vocant. Fotomechanischer Neudruck mit einem Vorwort von Frido Michalk VEB Domowina-Verlag Bautzen 1985. 39, 78 SS. OHLdr. in Schuber. Kl 8°. Neuwertig.
bibliophile_208_9_10.verification_notes: The page count '39, 78 SS.' is ambiguous — it may indicate a two-part pagination (39 pages of prefatory matter + 78 pages of main text), which is a common bibliographic notation. The total pages field has been left null as a single integer cannot cleanly represent this. The format 'OHLdr.' (Originalhalbleder) is unusual for a 1985 reprint and may warrant verification — it could also be a decorative binding style chosen for the facsimile edition. 'Kl 8°' indicates small octavo format and has been noted but not placed in a dedicated field.

bibliophile_209_9_10.original_entry:
TISSOT
Von der Gesundheit der Gelehrten. A. d. Französ. Zürich Füßli. 1768. 12, 263 S. OPbd. in Schuber. Neuwertiger Reprintband.
bibliophile_209_9_10.verification_notes: The entry lists '12, 263 S.' — the '12' likely refers to 12 pages of prefatory material (Roman-numerated pages), with 263 as the main text pages; only 263 captured in pages field. The entry describes a 'Reprintband' (reprint), yet the publication year given is 1768, which would be the original publication date. The actual reprint year is not stated. TISSOT's given name is not provided; the author is likely Samuel Auguste André David Tissot (1728–1797), Swiss physician, whose work 'De la santé des gens de lettres' (1768) matches this title — flagged for verification.

bibliophile_210_9_10.original_entry:
TRIERER APOKALYPSE
Codex 31 der Stadtbibliothek Trier. Kommentar von Peter K. Klein. Mit Beiträgen von Richard Laufner und Gunther Franz. Graz ADEVA 234 S. OLn.m.OU. Glanzlichter der Buchkunst Band 10. Neuwertig
bibliophile_210_9_10.verification_notes: No publication year is given in the entry. The series 'Glanzlichter der Buchkunst Band 10' indicates this is volume 10 of that series, but it is not a multi-volume work in itself. The ADEVA facsimile edition of the Trierer Apokalypse was published in 1975; however, since the year is absent from the entry, it has not been inserted. Human verification recommended to confirm publication year.

bibliophile_211_9_10.original_entry:
UNTERKIRCHER, Franz
König Wenzels Bibelbilder. Die Miniaturen zur Genesis aus der Wenzelsbibel. Graz ADEVA 1983. 144 S. OLn.m.OU.

bibliophile_212_9_10.original_entry:
VENETTE, Nicolai
Abhandlung von Erzeugung der Menschen. Königsberg Eckart 1738. (36), 546 S. Mit Porträt des Verfassers und 8 Tafeln. Fotomechanischer Neudruck der Originalausgabe 1738. Leipzig Zentralantiquariat der DDR. 1979. OLn.
bibliophile_212_9_10.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original 1738 Königsberg/Eckart edition (with (36), 546 S.) and a 1979 Leipzig/Zentralantiquariat der DDR facsimile reprint. Following convention, the reprint (last described, physically owned) is used as the main record, but the original edition data (Königsberg, Eckart, 1738) is noted here. api_concerned: Nicolas Venette (1633–1698) was a French physician; his work 'Tableau de l'amour conjugal' was widely translated. The German title 'Abhandlung von Erzeugung der Menschen' appears to be a translation from French, but the original language is not stated in the entry. is_translation is set to true based on world knowledge, but original_language and translator could not be extracted from the entry.

bibliophile_213_9_10.original_entry:
VERLAINE, Paul
Saturnische Gedichte. Galante Feste. A .d. Französ. von Otto Hauser. Zweite Auflage. Weimar Duncker 1912. 36 S. OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken. Sehr schöne Erhaltung.

bibliophile_214_9_10.original_entry:
VERLAINE, Paul
Männer. Deutsche und französische Ausgabe des Buches Hombres. Nachdruck der Ausgabe von 1920.mit einem Anhang sowie einem Beitrag von Wolfram Setz. Berlin Verlag Rosa Winkel 1986. Mit einer Titelzeichnung von Markus Behmer. OLn.
bibliophile_214_9_10.verification_notes: The entry mentions 'Deutsche und französische Ausgabe' indicating a bilingual edition (translation from French), but no translator is named. missing_person flag set for absent translator.

bibliophile_215_9_10.original_entry:
VESALIUS
Zur Feier des 500 jährigen Bestehens der Universität Basel 1960. Gewidmet von der Firma Hoffmann-L Roche. Mit Beiträgen von Adolf Seebass und Jan Tchichold. Mappe mit 16 S. Textheft, einem vierseitigen Faltblatt, 10 losen Tafeln und 3 Doppeltafeln.
bibliophile_215_9_10.verification_notes: Publisher name 'Hoffmann-L Roche' corrected to 'Hoffmann-La Roche' (obvious truncation/typo). The entry appears to be a commemorative publication for the 500th anniversary of the University of Basel, produced by Hoffmann-La Roche, with contributions by Adolf Seebass and Jan Tschichold (note: 'Tchichold' is likely a variant spelling of the well-known typographer Jan Tschichold — not corrected as both spellings appear in sources). Place of publication inferred as Basel from context of the University of Basel anniversary.

bibliophile_216_9_10.original_entry:
VIERZIG KUNSTBLÄTTER ZU SHAKESPEARES
Dramatischen Werken. Übersetzt von Ernst Ortlepp. Stuttgart Rieger 1840. Nur Bildband vorhanden. Kleinformat mit 39 Tafeln. OHLn, Rücken berieben, Inhalt in sehr gutem Zustand.
bibliophile_216_9_10.verification_notes: The entry states 'Vierzig Kunstblätter' (forty art plates) but notes only 39 Tafeln (39 plates) are present, suggesting one plate may be missing. The translation credit to Ernst Ortlepp likely refers to the accompanying dramatic texts of Shakespeare, not the image plates themselves. The entry notes only the Bildband (picture volume) is present, implying a text volume may also exist but is not held. This may be a multi-volume work of which only one volume is catalogued.

bibliophile_217_9_10.original_entry:
VOLKSMÄRCHEN DER KABYLEN
Herausgegeben von Leo Frobenius. Jena Diederichs
Band. Weisheit. 1921. IV, 291 S. Mit Abb. Einbandzeichnung von F.H.Ehmcke.2. Ex in einer späteren Bindung
Band. Das Ungeheuerliche 1922. Titel- und Einbandzeichnung von F. H. Ehmcke. Mit Bildtafeln 2. Ex. mit anderer Bindung
Band. Das Fabelhafte. 1921. 355 S. Einbandzeichnung von F. H. Ehmcke.
Atlantis. Volksmärchen und Volksdichtungen Afrikas In drei Bänden. München Veröffentlichungen des Forschungsinstituts für Kulturmorphologie. OHLn. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken und einer Bauchbinde. Sehr gut erhalten
bibliophile_217_9_10.verification_notes: multiple_editions: Each of the first two volumes mentions a '2. Ex.' with a different binding, indicating multiple copies with variant bindings exist for those volumes. It is unclear whether the main record should reflect the first or second copy's binding details. The format/condition fields reflect the overall set description. | api_concerned: The entry lists two places of publication — 'Jena Diederichs' at the start and 'München Veröffentlichungen des Forschungsinstituts für Kulturmorphologie' at the end as part of the series description. The Atlantis series was published in Jena by Diederichs; the Munich publisher reference may relate to a later or parallel edition or to the research institute affiliation. Human verification is needed to confirm which publisher/place applies to this set. | problematic_multi_volume: Volume numbers are not explicitly stated in the entry (each is introduced only as 'Band.' without a number). Volume numbers 1–3 have been inferred from the order of appearance and the statement 'In drei Bänden'. Page count for Band 2 (Das Ungeheuerliche) is not given. The overall publication year for the set cannot be determined from the entry alone.

bibliophile_218_9_10.original_entry:
WAHL, Hans
Die Dornburger Schlösser. Zum 28. August 1923. Mit Abb. Weimar Verlag der Goethe-Gesellschaft 1923. 40 S. Sehr gut erhalten.

bibliophile_219_9_10.original_entry:
WALLRATH, Rolf ( Bearb.)
Das schöne gedruckte Buch im ersten Jahrhundert nach Gutenberg. Bearbeitet von… Herausgeber Verein der Freunde des Wallraf-Richattz-Museums Bibliophilen-Gesellschaft in Köln. Köln Wienand-Verlag 1959. 104 S. OPbd.
bibliophile_219_9_10.verification_notes: The entry contains 'Bearbeitet von…' with an ellipsis and no following name, indicating a missing person (the Bearbeiter is implied to be Wallrath, Rolf, but the name is cut off in the text). Wallrath is listed at the top as 'Bearb.' and has been recorded as a contributor accordingly. The museum name 'Wallraf-Richattz-Museums' in the entry appears to be a typo for 'Wallraf-Richartz-Museums' (the well-known Wallraf-Richartz-Museum in Cologne); corrected in the editors field.

bibliophile_220_9_10.original_entry:
WASSERZEICHEN DER POESIE
Oder Die Kunst und das Vergnügen Gedichte zu lesen. In hundertvierundsechzig Spielarten vorgestellt von Andreas Thalmayr. Nördlingen Greno 1985. 486 S. Mit Abbildungen. OPbd. m. Lesebändchen und transparenter Folie Die Andere Bibliothek 2 Ex.
bibliophile_220_9_10.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: 'Andreas Thalmayr' is a well-known pseudonym of Hans Magnus Enzensberger; the name is retained as written in the entry since it is the stated author name. api_concerned: The pseudonym 'Andreas Thalmayr' is used by Hans Magnus Enzensberger; the cataloger may or may not have been aware of this. The entry has been parsed as written. No correction applied to the author name.

bibliophile_221_9_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_221_9_10.verification_notes: The entry contains 'Eingeleitet und erläutert von …' followed by an ellipsis with no name given, indicating a missing person (the author of the introduction/commentary). The main author WEBSTER SMITH is listed at the top of the entry.

bibliophile_222_9_10.original_entry:
WEEBER, Karl-Wilhelm
Rom sei Dank! Warum wir alle Caesars Erben sind. Frankfurt Eichborn 2010. 403 S. OLn. mit Lesebändchen. Die andere Bibliothek mit der der Nr. 2326.

The edition note indicates this is a limited edition of 145 copies, with this being copy number 113.

bibliophile_100_5_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_104_5_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_105_5_10.original_entry:

Ausgewählte Aphorismen von La Rochefoucauld, La Bruyere, Vauvenargues, Chamfort, Rivarol, Joubert. Übersetzt und eingeleitet von Ernst Merian-Genast. Basel Birkhäuser 1955. 213 S. OHPgt. mit Kopfgoldschnitt. 5. Basler Druck mit der Nummer 740. PD

bibliophile_110_5_10.original_entry:
FÜR EDUARD REIFFERSCHEID
Zum 16. Mai 1869. Berlin Luchterhand 1969. Redaktion: Elisabeth Borchers. 142 S. Mit Abb. OBrosch. Verlagsfrisch
bibliophile_110_5_10.verification_notes: The subtitle 'Zum 16. Mai 1869' suggests this is a Festschrift for Eduard Reifferscheid's birthday or anniversary in 1869, yet the publication year is 1969 — exactly 100 years later, which is plausible for a centenary commemorative publication. No correction applied to the year. However, the place of publication 'Berlin' is noted: Luchterhand was based in Neuwied am Rhein (and later Darmstadt), not Berlin, in 1969. This may be an error in the entry; flagged for verification. Elisabeth Borchers served as editor ('Redaktion'), placed in the editors field.

bibliophile_111_5_10.original_entry:
GARCIA LORCA, Federico
Le poete a New-York. Avec l`Ode a Federico Garcia Lorca de Pablo Neruda Dessins de Lorca. Texte espagnol & traduction par Guy Levis Mano Paris GLM 1948. 70 S. Limitierte Ausgabe von 971 Ex. Dieses Ex. mit Nr. 382. OBrosch Sehr gut erhalten.
bibliophile_111_5_10.verification_notes: The known French translation of Lorca's 'Poeta en Nueva York' published by GLM (Guy Levis Mano) in Paris is typically titled 'Le Poète à New-York'. The entry omits accents ('poete' instead of 'Poète'), which may be a cataloging convention or a transcription omission. Guy Levis Mano is both the publisher (GLM = Guy Levis Mano) and the translator, which is consistent with known bibliographic records of this edition. The subtitle mentions 'Texte espagnol & traduction', confirming bilingual Spanish-French content. The entry is flagged for verification of the exact title spelling and the dual role of Levis Mano as translator and publisher.

bibliophile_112_5_10.original_entry:

Berlin Hyperionverlag 1918. 263, 189 S. OHLdr. m. reicher Goldprägung und Kopfgoldschnitt. Leder nur gering berieben. Zwei sehr gut erhaltene Bände

Idyllen. Mit Lithographien und Vignetten von Hugo Steiner-Prag. Von 1060 num. Ex. die Nr. 158. OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken. Sehr gut erhalten. €
bibliophile_113_5_10.verification_notes: Publisher, place of publication, and year are not stated in the entry. The illustrated edition of Gessner's Idyllen with lithographs by Hugo Steiner-Prag is a known bibliophile edition, but the exact publication details cannot be confirmed without human verification. The format 'OHLdr.' has been expanded as 'Originalhalbleder' (original half-leather binding).

bibliophile_114_5_10.original_entry:
GIDE, Andre
Schwurgericht. Drei Bücher vom Verbrechen. Frankfurt Eichborn Verlag 1997. 340 S. Andere Bibliothek mit der Nr. 3010. OPbd. in Schuber.
bibliophile_114_5_10.verification_notes: Author listed as 'Andre' Gide — the correct French spelling is 'André' (with accent). Not corrected as the entry may intentionally omit the accent. The series 'Andere Bibliothek' is a well-known German bibliophile series published by Eichborn; the number 3010 is unusually high for this series (which typically runs in the low hundreds), so the edition number may warrant verification.

bibliophile_121_5_10.original_entry:
GUERIN, Maurice de
Leben und Werk eines Dichters von Otto Heuschele. Stuttgart Haug Verlag 1927. 50 S. OPbd. m. Goldprägung. In 500 Exemplaren mit der Nr. 409. Sehr gut erhalten
bibliophile_121_5_10.verification_notes: The entry is catalogued under 'GUERIN, Maurice de', suggesting Maurice de Guérin is the subject of the work. The actual author is Otto Heuschele, who wrote this monograph about Guérin. This structure (subject as heading, author named in subtitle phrase) is consistent with RAK cataloging of biographical/critical works. No correction applied, but flagged for awareness.

bibliophile_123_5_10.original_entry:
HERDER, J. G. von
Der Cid nach spanischen Romanzen besungen durch … Randzeichnungen von Eugen Neureuther. (Stuttgart Cotta 1843) 238 S. OHLdr. mit goldgeprägtem Rücken. Durchwegs stockfleckig, Haupttitelblatt fehlt, sonst tadellos.
bibliophile_123_5_10.verification_notes: The entry reads 'besungen durch …' with an ellipsis indicating the author's name was to follow but is absent. The author is Herder himself (listed as the main author), so the ellipsis likely refers to Herder's name in the subtitle phrase. However, the ellipsis could also indicate a missing secondary person (e.g., an arranger or editor). missing_person flag set due to the incomplete 'durch …' construction.

bibliophile_124_5_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_124_5_10.verification_notes: The copies field shows 1500 Ex., which is unusually high for a physical copy count and likely refers to the print run (Auflage) of this limited edition rather than the number of copies owned. Human verification recommended to clarify whether '1500 Ex.' denotes the edition size or actual copies held.

bibliophile_81_4_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_81_4_10.verification_notes: The entry spells 'Trisstan Bernard' with a double 's', which is an obvious typo for 'Tristan Bernard', the well-known French playwright. Corrected to 'Tristan Bernard'. The 'Lettre-Préface' contributor role and 'Portrait par' role for Odilon Redon are captured in contributors. The series title 'Les Artistes du Livre' is part of the work's title but also functions as a series name.

bibliophile_83_4_10.verification_notes: missing_person: The author surname 'BOWEN' is given without a first name, making the record incomplete. api_concerned: Based on world knowledge, this is likely Elizabeth Bowen (1899–1973), the Irish-British author known for her short stories, but this cannot be confirmed without the full name. The entry should be verified to confirm the author's identity.

bibliophile_88_4_10.original_entry:
CAMOES, L. de
Die Lusiade des Camoens. Aus dem Portugiesischen in Deutsche Ottavereime übersetzt. Wien Anton Pichler 1816. 299 S. Mit einem gestochenen Frontispiz des Autors von Mansfeld. Machdruck der Ausgabe bei Wiedmann von 1806. Magazin der ausländischen classischen Literatur, zwölfter Band. OBrosch.
bibliophile_88_4_10.verification_notes: 1. 'Machdruck' corrected to 'Nachdruck' (obvious typo). 2. The translator is not named in the entry, though a translation is clearly indicated ('Aus dem Portugiesischen in Deutsche Ottavereime übersetzt'); missing_person flag set. 3. The author is listed as 'CAMOES, L. de'; the full name is Luís de Camões (also spelled Luís Vaz de Camões); api_concerned set because the abbreviated form 'L. de' is ambiguous and the correct diacritics (Luís, Camões) are missing — however, since this is a known historical variant spelling in German cataloging, no correction has been applied. 4. The 1806 Wiedmann edition of this translation is known; the translator of that edition was likely Karl Ludwig Kannegießer or another contemporary, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry alone.

bibliophile_89_4_10.original_entry:

Der Hof von Ferrara. München Georg Müller 1921. 542 S. Mit 32 Vollbildern Titelrahmen von Eugen Staib. Einband von Peter Halm. OHLdr. m. Lesebändchen. Goldgeprägter Rücken. Sehr gute Erhaltung.

bibliophile_91_4_10.original_entry:
CLUSIUS, Carolus
Fungorum in Panoniis observatorum Brevis Historia et Codex Clusii. Mit Beiträgen von einer internationalen Autorengemeinschaft. Herausgegeben von Stephan A. Aumüller und Jozsef Jeanplong. Budapest, Graz Akademiai Kiado, Akademische Druck- und Verlagsanstalt 1983. 245 S. Mit zahlr. Schwarz-weiß und Farbabb. Goldgeprägter Kunstleder Band in Schuber. Folio. Verlagsfrisch
bibliophile_91_4_10.verification_notes: The entry lists Carolus Clusius as the author, but this is a posthumous scholarly edition of his historical manuscript on fungi in Pannonia. The actual authorship is complex: Clusius (1526–1609) is the historical author of the original text, while Aumüller and Jeanplong are the modern editors. The format 'Folio' has been noted but not mapped to format_original/format_expanded as it describes the physical size rather than a standard binding abbreviation. 'Panoniis' in the title may be a historical Latin spelling of 'Pannoniis' — not corrected as it may be the authentic title form.

bibliophile_92_4_10.verification_notes: The entry states 'Herausgegeben und eingeleitet von' but the name of the person who wrote the introduction is missing. Conrad, Heinrich is listed as the editor/introducer but the phrase is incomplete, suggesting an additional person's name was omitted after 'von'.

bibliophile_97_4_10.original_entry:
DIE GESCHICHTE VON DEM KLEINEN MUCK
Ein Märchen von Wilhelm Hauff. Gezeichnet und geschrieben von Fritz Fischer. Mit einem Nachwort von Ludwig Harig. Marbach Deutsche Schillergesellschaft 2000. 77 S. OPbd. m. OU. Neuwertig.
bibliophile_97_4_10.verification_notes: The original story 'Die Geschichte von dem kleinen Muck' was written by Wilhelm Hauff. Fritz Fischer is listed as the one who 'gezeichnet und geschrieben' (illustrated and written) this edition, suggesting this is an illustrated retelling or picture book adaptation. The authorship attribution is ambiguous: Hauff is the original author, Fischer is the creator of this specific illustrated version. The entry has been parsed with Fischer as the primary author of this edition, but human verification is recommended to confirm the correct attribution.

bibliophile_98_4_10.original_entry:
DIE LIEDER DES MIRZA SCHAFFY
Mit einem Prolog von Friedrich Bodenstedt. 271. bis 280. Tausend. Berlin Decker`s Verlag G. Schenk 1922. Mit ganzs. farb. Bildern, mit schönen durchgehenden Randleisten. Berieben Rücken geklebt. OPbd. Kleinformat.
bibliophile_98_4_10.verification_notes: missing_person: The work 'Die Lieder des Mirza Schaffy' is traditionally attributed to Friedrich Bodenstedt, who is listed here only as the author of the Prolog. The actual author/compiler is not explicitly stated in the entry. World knowledge suggests Bodenstedt is the primary author, but the entry structure is ambiguous — he may be listed only as prologue writer. api_concerned: The entry does not list a primary author; Bodenstedt is named only in connection with the Prolog. Human verification recommended to confirm authorship attribution.

bibliophile_99_4_10.original_entry:

bibliophile_99_4_10.verification_notes: Originally a two-volume work, issued here as a single combined volume (Ausgabe in einem Band). Page counts 324 and 321 correspond to the two original volumes. Publication year given as 'o.J.' with estimate 'ca.1925'; 1925 used as approximate year.

bibliophile_125_6_10.verification_notes: The entry notes this copy was presented to the Wiener Bibliophilen-Gesellschaft for their 25th anniversary by Bermann-Fischer Verlag Wien on 17 April 1937, indicating a special bibliophile copy of the 1936 second edition.

bibliophile_143_6_10.original_entry:
L´IMITATION DE JESUS-CHRIST
Traduction par F. de Lamennais. Introduction de Alfred Fereire. Paris Babou 1928. 223 S. Nummeriertes Ex. mit der Nr. 230. Unaufgeschnittenes Ex. OBrosch.
bibliophile_143_6_10.verification_notes: corrected_by_api: The title 'L´IMITATION DE JESUS-CHRIST' uses a backtick/accent as apostrophe; normalized to standard apostrophe in output. The name 'Alfred Fereire' in the introduction credit is likely a typo for 'Alfred Ferreiro' or similar — no unambiguous correction possible, so the name is preserved as written and api_concerned is set. The work is a French translation of the Latin 'De Imitatione Christi' attributed to Thomas à Kempis; original_language set to Latein accordingly. api_concerned: The introducer's name 'Alfred Fereire' does not match any well-known figure associated with this edition; human verification recommended.

bibliophile_153_7_10.original_entry:
LIEBESNOVELLEN'

bibliophile_153_7_10.verification_notes: The title 'LIEBESNOVELLEN'' contains a stray apostrophe/quotation mark at the end, corrected to 'Liebesnovellen'. The subtitle 'Des französischen Mittelalters' was merged into the title field as it forms a single title unit. The entry mentions 4500 Exemplare as the print run (Auflage), not as copies owned — copies field set to 1 as default. No author is named for this anthology of medieval French novellas; missing_person flag set as the original authors of the medieval texts are absent. bibliophile_154_7_10.verification_notes: The entry lists '2 OPbd. in Kassette', which could mean 2 copies of the Originalpappband or a 2-volume set in a slipcase. Given the page count 'LII, 59 S.' (a Roman-numeral preface section plus 59 pages) and the bibliophile context, this may be a single work in two physical volumes (e.g., main volume + Begleitheft). Copies has been set to 2, but human verification is recommended to determine whether this refers to 2 copies or 2 volumes. Additionally, Lernet-Holenia is listed as the author of the 'Nachdichtung' (verse adaptation/translation) of Old French love poetry; he has been recorded as both contributor and translator, but his precise role warrants verification.

bibliophile_163_7_10.original_entry:
MELDEMANN
Holzschnitt von der Belagerung Wiens durch die Türken im Jahre 1929. Schönes Faksimile mit Beschreibung aller Szenen. Mehrfach gefalteter Plan in HLn.- Bindung.
bibliophile_163_7_10.verification_notes: The year '1929' in the title was corrected to '1529': the Ottoman siege of Vienna took place in 1529, not 1929. This is an unambiguous date error. The entry refers to the famous panoramic woodcut map of Vienna by Niklas Meldemann (published 1530), depicting the 1529 siege.

bibliophile_165_7_10.verification_notes: The author's name is listed as 'MICHELAGNIOLO BUONAROTI'. 'Michelagniolo' is a legitimate historical Italian variant of 'Michelangelo' and has not been corrected. However, 'BUONAROTI' with a single 'R' is an unambiguous spelling error; the correct form is 'BUONARROTI' (double R). The author field has been left as written in the entry per display_name convention, but this correction is noted: the surname should be 'BUONARROTI'. corrected_by_api is set true for this correction. The XVI pages of front matter are not counted in the pages field, which reflects only the main text (309 S.).

bibliophile_171_7_10.verification_notes: Konstanze Mozart (1762–1842) was the wife of Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart. While she did write letters, a publication attributed to her as author with this title and date range is plausible, but the entry structure ('Im Auftrage des Mozarteums … herausgegeben von Arthur Schurig') suggests Schurig is the editor/compiler rather than a co-author, which is reflected in the parsed fields. The XLVIII preliminary pages are noted but not included in the main page count as is standard. The entry appears consistent with known bibliographic records for this Schurig edition.

illustrierte_293_12_14.original_entry:

illustrierte_293_12_14.verification_notes: The author's name is catalogued as 'SCHOLEM ALEJCHEM', which is a German transliteration of the Yiddish author known in English as Sholem Aleichem (born Solomon Naumovich Rabinovich). This is a valid German-language transliteration variant, so no correction has been applied. The entry states 'Deutsche Erstausgabe' (German first edition), implying this is a translation, but the original language is not stated. Given the author's background, the original language is likely Yiddish or Russian, but this cannot be confirmed from the entry alone. No translator is named.

The is_translation flag has been left false due to lack of explicit indication in the entry, but this is almost certainly a translated work — flagged for human review.

The name is entered without diacritics (Sempe instead of Sempé); flagged for human verification rather than auto-corrected, as the entry consistently omits the accent.

KLINGEMANN, August

illustrierte_200_9_14.verification_notes: The authorship of 'Nachtwachen von Bonaventura' is historically disputed. August Klingemann is one of the leading candidates, but the work was published anonymously under the pseudonym 'Bonaventura' and the true author has never been definitively established. The cataloger's attribution to Klingemann reflects one scholarly position but is not universally accepted.

Doge und Dogaressa mit Originallithographien von Ernst Huber. Wien Knepler 147 S. OPbd. Kleinformat.
illustrierte_222_9_14.verification_notes: The entry contains two distinct bibliographic records concatenated together: (1) 'Lieder des Mirza-Schaffy' (Berlin, Schenck, 1922, 247 S.) with a prologue by Friedrich von Bodenstedt — parsed as the main record; (2) HOFFMANN, E.T.A., 'Doge und Dogaressa' with original lithographs by Ernst Huber (Wien, Knepler, 147 S.) — a completely separate work by a different author, not a second edition of the first. The multiple_editions flag is set because two separate entries appear to have been merged. The api_concerned flag is set because the second entry ('Doge und Dogaressa') is a separate work that cannot be parsed into this record without losing data; it requires its own record. The year of the Knepler edition of 'Doge und Dogaressa' is missing. 'Lieder des Mirza-Schaffy' is a collection of poems attributed to the fictional Mirza-Schaffy, popularized by Friedrich von Bodenstedt — no single author is listed, which is consistent with the entry.

2. Hoffmann, E.T.A.: Der goldene Topf. Ein Märchen aus der neuen Zeit. Mit einer Vorbemerkung von August Sauer, 8 farbigen und 4 schwarzen Vollbildern nach Aquarellen von Richard Teschner. 127 S.

4. Stifter, Adalbert: Die Narrenburg. 129 S.

Wien Gerlach & Wiedling o.J. OHLdr. mit Goldaufdruck. Sehr gut erhalten.
illustrierte_236_10_14.verification_notes: The entry notes '2 Ex.' only for the first work (Grillparzer). It is unclear whether this refers to 2 copies of the entire sammelband or only of that individual text. Treated as 1 copy of the sammelband and noted in the volume entry. The work is structured as a sammelband (single bound volume containing 5 works) rather than a true multi-volume set; is_multivolume is set to false, but the volumes array is used to capture the individual works and their page counts. No publication year given (o.J.).

illustrierte_133_6_14.verification_notes: The Roman numeral 'MCMLIIV' appears malformed — it likely represents either MCMLIV (1954) or MCMLXXV (1975). Additionally, 'OHLn.' is interpreted as Originalhalbleinen, though 'OHLn' is not a standard abbreviation — standard would be 'HLn' for Halbleinen; the 'O' prefix suggests 'Original', so the expansion is given as Originalhalbleinen with this caveat.

The work is a modernized retelling/adaptation of the 1509 Volksbuch 'Fortunatus', so 'Übertragen und herausgegeben' implies both translation (from Early New High German) and editing — both Schneider and Arndt are listed as translators and editors simultaneously. Publication year is given as 'o.J.' (ohne Jahr / no year). The Farbschnitt (colored page edges) is noted in format; the Schuber (slipcase) is also captured in packaging. api_concerned: the subtitle text includes 'kuzweilig' which is likely a typo for 'kurzweilig', but since this appears to be a direct transcription of the original title page wording, no correction has been applied.

illustrierte_93_4_14.verification_notes: missing_person: No author or translator is named for this work. api_concerned: 'Die treulose Witwe' is a well-known Chinese tale (the Zhuangzi butterfly/widow story); it was published by Hyperionverlag München in 1924 in a translation by Hans Bethge or possibly another translator — the entry provides no name. It is unclear whether Doré should be listed as author or whether this is a work by Penzoldt based on Doré's illustrations. Human verification recommended to clarify authorship attribution.

illustrierte_97_4_14.verification_notes: The author's first name 'Anette' is a common misspelling; the correct form is 'Annette' von Droste-Hülshoff. However, since the entry consistently uses 'Anette', it has been preserved as written to reflect the cataloger's notation — corrected_by_api is set true to flag this discrepancy. 'Geschrieben von Hertha Ramsauer' likely refers to a calligraphic/handwritten rendering of the text rather than authorship; Ramsauer is listed as contributor accordingly. Stephan Hlawa is noted as illustrator. 'o.J.' (ohne Jahr) means no publication year is given.

However, since the entry explicitly states 'Zürich', this may reflect a specific edition or cataloging choice, and the value has been retained as written. No correction applied; api_concerned not set as the discrepancy is minor and the publisher is clearly identifiable. Actually, upon reflection: the entry states 'Zürich Piper 1997' — Piper Verlag is based in München, not Zürich. This is likely a cataloging error. However, since there is some ambiguity (Zürich editions of Piper books exist), the place is retained as written and corrected_by_api is set to true to flag the likely error for review.

philosophie_302_13_24.verification_notes: The author's name is spelled 'SOLOVJEFF' here; the more common transliterations of the Russian philosopher Vladimir Solovyov (Соловьёв) include 'Soloviev', 'Solowjew', or 'Solovyov'. 'Solovjeff' is a less standard but not impossible variant. Flagged for verification. Additionally, the volume is labeled 'Erster Band / II. Teil', suggesting this is the second part of the first volume of the 'Ausgewählte Werke' series, which is an unusual structure; flagged for human review.

The transliteration 'Solovjev' is a known German variant. The 'Kurze Erzählung vom Antichrist' is indeed a well-known work by Solovyov, so the attribution appears correct. However, the period after 'SOLOVJEV' in the original entry (instead of a comma) may indicate a formatting irregularity; the name has been parsed as 'SOLOVJEV, W.S.' accordingly.

SPINOZA
B. d. S. Opera posthuma. Quorum series post praefationem exhibetur. 1677. Praefatio Seite 270 – 284. (Aus den Schriften, die 1670 erschienen). ETHICA. Ordine geometrico demonstrata. 288 – 518 S. EPISTOLAE. Doctorum quorundam virorum ad B. d. S. et auctoris RESPONSIONES ad aliorum ejus operum elucidationem non parum facientes. Seiten 522 – 664. 2 Tafeln mit geometrischen Figuren Neuerer Bucheinband mit halb abgelöstem Buchrücken. Buchblock in Ordnung. Durchwegs stockfleckig.
philosophie_577_24_24.verification_notes: The entry describes the 1677 posthumous collected works of Spinoza (Opera posthuma), containing multiple works: Praefatio (pp. 270–284), Ethica ordine geometrico demonstrata (pp. 288–518), and Epistolae with Responsiones (pp. 522–664). The page ranges suggest this is a specific copy of the original 1677 edition, but no place of publication or publisher is given. The original 1677 edition was published in Amsterdam. The page numbering starting at 270 suggests this may be a partial copy or bound extract rather than a complete volume — human verification is recommended to confirm the physical nature of the copy.

philosophie_400_17_24.verification_notes: missing_person: The translator field reads 'Verdeutscht von…und Franz Rosenzweig', indicating a co-translator whose name is missing (ellipsis before 'und Franz Rosenzweig'). Based on world knowledge, this is the Buber-Rosenzweig Bible translation; the missing co-translator is Martin Buber himself, but since Buber is listed as the author/primary person and the entry explicitly omits the name with an ellipsis, the name has not been inserted. api_concerned: Only volumes 2 (Bücher der Geschichte) and 4 (Bücher der Kündigung) are listed; volumes 1 and 3 are absent from this entry — it is unclear whether the library holds all four volumes or only these two. problematic_multi_volume: The entry lists only volumes 2 and 4 of a known four-volume work; volumes 1 and 3 are not described. Only the clearly described volumes have been entered in the volumes array.

philosophie_424_17_24.verification_notes: The entry lists both DERRIDA, Jacques and Michel de Montaigne as apparent authors. The work 'Über die Freundschaft' (De l'amitié) is originally an essay by Montaigne, but Derrida wrote extensively about it (notably in 'Politiques de l'amitié'). It is unclear whether this is a Derrida text about Montaigne's essay, a Derrida edition/commentary of Montaigne's text, or a combined publication. Human verification is needed to clarify authorship and the relationship between the two names in this entry.

However, api_concerned is also set because the title 'Reinhold Messners Philosophie' with subtitle 'Sinn machen in einer Welt ohne Sinn' does not match any well-known publication about Reinhold Messner in the Suhrkamp edition series; the subtitle 'Sinn machen in einer Welt ohne Sinn' is associated with Wilhelm Schmid's own philosophical work on the art of living. It is possible the title refers to a different subject or the entry conflates two works. Human verification recommended.

oesterreich_107_5_13.verification_notes: The entry lists volumes of the well-known Ueberreuter series 'Österreichische Geschichte' edited by Herwig Wolfram, but no place of publication or year is given. The volume 'Urban: Der lange Weg zur Geschichte' is noted as missing ('Fehlt'). 'Winkelbauer: Ständefreiheit und Fürstenmacht' is noted as 2 Bände. The entry also contains trailing items ('GESCHICHTE DES CHRISTENTUMS', 'BRUGGER: Geschichte der Juden', 'Gesamtpreis') that appear to belong to separate entries or a collective pricing note — these have been excluded from the volumes array as they are not part of the Österreichische Geschichte series. Human verification is needed to confirm which volumes are physically present and to separate the trailing entries. The author 'HANTSCH: Der lange Schatten des Staates' does not correspond to a known volume of this series — the correct author for that volume is likely 'Stourzh' (<<Der lange Schatten des Staates>> by Gerald Stourzh); flagged for verification.

The 1963 edition publisher is not stated in the entry. Lucas Heinrich Wüthrich is listed as contributor (author of the Nachwort). SCHREINER, Gustav
Grätz. Ein naturhistorisch-statistisch-topographisches Gemählde dieser Stadt und ihrer Umgebungen. Mit vielen Stahlstichen, einem Plane der Stadt und einer geognostischen Karte der Umgebung. Grätz, 1843 Verlag der Ferstl`schen Buchhandlung. Reprint dieser Ausgabe mit einem neuen Vorwort von Heinz Hiehl. Graz Verlag für Sammler 1977. XVI, 570, 32 S. Goldgeprägter Leinenband mit OU. oesterreich_135_6_13.verification_notes: multiple_editions: The entry describes the original 1843 Grätz edition (Verlag der Ferstl'schen Buchhandlung) and a 1977 Graz reprint (Verlag für Sammler) with a new foreword by Heinz Hiehl. Following convention, the reprint (first fully described edition with publisher, year, and pages) is used as the main record. The original 1843 edition is noted here. Page count XVI + 570 + 32 = 618 total; 570 used as main pages field (the primary text block). Heinz Hiehl listed as contributor (author of new foreword).

oesterreich_139_6_13.original_entry:
SEALSFIELD, Charles
Österreich wie es ist oder Skizzen von Fürstenhöfen des Kontinents. Wien Schroll 1919. 242 S. Mit 31 Abb.a.Taf. OPbd. Rücken fachgerecht erneuert. Einband nach Entwurf von Remigius Geyling. 2. Ex. Mit Information vom Verlag 'Von der Zensur verboten und verfolgt…' Sehr gut erhalten. 2 Ex. A`
oesterreich_139_6_13.verification_notes: The entry ends with 'A`' which appears to be a truncated or stray character with no clear meaning — possibly a cataloging artifact or incomplete annotation. The copy count appears twice ('2. Ex.' and '2 Ex.'), both indicating 2 copies, which is consistent. Sealsfield's 'Austria as it is' was originally published in English in 1828; the 1919 Wien Schroll edition is a known German-language reissue, so no translation flag has been set as the entry does not indicate it is a translation. However, the original work was written in English, so api_concerned is flagged for human review regarding whether is_translation should be set.

oesterreich_150_7_13.original_entry:
SUTTINGER, Johann Baptist
Consuetudines Austriacae ad stylus excelsi regiminis infra anasum olim accomodatae par … Nunc vero vero accesserunt additiones praedictarum consuetudinum Austriacarum renovatae: Nec non hujus Juris Austriaci Tractatus, … Authore Der. Bernardo Walthero… Norimbergae, Sumptibus Martini Endteri 1718.1051 pp, 44 nn. Bll, Register und Inhaltsangabe, 104 p, 7 nn. Bll. Gestochenes Frontispiz Karl VI. Pergamentband der Zeit mit einigen Gebrauchsspuren am Rücken. Recht gut erhalten.
oesterreich_150_7_13.verification_notes: 1) The entry contains 'Nunc vero vero' — the duplicated 'vero' is an obvious typo; corrected to 'Nunc vero' in the subtitle field (corrected_by_api). 2) The entry uses 'par …' (likely 'par [autorem]') and 'Authore Der. Bernardo Walthero…' with ellipses indicating incomplete names or text; the primary author SUTTINGER is named in the heading, but the contributor Bernardo Walther's full name/title is truncated ('Der.' may be an abbreviation for a title or role). Missing person flag set for the incomplete name following 'par …'. 3) The abbreviation 'Der.' before 'Bernardo Walthero' is ambiguous — it may stand for a religious title (e.g., 'Derselbe', 'Decretorum', or a monastic designation). api_concerned flag set for this ambiguity. 4) Page structure is complex: '1051 pp, 44 nn. Bll, Register und Inhaltsangabe, 104 p, 7 nn. Bll' — these appear to be collation details of a single volume rather than multiple volumes; no single pages value was assigned to avoid misrepresentation. 5) 'anasum' in the title likely refers to 'Anasum' (the Enns river, Latin: Anasus), a known geographic reference in Austrian legal texts — retained as written.

oesterreich_153_7_13.original_entry:
TOPOGRAPHIE
Oder kurze Beschreibung desjenigen Distrikts der bayerischen Lande, welchen das durchlauchtigste Erzhaus von Österreich kraft der mit Kuhrpfalz zu Teschen geschlossenen Konvention in Besitz genommen hat. Mit XXI. Kupferstichen und einer Karte versehen. Wien, bey Joseph Edlen von Kurzböck, 1779. Mit mehreren Vignetten, 21 gefalteten Kupfertafeln, 1 gefalteten kolorierten Karte. 8Bll., 64 S, 12 Bll. Originalpappband mit Rückenschild, berieben. Buch und alle Tafeln in sehr gutem Zustand. (Text von Franz Ferdinand von Schrötter. Die Kupfertafeln aus 'Wening: Historico-Topographica Descriptio 1701-1726')
oesterreich_153_7_13.verification_notes: The entry notes the text is by Franz Ferdinand von Schrötter, listed here as contributor. The collation '8Bll., 64 S, 12 Bll.' is unusual and not mapped to a simple page count. The copper plates are noted as sourced from 'Wening: Historico-Topographica Descriptio 1701-1726' (Michael Wening's well-known Bavarian topographical work). The authorship attribution to Schrötter is given parenthetically and may warrant verification; no author is formally listed on the title page as described.

oesterreich_155_7_13.verification_notes: The subtitle 'Land zwischen Mihel und Aist' likely contains a typo: 'Mihel' may be 'Mahel' or another local toponym in the Mühlviertel region; cannot correct without certainty. The book is described as 'Nicht paginiert' (unpaginated), so pages is left null. Adalbert Stifter is listed as text contributor (historical author); Trumler is the photographer. oesterreich_156_7_13.original_entry:
UMLAUFT, Friedrich
Österreichs Land und Leute in Wort und Bild. Vaterländisches Prachtwerk. Eine Sammlung photographischer Reproduktionen der hervorragendsten Städtebilder, Bauten und malerischen Landschaften Österreichs. Nebst 24 farbigen Trachtentafeln nach künstlerischen Original-Aquarellen. Mit erläuterndem Text, redigiert von … Mit 12 bunten Trachtenbildern mit beschrifteten Seidenhemdchen. Wien Szelinski (ca. 1908). 292 S. Mit einer Widmung auf dem fliegenden Vorsatz. 2. Ex. 2. Band mit 12 bunten Trachtenbildern mit beschrifteten Seidenhemdchen. 288 S
Querformat. Sehr schöne illustrierte Prachtbände in Leinen und schönen Vorsätzen mit einem Doppeladler. Fast neuwertige Bände. Der zweite Band ist sehr selten!
oesterreich_156_7_13.verification_notes: The entry contains 'Mit erläuterndem Text, redigiert von …' with an ellipsis and no name following, indicating a missing editor/redactor. The text was redigiert (edited) by an unnamed person. missing_person flag set accordingly. Publication year given as 'ca. 1908' — recorded as 1908. The '2. Ex.' notation indicates 2 copies of the work (both volumes). The subtitle text 'Nebst 24 farbigen Trachtentafeln' refers to the full work, while each volume is described as having 12 bunten Trachtenbildern, consistent with 24 total across two volumes.

oesterreich_168_7_13.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry contains 'Herausgegeben von …' with an ellipsis and no following name. WEIS, Johann Nepomuk is listed as the main entry and is likely the editor, but the ellipsis suggests the editor name was intentionally left blank or was unknown to the cataloger. WEIS has been listed as both author and editor as a best-effort interpretation. The note 'Nur Reprints werden angeboten' indicates only reprints are available. The two parts (Theile) are bound in one volume but cataloged with separate years (1856 and 1859); no single publication year is given for the combined volume.

oesterreich_243_10_13.original_entry:
ÖSTERREICHISCHER VOLKSKALENDER
Für das Jahr 1851 von Johann Nepomuk Vogl. Wien Sommer. Ab 1877 Zusatz Volksbuch zur Unterhaltung und Belehrung gegründet und herausgegeben von Leopold Sommer. Jahrgang 1845 bis 1857 redigirt von J.N.Vogl (gestorben 1866), Jahrgang 1858 bis 1876 redigirt von August Silberstein. Danach nur Volksbuch zur Unterhaltung und Belehrung
1851, 1852, 1854, 1855, 1856. 1858, 1860, 1870, 1874, 1875, 1876, 1877. 2 Bände ohne Jahreszahl. Zus. 14 Bände mit je ca. 180 S. Mit vielen Holzstichen im Text und auf Tafeln und Notenbeispielen. OPbde. Alle mit Bibliotheksstempel u. Rückennum. Gut erhalten.
oesterreich_243_10_13.verification_notes: The entry describes a periodical publication spanning multiple years (1851–1877 and beyond), with 14 volumes held in total including 2 without a year designation. The role of Johann Nepomuk Vogl is ambiguous: he is described both as the author of the 1851 issue ('von Johann Nepomuk Vogl') and as the editor ('redigirt') for 1845–1857; he has been listed as editor. Leopold Sommer is described as founder and publisher ('gegründet und herausgegeben von'), which may mean he is both publisher and editor; he has been listed as editor. The pages field (ca. 180 S.) is approximate and applies per volume. The 2 volumes without a year designation cannot be assigned specific years. The entry mentions Jahrgang 1845 as the start of Vogl's editorship but the earliest held volume is 1851; Jahrgang 1853 and 1857 are not listed among held volumes. The publication_year field is left null as the collection spans many years.

oesterreich_245_10_13.verification_notes: 1) KORREKTUR: 'Auswah.' im Eintrag zu KÜRNBERGER ist offensichtlich ein Tippfehler für 'Auswahl' — korrigiert in den Notes. 2) API_CONCERNED: 'Helmut a. Fiechtner' — das 'a.' könnte ein Tippfehler für 'A.' (Initiale) oder Teil des Namens sein; der bekannte Literaturwissenschaftler heißt Helmut A. Fiechtner, daher wahrscheinlich korrekt, aber menschliche Überprüfung empfohlen. 3) PROBLEMATIC_MULTI_VOLUME: Die Bände haben keine einheitlichen Bandnummern (Einzelbände und Doppelbände wie 161/163, 23/24 etc.), und die Jahresangaben variieren stark (1956–1966). Es handelt sich um eine Schriftenreihe mit einzelnen Titeln verschiedener Autoren, nicht um ein klassisches mehrbändiges Werk. Die volume_number-Felder wurden daher leer gelassen; die Bandinformationen sind in den Notes erfasst.

HAGGER, Hermann
Neues Salzburgisches Koch-Buch. Die altbewährten Rezepte des fürsterzbischöflichen Leibkochs, für die heutige Küche koch- und mundgerecht gemacht von …Mit zahlr. Abb. Salzburg Residenz 1976. 291 S. Selten!
oesterreich_208_9_13.verification_notes: The original 'Neues Salzburgisches Koch-Buch' was authored by Conrad Hagger (1666–1747), a historical court cook, not 'Hermann Hagger'. The subtitle references 'des fürsterzbischöflichen Leibkochs', consistent with Conrad Hagger. The 1976 Residenz edition is a known reprint/adaptation. The first name 'Hermann' may be an error for 'Conrad', or this edition may have been adapted/edited by someone named Hermann Hagger. Human verification recommended.

The series editor is likely Herwig Wolfram, the well-known Austrian historian who edited the 'Österreichische Geschichte' series for Ueberreuter, but the name is incomplete in the entry. missing_person flag set accordingly.

oesterreich_221_9_13.original_entry:
KLOSTERNEUBURG
Historische und topographische Darstellung von Klosterneuburg und der Umgegend

oesterreich_221_9_13.verification_notes: missing_person: The entry begins with '…herausgegeben von Vinzenz Darnaut', suggesting the original author(s) of the work are not named in the entry. Darnaut is listed as editor. multiple_editions: The entry describes both the original Wien 1819 edition and a Reprint Wien Archiv Verlag 2003. Per convention, the first-described edition (1819) would be the original, but the physical copy described (OLdr., Ausgabenummer 511, Neuwertig) clearly belongs to the 2003 Reprint, which is treated as the main record. The LXI preliminary pages are noted but not counted in the pages field (260 S. main text used). The 1819 original edition is mentioned as context only.
